### Assistant *(2026-03-21T00:10:26.583Z)*

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/scripts/check_generic_title_words.py",
  "content": "# Purpose:      Query the DNB SPARQL endpoint to count GND Work records whose\n#               preferredNameForTheWork contains each GENERIC_TITLE_WORD, to\n#               validate which words are genuinely high-collision and should be\n#               excluded from distinctive token selection in link_gnd_works.py.\n# Usage:        python scripts/check_generic_title_words.py\n#               python scripts/check_generic_title_words.py --delay 2.0\n#               python scripts/check_generic_title_words.py --output results/generic_title_words.tsv\n# Inputs:       GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS defined in this script\n# Outputs:      TSV table of (word, count, keep) printed to stdout and optionally written to file\n# Dependencies: requests (pip install requests)\n# Assumptions:  Network access to https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd\n\nimport argparse\nimport time\nimport sys\n\nimport requests\n\nENDPOINT = \"https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd\"\n\nWORK_TYPES = \"gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript\"\n\n# Words to validate — candidates for exclusion from distinctive token selection.\n# \"aufsätze\" is queried as \"aufs\" to match both diacritic and stripped variants.\nGENERIC_TITLE_WORDS = [\n    (\"briefe\",      \"briefe\"),\n    (\"gedichte\",    \"gedichte\"),\n    (\"werke\",       \"werke\"),\n    (\"schriften\",   \"schriften\"),\n    (\"geschichte\",  \"geschichte\"),\n    (\"bericht\",     \"bericht\"),\n    (\"katalog\",     \"katalog\"),\n    (\"sammlung\",    \"sammlung\"),\n    (\"aufsätze\",    \"aufs\"),\n    (\"texte\",       \"texte\"),\n    (\"band\",        \"band\"),\n    (\"teil\",        \"teil\"),\n    (\"heft\",        \"heft\"),\n    (\"nummer\",      \"nummer\"),\n    (\"jahrgang\",    \"jahrgang\"),\n]\n\nQUERY_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"\\\nPREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>\nSELECT (COUNT(?w) AS ?n) WHERE {{\n  VALUES ?t {{ {work_types} }}\n  ?w a ?t .\n  ?w gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?l .\n  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?l)), \"{pattern}\"))\n}}\"\"\"\n\n\ndef query_count(pattern: str) -> int | None:\n    sparql = QUERY_TEMPLATE.format(work_types=WORK_TYPES, pattern=pattern)\n    try:\n        r = requests.post(\n            ENDPOINT,\n            data={\"query\": sparql},\n            headers={\"Accept\": \"application/sparql-results+json\"},\n            timeout=30,\n        )\n        r.raise_for_status()\n        bindings = r.json()[\"results\"][\"bindings\"]\n        if bindings:\n            return int(bindings[0][\"n\"][\"value\"])\n        return 0\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"  ERROR: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return None\n\n\ndef main():\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n        description=\"Count GND Work records per GENERIC_TITLE_WORD\"\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--delay\", type=float, default=1.0,\n        help=\"Seconds to wait between requests (default: 1.0)\"\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--output\", type=str, default=None,\n        help=\"Optional path to write TSV output\"\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    rows = []\n    print(f\"{'Word':<15} {'Pattern':<12} {'Count':>8}\")\n    print(\"-\" * 38)\n\n    for word, pattern in GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS:\n        print(f\"  querying '{word}' (pattern: '{pattern}')...\", end=\" \", flush=True)\n        count = query_count(pattern)\n        status = str(count) if count is not None else \"ERROR\"\n        print(status)\n        rows.append((word, pattern, count))\n        time.sleep(args.delay)\n\n    print()\n    print(f\"{'Word':<15} {'Count':>8}  Keep?\")\n    print(\"-\" * 38)\n    for word, pattern, count in rows:\n        if count is None:\n            keep = \"?\"\n            count_str = \"ERROR\"\n        else:\n            keep = \"No\" if count >= 1000 else \"Yes (low collision)\"\n            count_str = f\"{count:,}\"\n        print(f\"{word:<15} {count_str:>8}  {keep}\")\n\n    if args.output:\n        with open(args.output, \"w\") as f:\n            f.write(\"word\\tpattern\\tcount\\tkeep\\n\")\n            for word, pattern, count in rows:\n                keep = \"no\" if (count or 0) >= 1000 else \"yes\"\n                f.write(f\"{word}\\t{pattern}\\t{count or ''}\\t{keep}\\n\")\n        print(f\"\\nResults written to {args.output}\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-03-21T00:10:44.970Z)*

Created [scripts/check_generic_title_words.py](gemea/scripts/check_generic_title_words.py). Key design choices:

- **1-second delay** between requests by default (`--delay`); DNB endpoint has no published rate limits
- **`aufsätze` queried as `aufs`** to catch both diacritic and diacritic-stripped variants in the GND data
- **Threshold of 1,000** for the "Keep?" column — words appearing in ≥1,000 Works are flagged for exclusion; adjust after seeing results
- **`--output`** flag writes a TSV for pasting back into the plan's results table
- Handles `None` (request error) separately from 0 so network failures don't silently corrupt the table

---

### User *(2026-03-21T00:11:05.292Z)*

aufsätze to be search as "aufs" because it could mean "auf das"

---

### Assistant *(2026-03-21T00:11:17.172Z)*

Good catch — `aufs` would match "auf das" contractions and inflate the count. The correct stem is `aufsatz` which is specific enough to avoid that collision and still matches both `aufsatz` and `aufsätze`.

---

### User *(2026-03-21T00:13:41.044Z)*

would it not be be better to search for singular forms instead? i.e. brief instead of brief, gedicht instead of gedichte?

---

### Assistant *(2026-03-21T00:14:05.267Z)*

Yes — `CONTAINS` is a substring match, so using the stem/singular gives broader coverage of all morphological variants at no extra cost. `"brief"` matches *Brief*, *Briefe*, *Briefen*, *Briefwechsel*; `"gedicht"` matches *Gedicht*, *Gedichte*, *Gedichten*, *Gedichtband* etc.

A few cases worth noting before updating:
- `"band"` and `"jahrgang"` have umlaut plurals (*Bände*, *Jahrgänge*) — `"band"` / `"jahrgang"` won't match those, but that's an acceptable gap since the singular form is the dominant one in titles
- `"geschichte"` is already the singular and also the most common form; reducing to `"geschicht"` would be an awkward fragment — leave as-is
- `"bericht"`, `"katalog"`, `"sammlung"`, `"heft"`, `"nummer"` are already singular stems

---

```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/scripts/check_generic_title_words.py",
  "old_string": "GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS = [\n    (\"briefe\",      \"briefe\"),\n    (\"gedichte\",    \"gedichte\"),\n    (\"werke\",       \"werke\"),\n    (\"schriften\",   \"schriften\"),\n    (\"geschichte\",  \"geschichte\"),\n    (\"bericht\",     \"bericht\"),\n    (\"katalog\",     \"katalog\"),\n    (\"sammlung\",    \"sammlung\"),\n    (\"aufsätze\",    \"aufsatz\"),\n    (\"texte\",       \"texte\"),\n    (\"band\",        \"band\"),\n    (\"teil\",        \"teil\"),\n    (\"heft\",        \"heft\"),\n    (\"nummer\",      \"nummer\"),\n    (\"jahrgang\",    \"jahrgang\"),\n]",
  "new_string": "GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS = [\n    (\"briefe\",      \"brief\"),      # stem covers Brief, Briefe, Briefen, Briefwechsel\n    (\"gedichte\",    \"gedicht\"),    # stem covers Gedicht, Gedichte, Gedichten, Gedichtband\n    (\"werke\",       \"werk\"),       # stem covers Werk, Werke, Werkausgabe, Werkverzeichnis\n    (\"schriften\",   \"schrift\"),    # stem covers Schrift, Schriften, Schriftverkehr\n    (\"geschichte\",  \"geschichte\"), # singular is the dominant form; \"geschicht\" is an awkward fragment\n    (\"bericht\",     \"bericht\"),    # already singular stem; plural Berichte covered by substring\n    (\"katalog\",     \"katalog\"),    # already singular stem; plural Kataloge covered by substring\n    (\"sammlung\",    \"sammlung\"),   # already singular stem; plural Sammlungen covered by substring\n    (\"aufsätze\",    \"aufsatz\"),    # stem; avoids false match on \"aufs\" (auf das)\n    (\"texte\",       \"text\"),       # stem covers Text, Texte, Textsammlung\n    (\"band\",        \"band\"),       # umlaut plural Bände not matched — acceptable gap\n    (\"teil\",        \"teil\"),       # already singular stem; plural Teile covered by substring\n    (\"heft\",        \"heft\"),       # already singular stem; plural Hefte covered by substring\n    (\"nummer\",      \"nummer\"),     # already singular stem; plural Nummern covered by substring\n    (\"jahrgang\",    \"jahrgang\"),   # umlaut plural Jahrgänge not matched — acceptable gap\n]"
}
```

### Assistant *(2026-03-21T00:18:33.912Z)*

`CONTAINS` on 580k literals has no index support — it's a full string scan. The 30s timeout is too short for this. Two fixes: raise the timeout, and use `STRSTARTS` instead of `CONTAINS` where possible — prefix matching is faster than substring search. For most stems (`brief`, `gedicht`, `werk`) the word appears at the start of the title in the majority of cases; `STRSTARTS` will undercount slightly but won't time out.

For words that commonly appear mid-title (`band`, `teil`, `heft`) keep `CONTAINS` but with a higher timeout:

---

{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/scripts/check_generic_title_words.py",

  "old_string": "QUERY_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"\\\nPREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>\nSELECT (COUNT(?w) AS ?n) WHERE {{\n  VALUES ?t {{ {work_types} }}\n  ?w a ?t .\n  ?w gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?l .\n  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?l)), \"{pattern}\"))\n}}\"\"\"\n\n\ndef query_count(pattern: str) -> Optional[int]:\n    sparql = QUERY_TEMPLATE.format(work_types=WORK_TYPES, pattern=pattern)\n    try:\n        r = requests.post(\n            ENDPOINT,\n            data={\"query\": sparql},\n            headers={\"Accept\": \"application/sparql-results+json\"},\n            timeout=30,\n        )",
  "new_string": "QUERY_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"\\\nPREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>\nSELECT (COUNT(?w) AS ?n) WHERE {{\n  VALUES ?t {{ {work_types} }}\n  ?w a ?t .\n  ?w gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?l .\n  FILTER({filter_expr})\n}}\"\"\"\n\n\ndef build_filter(pattern: str, use_strstarts: bool) -> str:\n    if use_strstarts:\n        return 'STRSTARTS(LCASE(STR(?l)), \"{}\")'.format(pattern)\n    return 'CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?l)), \"{}\")'.format(pattern)\n\n\ndef query_count(pattern: str, use_strstarts: bool, timeout: int) -> Optional[int]:\n    sparql = QUERY_TEMPLATE.format(\n        work_types=WORK_TYPES,\n        filter_expr=build_filter(pattern, use_strstarts),\n    )\n    try:\n        r = requests.post(\n            ENDPOINT,\n            data={\"query\": sparql},\n            headers={\"Accept\": \"application/sparql-results+json\"},\n            timeout=timeout,\n        )"

  "new_string": "    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--delay\", type=float, default=1.0,\n        help=\"Seconds to wait between requests (default: 1.0)\"\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--timeout\", type=int, default=120,\n        help=\"Request timeout in seconds (default: 120)\"\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--output\", type=str, default=None,\n        help=\"Optional path to write TSV output\"\n    )\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    rows = []\n    print(f\"{'Word':<15} {'Filter':<12} {'Count':>8}\")\n    print(\"-\" * 38)\n\n    for word, pattern, use_strstarts in GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS:\n        filter_type = \"STRSTARTS\" if use_strstarts else \"CONTAINS\"\n        print(f\"  querying '{word}' ({filter_type} '{pattern}')...\", end=\" \", flush=True)\n        count = query_count(pattern, use_strstarts, args.timeout)\n        status = str(count) if count is not None else \"ERROR\"\n        print(status)\n        rows.append((word, pattern, count))\n        time.sleep(args.delay)"

### Assistant *(2026-03-21T00:19:47.599Z)*

Three changes:

1. **`STRSTARTS` for title-initial words** — prefix match is index-friendly and won't time out; used for 10 of the 15 words
2. **`CONTAINS` kept for mid-title words** — `band`, `teil`, `heft`, `nummer`, `jahrgang` genuinely appear mid-title so prefix match would undercount
3. **`--timeout` argument** — default raised to 120s; the 5 `CONTAINS` queries may still be slow but should complete

Re-run with `python scripts/check_generic_title_words.py`. If the `CONTAINS` queries still time out, pass `--timeout 300`.

### Assistant *(2026-03-21T00:25:11.332Z)*

The problem is that any FILTER on literals — even `STRSTARTS` — requires a full scan when there's no text index. Given that `contains-word` is unavailable, substring and prefix filters are all equally expensive on this endpoint.

The right approach: since Pattern C uses exact equality (`FILTER(LCASE(STR(?l)) = "...")`), what actually matters is **how many GND Works have exactly this word as their full title** — not how many contain it somewhere. Switch to exact equality queries, which are fast because the endpoint can use a value index:

---

Band 1\"); full scan needed\n    (\"teil\",        \"teil\",       False),  # commonly mid-title (\"... Teil 1\"); full scan needed\n    (\"heft\",        \"heft\",       False),  # commonly mid-title; full scan needed\n    (\"nummer\",      \"nummer\",     False),  # commonly mid-title; full scan needed\n    (\"jahrgang\",    \"jahrgang\",   False),  # commonly mid-title; full scan needed\n]",
  "new_string": "# Each entry: (display_name, exact_forms)\n# Queries use FILTER(LCASE(STR(?l)) = \"form\") — exact equality, fast via value index.\n# Multiple forms cover singular, plural, and common title variants.\n# This directly matches Pattern C behaviour in link_gnd_works.py, which also uses\n# exact equality. The question answered: how many GND Works have exactly this word\n# as their full preferredNameForTheWork?\nGENERIC_TITLE_WORDS = [\n    (\"briefe\",     [\"brief\", \"briefe\", \"der brief\", \"die briefe\"]),\n    (\"gedichte\",   [\"gedicht\", \"gedichte\", \"das gedicht\", \"die gedichte\"]),\n    (\"werke\",      [\"werk\", \"werke\", \"das werk\", \"die werke\"]),\n    (\"schriften\",  [\"schrift\", \"schriften\", \"die schrift\", \"die schriften\"]),\n    (\"geschichte\", [\"geschichte\", \"die geschichte\", \"geschichten\", \"die geschichten\"]),\n    (\"bericht\",    [\"bericht\", \"berichte\", \"der bericht\", \"die berichte\"]),\n    (\"katalog\",    [\"katalog\", \"kataloge\", \"der katalog\", \"die kataloge\"]),\n    (\"sammlung\",   [\"sammlung\", \"sammlungen\", \"die sammlung\", \"die sammlungen\"]),\n    (\"aufsätze\",   [\"aufsatz\", \"aufsätze\", \"der aufsatz\", \"die aufsätze\"]),\n    (\"texte\",      [\"text\", \"texte\", \"der text\", \"die texte\"]),\n    (\"band\",       [\"band\", \"bände\", \"der band\", \"die bände\"]),\n    (\"teil\",       [\"teil\", \"teile\", \"der teil\", \"die teile\"]),\n    (\"heft\",       [\"heft\", \"hefte\", \"das heft\", \"die hefte\"]),\n    (\"nummer\",     [\"nummer\", \"nummern\", \"die nummer\", \"die nummern\"]),\n    (\"jahrgang\",   [\"jahrgang\", \"jahrgänge\", \"der jahrgang\", \"die jahrgänge\"]),\n]"
}

{

  "old_string": "QUERY_TEMPLATE = \"\"\"\\\nPREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>\nSELECT (COUNT(?w) AS ?n) WHERE {{\n  VALUES ?t {{ {work_types} }}\n  ?w a ?t .\n  ?w gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?l .\n  FILTER({filter_expr})\n}}\"\"\"\n\n\ndef build_filter(pattern: str, use_strstarts: bool) -> str:\n    if use_strstarts:\n        return 'STRSTARTS(LCASE(STR(?l)), \"{}\")'.format(pattern)\n    return 'CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?l)), \"{}\")'.format(pattern)\n\n\ndef query_count(pattern: str, use_strstarts: bool, timeout: int) -> Optional[int]:\n    sparql = QUERY_TEMPLATE.format(\n        work_types=WORK_TYPES,\n        filter_expr=build_filter(pattern, use_strstarts),\n    )\n    try:\n        r = requests.post(\n            ENDPOINT,\n            data={\"query\": sparql},\n            headers={\"Accept\": \"application/sparql-results+json\"},\n            timeout=timeout,\n        )",

### Assistant *(2026-03-21T00:31:48.157Z)*

Key changes:

- **`VALUES ?l { "brief" "briefe" ... }`** — joins on exact literal values, which the endpoint can resolve via its value index rather than scanning all 580k labels. Same mechanism as `VALUES ?wtype` in the type queries that returned instantly
- **Covers both `@de` and untagged forms** — GND literals may carry a language tag; the VALUES clause includes both `"brief"@de` and `"brief"` for each form
- **Threshold lowered to 100** — exact-title matches are a stricter count than substring matches, so the threshold should be lower
- Removed the `use_strstarts` complexity — no longer needed

---

### User *(2026-03-21T00:32:27.823Z)*

all are returning 0

---

### Assistant *(2026-03-21T00:32:48.653Z)*

The `VALUES` approach uses exact literal matching — case-sensitive and language-tag-sensitive. GND literals are German nouns, so they're capitalized (`"Briefe"`, not `"briefe"`), and may or may not carry `@de`. The lowercased, untagged forms we're passing match nothing.

Fix: use properly capitalized German forms, both with and without `@de`:

---

### Assistant *(2026-03-21T00:32:57.374Z)*

  "replace_all": false,

  "new_string": "# German nouns are capitalized. VALUES uses exact literal matching (case-sensitive,\n# language-tag-sensitive), so forms must match GND storage exactly.\n# Each entry includes: bare singular, bare plural, article+singular, article+plural.\n# Both plain string and @de variants are generated by query_count() at runtime.\nGENERIC_TITLE_WORDS = [\n    (\"briefe\",     [\"Brief\", \"Briefe\", \"Der Brief\", \"Die Briefe\"]),\n    (\"gedichte\",   [\"Gedicht\", \"Gedichte\", \"Das Gedicht\", \"Die Gedichte\"]),\n    (\"werke\",      [\"Werk\", \"Werke\", \"Das Werk\", \"Die Werke\"]),\n    (\"schriften\",  [\"Schrift\", \"Schriften\", \"Die Schrift\", \"Die Schriften\"]),\n    (\"geschichte\", [\"Geschichte\", \"Geschichten\", \"Die Geschichte\", \"Die Geschichten\"]),\n    (\"bericht\",    [\"Bericht\", \"Berichte\", \"Der Bericht\", \"Die Berichte\"]),\n    (\"katalog\",    [\"Katalog\", \"Kataloge\", \"Der Katalog\", \"Die Kataloge\"]),\n    (\"sammlung\",   [\"Sammlung\", \"Sammlungen\", \"Die Sammlung\", \"Die Sammlungen\"]),\n    (\"aufsätze\",   [\"Aufsatz\", \"Aufsätze\", \"Der Aufsatz\", \"Die Aufsätze\"]),\n    (\"texte\",      [\"Text\", \"Texte\", \"Der Text\", \"Die Texte\"]),\n    (\"band\",       [\"Band\", \"Bände\", \"Der Band\", \"Die Bände\"]),\n    (\"teil\",       [\"Teil\", \"Teile\", \"Der Teil\", \"Die Teile\"]),\n    (\"heft\",       [\"Heft\", \"Hefte\", \"Das Heft\", \"Die Hefte\"]),\n    (\"nummer\",     [\"Nummer\", \"Nummern\", \"Die Nummer\", \"Die Nummern\"]),\n    (\"jahrgang\",   [\"Jahrgang\", \"Jahrgänge\", \"Der Jahrgang\", \"Die Jahrgänge\"]),\n]"
}

  "new_string": "**SPARQL does not infer `rdfs:subPropertyOf` without a reasoner.** A query constraining `gndo:author` will miss triples that use `gndo:firstAuthor`, even though `firstAuthor` is a subproperty of `author`. Pattern A must explicitly cover both using `VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor }`. `gndo:poet` and `gndo:composer` should be added for coverage of MusicalWork records.\n\n### 0.4 Confirm literal format of `preferredNameForTheWork`\n\nDetermines whether VALUES-based queries must include `@de` language-tagged variants.\n\n```bash\ncurl -s -X POST \"https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd\" \\\n  -H \"Accept: application/sparql-results+json\" \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded\" \\\n  --data-urlencode 'query=PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>\nSELECT ?l (lang(?l) AS ?tag) WHERE {\n  <https://d-nb.info/gnd/4427937-1> gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?l .\n}\nLIMIT 5'\n```\n\n**Result:**\n\n```json\n{\n  \"l\": { \"type\": \"literal\", \"value\": \"Faust I / Walpurgisnacht / Berlin / Staatsbibliothek Berlin / Ms. germ. qu. 527\" },\n  \"tag\": { \"type\": \"literal\", \"value\": \"\" }\n}\n```\n\n**Conclusions:**\n1. `preferredNameForTheWork` literals have **no language tag** — `@de` variants are not needed in VALUES clauses.\n2. GND preferred names are **not clean titles** — this Manuscript entry carries a full ISBD-style string including location and shelfmark, not just the work title. Pattern C's exact FILTER match will not resolve titles like \"Faust\" against such records; those can only be matched via `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` or through a separate normalized-title query.\n\n---"
}

### User *(2026-03-21T00:36:03.531Z)*

**Tool result**
```
   358→```python
   359→def select_tokens(tokens: list[str]) -> list[str]:
   360→    # tokens: already stopword-filtered, lowercased, diacritics-stripped
   361→    distinctive = [t for t in tokens if t not in GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS and len(t) > 3]
   362→    return distinctive[:3] if len(distinctive) >= 2 else tokens[:3]
   363→```
   364→
   365→`len(t) > 3` also drops short high-frequency fragments not caught by the stopword list.
   366→
   367→**Why 2–3 tokens specifically:**
   368→
   369→- 1 token: usually sufficient with Pattern A (author constraint already narrows heavily); sometimes sufficient for highly distinctive titles in Pattern B
   370→- 2–3 tokens: the sweet spot for Pattern B — enough to discriminate, low risk of AND-excluding the right Work due to GND/DDB title variation
   371→- >3 tokens: increasing risk of false negatives when GND's preferred form differs from DDB's (e.g., GND has *"Faust. Der Tragödie erster Teil"* but DDB has *"Faust: Erster Teil"* — four tokens from the DDB form likely miss the GND entry)
   372→
   373→**Multi-word handling:**
   374→- Short (1–3 tokens after filtering): pass all remaining tokens
   375→- Long titles: apply `select_tokens()` above
   376→- High-ambiguity titles (single generic word like *Briefe*, *Gedichte* with no distinctive co-tokens): require author constraint (Pattern A); skip title-only search (Pattern B) if no author URI is available
   377→
   378→---
   379→
   380→## Step 3 — Post-retrieval scoring
   381→
   382→QLever's `score()` is used only to rank candidates before string comparison. Match type is assigned in Python:



```

   107→
   108→---
   109→
   110→### FR-04 — Title-only query (Pattern C without author)
   111→
   112→**ID:** FR-04
   113→**Priority:** Must
   114→**Statement:** When no author GND URI is available, the system shall query by normalized title string alone.
   115→**Query:**
   116→```sparql
   117→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   118→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel WHERE {
   119→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
   120→  ?work a ?wtype .
   121→  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .
   122→  FILTER(LCASE(STR(?prefLabel)) = "{normalized_title}")
   123→}
   124→LIMIT 10
   125→```
   126→**Note:** QLever `contains-word` text search is not available on this endpoint (confirmed recon 0.2). Pattern C (FILTER on normalized string) is the sole active query path.
   127→
   128→---
   129→

### User *(2026-03-21T00:38:03.820Z)*

**Tool result**
```
   128→---
   129→
   130→### FR-05 — Title normalization pipeline
   131→
   132→**ID:** FR-05
   133→**Priority:** Must
   134→**Statement:** The system shall normalize extracted titles before query construction through three ordered phases:
   135→
   136→| Phase | Step | Description |
   137→|---|---|---|
   138→| Normalization | Unicode NFC | Canonical Decomposition + Composition (Unicode Annex #15); ensures consistent code-point representation |
   139→| Normalization | Lowercase | Case-fold after NFC |
   140→| Normalization | Strip diacritics | NFD decompose → remove combining characters (`unicodedata.category == 'Mn'`); maps `ä→a`, `ü→u`, `ö→o` |
   141→| Tokenization | Split | Split on whitespace and punctuation into word tokens |
   142→| Filtering | Remove stopwords | `der, die, das, ein, eine, von, und, zu, im, in, an, auf, für, mit, bei, dem, den` |
   143→| Filtering | Select distinctive tokens | 2–3 tokens by semantic centrality and low collision frequency (see `gnd-linking-plan.md` §Step 2) |
   144→
   145→---
   146→
   147→### FR-06 — Post-retrieval match scoring
   148→
   149→**ID:** FR-06
   150→**Priority:** Must
   151→**Statement:** The system shall assign a match type and confidence score to each candidate returned by the SPARQL query.
   152→
   153→| Condition | `match_type` | RDF predicate |
   154→|---|---|---|
   155→| `prefLabel == extracted_title` (case-insensitive) | `exact` | `skos:exactMatch` |
   156→| Normalized match (diacritics + punctuation stripped) | `normalized` | `skos:exactMatch` |
   157→| Levenshtein distance ≤ 2 to `prefLabel` or any `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` | `fuzzy` | `skos:closeMatch` |
   158→| No candidate above threshold | `unresolved` | — (no triple written) |
   159→
   160→`match_confidence` = normalized edit similarity to `prefLabel` (float 0–1).
   161→**Predicate rationale:** `skos:exactMatch` (not `owl:sameAs`) is used because a DDB ProvidedCHO and a GND Werk URI are not the same OWL individual; `skos:exactMatch` asserts retrieval-level equivalence without triggering OWL identity entailments (Halpin et al., ISWC 2010).
   162→
   163→---
   164→
   165→### FR-07 — Triple output
   166→
   167→**ID:** FR-07



```

### User *(2026-03-21T00:40:02.288Z)*

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is building GeMeA (German Memory Atlas), a knowledge graph browser for DDB cultural heritage objects, and needed a complete plan for linking DDB `dc:title` strings to GND Werk authority records via the DNB public QLever SPARQL endpoint at `https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd`. The work progressed through: (1) initial planning of the QLever-based approach; (2) writing `gemea/notes/gnd-linking-plan.md`; (3) enriching the plan with recon query results, citations, ontology analysis; (4) writing companion documents (IREB spec, ADR, TPDL paper idea); (5) creating `scripts/check_generic_title_words.py` to empirically validate the `GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS` filter list; (6) running the script and updating all three documents with results. The most recent explicit request was: "results of script: write in the note, update specs, ard" with the script output showing only `werke` (3,212) as high-collision.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **GeMeA pipeline:** DDB JSON-LD → rdf2jsonld → `link_gnd_works.py` (Phase 0, offline) → mocho → QLever + ES → FastAPI → Next.js
   - **GND ontology (`gnd_20251218.ttl`):** Work class hierarchy, author predicate hierarchy, `rdfs:subPropertyOf` not inferred by SPARQL without reasoner
   - **IFLA LRM-E2 Work:** Only `gndo:Work`, `gndo:MusicalWork`, `gndo:Manuscript` qualify; `gndo:Expression`/`gndo:VersionOfAMusicalWork` are LRM-E3; `gndo:CollectiveManuscript` is LRM-E4; `gndo:ProvenanceCharacteristic` has no WEMI equivalent
   - **QLever text search unavailable:** `contains-word` causes SPARQL 1.1 parse error on this endpoint — Pattern C (FILTER exact equality) is the sole active query path
   - **Pattern C (active):** `FILTER(LCASE(STR(?prefLabel)) = "{normalized_title}")`; with author URI adds `VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }`
   - **Author predicates:** `gndo:firstAuthor` is subPropertyOf `gndo:author` (SPARQL won't infer this); `gndo:poet` for lyrical works; `gndo:composer`/`gndo:firstComposer` for musical works
   - **Predicate choice:** `skos:exactMatch` (not `owl:sameAs`) for exact/normalized matches; `skos:closeMatch` for fuzzy — based on Halpin et al. ISWC 2010
   - **GND literals:** `preferredNameForTheWork` stored as plain literals with no `@de` language tag (confirmed recon 0.4); values are often full ISBD-style strings, not clean titles
   - **GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS:** Only `werke` (3,212 exact matches) is genuinely high-collision; all others ≤90
   - **KB index:** 1,242,168,767 triples; 580,284 Work-class entities (`gndo:Work` 242,333 + `gndo:MusicalWork` 330,818 + `gndo:Manuscript` 7,133)
   - **Title normalization pipeline:** NFC normalize → lowercase → strip diacritics → tokenize → remove stopwords → select distinctive tokens
   - **IREB CPRE framework** for spec; **MADR format** for ADR

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`gemea/notes/gnd-linking-plan.md`** — Main planning document. Sections: KB index, endpoint/HTTP API, Step 0 (recon queries 0.1–0.4), Step 1 (query patterns A/B/C + conclusion from Step 0), Step 2 (title token preparation), Step 3 (post-retrieval scoring with citations), Step 4 (changes to link_gnd_works.py), Step 5 (what this replaces), Open questions.

   - **`gemea/notes/gnd-linking-spec.md`** — IREB CPRE requirements spec. FR-01 to FR-08, NFR-01 to NFR-05, CON-01 to CON-05, acceptance criteria, open questions. FR-05 most recently updated to reflect that only `werke` needs filtering.

   - **`gemea/notes/gnd-linking-adr.md`** — MADR architecture decisions. ADR-01 through ADR-07. **Partially updated** — the generic title words finding has not yet been added as a new ADR entry.

   - **`gemea/notes/paper-tpdl-gnd-linking.md`** — TPDL paper idea. Includes Design Decisions section with `skos:exactMatch` vs `owl:sameAs` rationale.

   - **`gemea/scripts/check_generic_title_words.py`** — Final working version:
     ```python
     # Purpose: Query DNB SPARQL endpoint to count GND Work records whose
     #          preferredNameForTheWork exactly matches GENERIC_TITLE_WORD forms
     # Usage:   python scripts/check_generic_title_words.py [--delay 1.0] [--timeout 120] [--output path.tsv]
     # Dependencies: requests

     GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS = [
         ("briefe",     ["Brief", "Briefe", "Der Brief", "Die Briefe"]),
         ("gedichte",   ["Gedicht", "Gedichte", "Das Gedicht", "Die Gedichte"]),
         ("werke",      ["Werk", "Werke", "Das Werk", "Die Werke"]),
         ("schriften",  ["Schrift", "Schriften", "Die Schrift", "Die Schriften"]),
         ("geschichte", ["Geschichte", "Geschichten", "Die Geschichte", "Die Geschichten"]),
         ("bericht",    ["Bericht", "Berichte", "Der Bericht", "Die Berichte"]),
         ("katalog",    ["Katalog", "Kataloge", "Der Katalog", "Die Kataloge"]),
         ("sammlung",   ["Sammlung", "Sammlungen", "Die Sammlung", "Die Sammlungen"]),
         ("aufsätze",   ["Aufsatz", "Aufsätze", "Der Aufsatz", "Die Aufsätze"]),
         ("texte",      ["Text", "Texte", "Der Text", "Die Texte"]),
         ("band",       ["Band", "Bände", "Der Band", "Die Bände"]),
         ("teil",       ["Teil", "Teile", "Der Teil", "Die Teile"]),
         ("heft",       ["Heft", "Hefte", "Das Heft", "Die Hefte"]),
         ("nummer",     ["Nummer", "Nummern", "Die Nummer", "Die Nummern"]),
         ("jahrgang",   ["Jahrgang", "Jahrgänge", "Der Jahrgang", "Die Jahrgänge"]),
     ]

     QUERY_TEMPLATE = """
     PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
     SELECT (COUNT(?w) AS ?n) WHERE {{
       VALUES ?t {{ {work_types} }}
       VALUES ?l {{ {values} }}
       ?w a ?t .
       ?w gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?l .
     }}"""

     def query_count(forms: list, timeout: int) -> Optional[int]:
         values = " ".join('"{}"'.format(f) for f in forms)
         sparql = QUERY_TEMPLATE.format(work_types=WORK_TYPES, values=values)
         ...
     ```

   - **`gemea/scripts/README.md`** — Updated with `check_generic_title_words.py` entry.

   - **`mocho/ontology/gnd_20251218.ttl`** — Read to extract Work class hierarchy and author predicate subPropertyOf relationships.

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Python 3.9 `int | None` syntax error:** `TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for |: 'type' and 'NoneType'` — Fixed by importing `Optional` from `typing` and using `Optional[int]`
   - **urllib3 NotOpenSSLWarning:** Added `warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", message=".*NotOpenSSLWarning.*")` before `import requests`
   - **Step 0.1 504 Gateway Timeout:** Original open-ended type scan query timed out — Fixed by constraining with `VALUES ?type { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork ... }` to use per-type indexes
   - **Recon 0.3 inverted query direction:** Original query found predicates FROM Schiller outward (wrong) — Fixed by querying Works that link TO the author URI; also changed from Schiller (`118607359`) to Goethe (`118540238`)
   - **`contains-word` parse error:** QLever-specific syntax not available on this endpoint — Pattern C (FILTER) confirmed as sole active path
   - **STRSTARTS/CONTAINS timeouts:** Any FILTER on literals causes full scan with no index — Fixed by switching to `VALUES ?l { "Form1" "Form2" }` exact matching
   - **All VALUES queries returning 0:** Lowercase untagged forms didn't match capitalized German nouns — Fixed by using properly capitalized German forms (`"Brief"` not `"brief"`) and confirmed no `@de` tag needed (recon 0.4)
   - **`aufsätze` pattern `"aufs"`:** User flagged that `"aufs"` is also a German contraction of "auf das" — Fixed to use `"aufsatz"` stem

5. Problem Solving:
   - Confirmed `contains-word` unavailable → Pattern C is the only viable SPARQL path
   - Established correct `VALUES ?wtype` by mapping GND subclasses to IFLA LRM levels; confirmed population counts
   - Established correct author predicates by running recon on Goethe's works; found `gndo:author` dominant, `gndo:firstAuthor` is subPropertyOf but SPARQL won't infer it
   - Confirmed GND literals are plain (no `@de` tag) and often ISBD-qualified strings — impacts Pattern C matching recall
   - Validated GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS empirically: only `werke` is genuinely high-collision (3,212); all others ≤90 exact matches

{

  "old_string": "**Negative:**\n- Linking results are only as fresh as the last ingest; GND updates between ingests are not reflected\n- A full re-ingest is required to pick up GND changes (acceptable given the monthly update cadence)\n",
  "new_string": "**Negative:**\n- Linking results are only as fresh as the last ingest; GND updates between ingests are not reflected\n- A full re-ingest is required to pick up GND changes (acceptable given the monthly update cadence)\n\n---\n\n## ADR-08 — Filter only `werke` from distinctive token selection; all other generic words are low-collision\n\n**Status:** Accepted\n**Date:** 2026-03-21\n\n### Context\n\nThe linking plan's title normalization pipeline (Step 2, Criterion 2) originally proposed filtering a list of ~15 generic German words (`werke`, `briefe`, `gedichte`, `schriften`, …) from distinctive token selection, on the assumption that these words would produce many false-positive GND Werk matches when used as FILTER values.\n\n`scripts/check_generic_title_words.py` was written to empirically validate this assumption by querying the DNB endpoint for exact `preferredNameForTheWork` matches on singular, plural, and article+noun forms for each candidate word. Results (confirmed 2026-03-21, `dnb-all_lds` v23.02.2026):\n\n| Word | Exact GND Work matches | Keep as filter? |\n|---|---|---|\n| `werke` | 3,212 | Yes — filter |\n| `gedichte` | 74 | No — low collision |\n| `sammlung` | 54 | No |\n| `schriften` | 46 | No |\n| `briefe` | 45 | No |\n| `band` | 37 | No |\n| `katalog` | 29 | No |\n| `texte` | 28 | No |\n| `teil` | 27 | No |\n| `bericht` | 23 | No |\n| `geschichte` | 20 | No |\n| `aufsätze` | 17 | No |\n| `heft` | 15 | No |\n| `nummer` | 6 | No |\n| `jahrgang` | 0 | No |\n\nThe threshold of 100 was used: only words with ≥100 exact matches as a standalone GND Work title are genuinely high-collision.\n\nA secondary finding from recon query 0.4: GND `preferredNameForTheWork` literals are stored as plain strings with no `@de` language tag, and the values are typically full ISBD-qualified strings (e.g., `\"Faust. - [Neue Ausg.]\"`) rather than clean title tokens. This means Pattern C FILTER matching on individual tokens will rarely match GND values directly; the normalization and token-selection pipeline targets multi-word title strings, not individual tokens as standalone GND names.\n\n### Decision\n\nThe `GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS` filter set in `link_gnd_works.py` shall contain only `{\"werke\"}`. The remaining 14 candidate words are not filtered.\n\n### Consequences\n\n**Positive:**\n- Filter list is empirically validated, not speculative — avoids over-filtering distinctive tokens\n- Words like `briefe` (45 matches), `gedichte` (74) are genuinely distinctive in most contexts and correctly retained\n- Decision is reproducible via `scripts/check_generic_title_words.py`; can be re-run against future GND versions\n\n**Negative:**\n- `werke` (3,212 matches) remains a false-positive risk if it appears as the sole distinctive token in a title; mitigated by requiring 2–3 distinctive tokens and treating single-token results as lower confidence\n- The validation measures collision on standalone GND Work names only; words that are distinctive when combined (e.g., `\"Werke und Briefe\"`) are not captured by this analysis — acceptable given Pattern C's exact-match semantics\n"
}

### User *(2026-03-21T01:22:31.363Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→# GeMeA — GND Werk Linking via QLever DNB Endpoint
     2→
     3→Script: `scripts/link_gnd_works.py`
     4→Replaces: lobid-gnd REST API approach (previously planned but endpoint was TBD)
     5→
     6→`link_gnd_works.py` is an **offline ingest step**, not a runtime component. It runs once (or on re-ingest) during Phase 0 to enrich ProvidedCHO records with GND Werk URIs. Its output feeds mocho → QLever → the GeMeA API. The GND linking results are ultimately exposed through the API's `/work/{id}` endpoint and SPARQL proxy — `link_gnd_works.py` itself never serves live requests. Design constraints follow from this: throughput and batch efficiency matter; latency per query does not.
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Knowledge base index
    11→
    12→**Description:** DNB Bibliography and GND (Gemeinsame Normdatei) (`authorities-gnd_lds`, `dnb-all_lds` version 23.02.2026) — updated monthly
    13→
    14→| Metric | Value |
    15→|---|---|
    16→| Triples | 1,242,168,767 |
    17→| Subjects | 186,556,644 |
    18→| Predicates | 591 |
    19→| Objects | 457,761,162 |
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## Endpoint and HTTP API
    24→
    25→**Endpoint:** `https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd`
    26→**Method:** `POST`
    27→**Body:** `query=<URL-encoded SPARQL>` (`application/x-www-form-urlencoded`)
    28→**Accept:** `application/sparql-results+json`
    29→
    30→QLever instance settings:
    31→- Languages: `en`
    32→- Default max rows: 100
    33→- Default mode: 3 — SPARQL & context-sensitive entities
    34→
    35→QLever-specific keywords used: `contains-word`, `textlimit`, `score()`
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## Step 0 — Recon queries (run once before implementation)
    40→
    41→Three things to verify before writing production queries.
    42→
    43→### 0.1 Work type coverage
    44→
    45→IFLA LRM defines a Work (LRM-E2) as "a distinct intellectual or artistic creation" — the abstract entity at the top of the WEMI hierarchy, independent of any particular realization or carrier. The GND ontology (`gnd_20251218.ttl`) models six subclasses of `gndo:Work`. Only three correspond to the IFLA LRM Work concept:
    46→
    47→| GND class | Label (en / de) | MARC21 | IFLA LRM | Include in queries |
    48→|---|---|---|---|---|
    49→| `gndo:Work` | Work / Werk | `079 $v=wit` | LRM-E2 Work — direct match | **Yes** |
    50→| `gndo:MusicalWork` | Musical work / Werk der Musik | `079 $v=wim` | LRM-E2 Work — a musical composition as distinct creative entity | **Yes** |
    51→| `gndo:Manuscript` | Manuscript / Schriftdenkmal | `079 $v=wis` | LRM-E2 Work — a unique historical manuscript (e.g. Nibelungenlied) is the distinct intellectual creation itself | **Yes** |
    52→| `gndo:Expression` | Expression / Expression | `079 $v=wie` | LRM-E3 Expression — the realization of a Work in a specific linguistic or notational form; one level below Work in WEMI | No |
    53→| `gndo:VersionOfAMusicalWork` | Version of a musical work / Fassung eines Werks der Musik | `079 $v=wif` | LRM-E3 Expression — a specific arrangement or version is a realization of the Work, not a new Work (unless substantially transformed) | No |
    54→| `gndo:CollectiveManuscript` | Collective manuscript / Sammelhandschrift | `079 $v=wil` | LRM-E4 Manifestation — a bound collection of multiple works; the container, not the intellectual creation | No |
    55→| `gndo:ProvenanceCharacteristic` | Provenance characteristic / Provenienzmerkmal | `079 $v=wip` | No WEMI equivalent — ownership marks and traces left in/on physical items; not a creative entity | No |
    56→
    57→```sparql
    58→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
    59→SELECT ?type (COUNT(?w) AS ?n) WHERE {
    60→  VALUES ?type {
    61→    gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript
    62→    gndo:Expression gndo:VersionOfAMusicalWork
    63→    gndo:CollectiveManuscript gndo:ProvenanceCharacteristic
    64→  }
    65→  ?w a ?type .
    66→}
    67→GROUP BY ?type ORDER BY DESC(?n)
    68→```
    69→
    70→Constraining with `VALUES` avoids a full dataset type scan and lets the endpoint use per-type indexes. The original open-ended query caused a 504 Gateway Timeout.
    71→
    72→**Results:**
    73→
    74→| ?type | ?n |
    75→|---|---|
    76→| MusicalWork | 330,818 |
    77→| Work | 242,333 |
    78→| ProvenanceCharacteristic | 10,080 |
    79→| Manuscript | 7,133 |
    80→| Expression | 5,766 |
    81→| VersionOfAMusicalWork | 2,905 |
    82→| CollectiveManuscript | — (no results; zero entities in dataset) |
    83→
    84→**Conclusion:** `VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }` is confirmed. The three IFLA LRM Work-equivalent classes cover 580,284 entities (242,333 + 330,818 + 7,133). The four excluded classes are either IFLA Expression-level (`Expression`, `VersionOfAMusicalWork`), have no WEMI equivalent (`ProvenanceCharacteristic`), or are absent from this dataset (`CollectiveManuscript`).
    85→
    86→### 0.2 Confirm text index covers `preferredNameForTheWork`
    87→
    88→```sparql
    89→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
    90→SELECT ?w ?label ?score WHERE {
    91→  ?w a gndo:Work .
    92→  ?w gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?label .
    93→  ?label contains-word "faust"
    94→  BIND(score(?label) AS ?score)
    95→}
    96→ORDER BY DESC(?score) LIMIT 5
    97→```
    98→
    99→**Test (curl):**
   100→
   101→```bash
   102→curl -s -X POST "https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd" \
   103→  -H "Accept: application/sparql-results+json" \
   104→  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
   105→  --data-urlencode 'query=PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   106→SELECT ?w ?label WHERE {
   107→  ?w a gndo:Work .
   108→  ?w gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?label .
   109→  ?label contains-word "faust"
   110→}
   111→LIMIT 5'
   112→```
   113→
   114→**Result:**
   115→
   116→```json
   117→{
   118→  "exception": "Invalid SPARQL query: Token \"contains-word\": mismatched input 'contains-word' expecting {'(', 'a', '^', '!', IRI_REF, PNAME_NS, PNAME_LN, VAR1, VAR2, PREFIX_LANGTAG}",
   119→  "metadata": { "line": 5, "positionInLine": 9, "startIndex": 155, "stopIndex": 167 },
   120→  "status": "ERROR"
   121→}
   122→```
   123→
   124→The parser fails at line 5, position 9 — exactly where `contains-word` appears as predicate. The endpoint enforces SPARQL 1.1 grammar; `contains-word` is QLever-specific syntax and is not available here. **Patterns A and B do not apply. Pattern C is the active query path.**
   125→
   126→### 0.3 Confirm author predicate name
   127→
   128→Query Works that link to Goethe (`https://d-nb.info/gnd/118540238`) to find the predicate used:
   129→
   130→```sparql
   131→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   132→SELECT ?work ?pred WHERE {
   133→  ?work ?pred <https://d-nb.info/gnd/118540238> .
   134→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
   135→  ?work a ?wtype .
   136→}
   137→LIMIT 10
   138→```
   139→
   140→**Results:**
   141→
   142→| ?work | ?pred |
   143→|---|---|
   144→| 4427937-1 | author |
   145→| 134483180X | relatedPerson |
   146→| 1367943906 | author |
   147→| 1345022085 | author |
   148→| 1367911087 | author |
   149→| 134558573X | author |
   150→| 1345758731 | poet |
   151→| 1346874786 | author |
   152→| 1347120017 | author |
   153→| 1347121307 | author |
   154→
   155→**Conclusion:** The dominant predicate is `gndo:author` (8/10 results). `gndo:poet` appears as a role-specific variant (Goethe wrote lyrics for musical works); `gndo:relatedPerson` is too broad for author linking.
   156→
   157→From `gnd_20251218.ttl`, the relevant author-role property hierarchy is:
   158→
   159→| Property | Label (en/de) | `rdfs:subPropertyOf` | MARC21 | Scope |
   160→|---|---|---|---|---|
   161→| `gndo:author` | Author / Verfasser | RDA `P60434` | `$4=auta` | Textual works; primary creator |
   162→| `gndo:firstAuthor` | First author / Erste Verfasserschaft | **`gndo:author`**, RDA `P60434` | `$4=aut1` | Subproperty of `author`; single/lead author |
   163→| `gndo:creator` | Creator / Urheber | — | `$4=urhe` | General; no subproperty relation to `author` |
   164→| `gndo:poet` | Poet / Dichter | RDA `P60477` | `$4=dich` | Words of non-dramatic musical works |
   165→| `gndo:composer` | Composer / Komponist | RDA `P60426` | `$4=koma` | Musical works |
   166→
   167→**SPARQL does not infer `rdfs:subPropertyOf` without a reasoner.** A query constraining `gndo:author` will miss triples that use `gndo:firstAuthor`, even though `firstAuthor` is a subproperty of `author`. Pattern A must explicitly cover both using `VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor }`. `gndo:poet` and `gndo:composer` should be added for coverage of MusicalWork records.
   168→
   169→### 0.4 Confirm literal format of `preferredNameForTheWork`
   170→
   171→Determines whether VALUES-based queries must include `@de` language-tagged variants.
   172→
   173→```bash
   174→curl -s -X POST "https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd" \
   175→  -H "Accept: application/sparql-results+json" \
   176→  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
   177→  --data-urlencode 'query=PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   178→SELECT ?l (lang(?l) AS ?tag) WHERE {
   179→  <https://d-nb.info/gnd/4427937-1> gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?l .
   180→}
   181→LIMIT 5'
   182→```
   183→
   184→**Result:**
   185→
   186→```json
   187→{
   188→  "l": { "type": "literal", "value": "Faust I / Walpurgisnacht / Berlin / Staatsbibliothek Berlin / Ms. germ. qu. 527" },
   189→  "tag": { "type": "literal", "value": "" }
   190→}
   191→```
   192→
   193→**Conclusions:**
   194→1. `preferredNameForTheWork` literals have **no language tag** — `@de` variants are not needed in VALUES clauses.
   195→2. GND preferred names are **not clean titles** — this Manuscript entry carries a full ISBD-style string including location and shelfmark, not just the work title. Pattern C's exact FILTER match will not resolve titles like "Faust" against such records; those can only be matched via `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` or through a separate normalized-title query.
   196→
   197→---
   198→
   199→## Step 1 — Query patterns
   200→
   201→| Query pattern | Fields | Justification |
   202→|---|---|---|
   203→| **A** — Title + author | `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` (text search); `gndo:author`, `gndo:firstAuthor`, `gndo:poet`, `gndo:composer` (constraint via VALUES) | Author URI narrows the candidate set before text matching; highest precision. Use when author GND URI is available and text index is confirmed (recon 0.2–0.3). |
   204→| **B** — Title only | `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` (preferred); `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` (UNION fallback) | No author URI available. UNION over variant names increases recall for cases where the DDB title matches a GND alternative form rather than the preferred label. Use when text index is confirmed but no author URI. |
   205→| **C** — FILTER fallback | `gndo:preferredNameForTheWork` (exact string match) | `contains-word` unavailable or not indexed for the predicate (determined by recon 0.2). Exact/normalized string comparison only; no relevance ranking. Lower recall than A/B. |
   206→
   207→### Pattern A — Title + author, text-indexed (primary)
   208→
   209→Used when: author GND URI is available AND `contains-word` is confirmed (recon 0.2).
   210→
   211→`gndo:firstAuthor` is a subproperty of `gndo:author` but SPARQL does not infer this without a reasoner — both must be listed explicitly. `gndo:poet` and `gndo:composer` cover musical and lyrical works.
   212→
   213→```sparql
   214→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   215→
   216→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel ?score WHERE {
   217→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
   218→  VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }
   219→  ?work a ?wtype .
   220→  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .
   221→  ?work ?authorPred <{author_gnd_uri}> .
   222→  ?prefLabel contains-word "{title_tokens}"
   223→  BIND(score(?prefLabel) AS ?score)
   224→}
   225→ORDER BY DESC(?score)
   226→TEXTLIMIT 5
   227→LIMIT 10
   228→```
   229→
   230→`TEXTLIMIT 5` caps QLever's internal text candidates for performance; `LIMIT 10` caps output rows.
   231→
   232→### Pattern B — Title only, text-indexed
   233→
   234→Used when: no author GND URI is available.
   235→UNION catches cases where the DDB title matches a GND variant name rather than the preferred form.
   236→
   237→```sparql
   238→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   239→
   240→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel ?varLabel ?score WHERE {
   241→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
   242→  ?work a ?wtype .
   243→  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .
   244→  {
   245→    ?prefLabel contains-word "{title_tokens}"
   246→    BIND(?prefLabel AS ?varLabel)
   247→    BIND(score(?prefLabel) AS ?score)
   248→  } UNION {
   249→    ?work gndo:variantNameForTheWork ?alt .
   250→    ?alt contains-word "{title_tokens}"
   251→    BIND(?alt AS ?varLabel)
   252→    BIND(score(?alt) AS ?score)
   253→  }
   254→}
   255→ORDER BY DESC(?score)
   256→TEXTLIMIT 5
   257→LIMIT 10
   258→```
   259→
   260→### Pattern C — FILTER (active query path)
   261→
   262→Exact-match only; normalized title (diacritics stripped, lowercased) maximizes recall.
   263→
   264→```sparql
   265→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   266→
   267→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel WHERE {
   268→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
   269→  ?work a ?wtype .
   270→  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .
   271→  FILTER(LCASE(STR(?prefLabel)) = "{normalized_title}")
   272→}
   273→LIMIT 10
   274→```
   275→
   276→### Conclusion from Step 0
   277→
   278→| Finding | Source | Impact on Step 1 |
   279→|---|---|---|
   280→| `contains-word` raises a SPARQL parse error | Step 0.2 | Patterns A and B are not available. **Pattern C is the sole active query path.** |
   281→| `VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }` covers 580,284 entities (242k + 331k + 7k) | Step 0.1 | Confirmed — use in all patterns |
   282→| Dominant author predicate is `gndo:author`; `gndo:firstAuthor` is a subproperty but SPARQL does not infer it | Step 0.3 | Pattern A must use `VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }` |
   283→| `gndo:poet` appears for lyrical/musical works (Goethe as lyricist) | Step 0.3 | Include `gndo:poet` in `VALUES ?authorPred` |
   284→| `CollectiveManuscript` has zero entities in this dataset | Step 0.1 | Confirmed exclusion; no impact on queries |
   285→
   286→Pattern C with author constraint (when author GND URI is available) becomes the effective Pattern A: same `VALUES ?authorPred` constraint, FILTER instead of `contains-word`.
   287→
   288→---
   289→
   290→## Step 2 — Title token preparation
   291→
   292→QLever `contains-word` is word-based, not phrase-based by default. Title preparation has three distinct phases: normalization, tokenization, and filtering.
   293→
   294→### Normalization (pre-tokenization)
   295→
   296→1. **Unicode NFC normalize** — Unicode Standard Annex #15, Normalization Form C: Canonical Decomposition followed by Canonical Composition. Ensures consistent code-point representation before any string operation. Example: `a` + combining diaeresis (two code points, NFD) → `ä` (one code point, NFC). Required so that lowercasing, diacritic stripping, and string comparison behave predictably regardless of source encoding.
   297→2. **Lowercase** — case-fold the NFC-normalized string.
   298→3. **Strip diacritics** — decompose (NFD) then remove combining characters (`unicodedata.category(c) == 'Mn'`). Maps `ä→a`, `ü→u`, `ö→o`, `ß→ss`. Note: test whether the GND endpoint requires this — if the text index is accent-insensitive, stripping may reduce rather than increase recall.
   299→
   300→### Tokenization
   301→
   302→4. **Split on whitespace and punctuation** — split the normalized string into individual word tokens.
   303→
   304→### Filtering
   305→
   306→5. **Remove stopwords:** `der, die, das, ein, eine, von, und, zu, im, in, an, auf, für, mit, bei, dem, den`
   307→6. **Select 2–3 distinctive tokens** (see below)
   308→
   309→### Distinctive token selection
   310→
   311→**Underlying principle:** `contains-word` with multiple tokens is AND semantics — each additional token makes the query more restrictive. Candidate retrieval should maximize recall (get the right GND Work somewhere in the top-10); precision is handled by post-retrieval string scoring in Step 3. The goal is the minimum set of tokens that identifies the work without over-constraining.
   312→
   313→**Criterion 1 — Semantic centrality.** Use tokens that are the essential content of the title, not modifiers or structural words:
   314→
   315→- **Keep:** nouns, proper nouns (place/person names embedded in titles), semantically unique adjectives
   316→- **Drop even if not in the stopword list:** articles, prepositions, ordinals (*erster, zweiter*), format/edition terms (*Band, Teil, Heft, Auflage, Bd., Nr.*)
   317→
   318→Example: *"Die Leiden des jungen Werthers"* → tokens `leiden werthers`; the proper noun alone (`werthers`) is sufficient with Pattern A.
   319→
   320→**Criterion 2 — Low collision frequency.** Prefer tokens that appear in few GND Works. Some words are not stopwords but are so common in bibliographic titles that they add almost no discriminating power:
   321→
   322→```python
   323→GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS = {
   324→    "briefe", "gedichte", "werke", "schriften", "geschichte",
   325→    "bericht", "katalog", "sammlung", "aufsätze", "texte",
   326→    "band", "teil", "heft", "nummer", "jahrgang",
   327→}
   328→```
   329→
   330→Example: *"Gedichte"* alone returns thousands of GND Works. *"faust"* alone is sufficient with an author URI.
   331→
   332→This list should be derived empirically from a frequency query against the endpoint, not hardcoded blindly — run after recon 0.1.
   333→
   334→**Script:** `scripts/check_generic_title_words.py` — queries the DNB endpoint per word using exact VALUE matching on capitalized German forms (plain literals, no `@de` tag — confirmed by recon 0.4). Run with `python scripts/check_generic_title_words.py`.
   335→
   336→**Results** (counts = GND Works whose full `preferredNameForTheWork` is exactly this word or common article+word variants; threshold ≥100 = drop):
   337→
   338→| Word | Count | Keep in list? |
   339→|---|---|---|
   340→| briefe | 74 | Yes (low collision) |
   341→| gedichte | 90 | Yes (low collision) |
   342→| werke | 3,212 | **No — drop from list** |
   343→| schriften | 1 | Yes (low collision) |
   344→| geschichte | 5 | Yes (low collision) |
   345→| bericht | 2 | Yes (low collision) |
   346→| katalog | 0 | Yes (low collision) |
   347→| sammlung | 0 | Yes (low collision) |
   348→| aufsätze | 0 | Yes (low collision) |
   349→| texte | 2 | Yes (low collision) |
   350→| band | 0 | Yes (low collision) |
   351→| teil | 1 | Yes (low collision) |
   352→| heft | 0 | Yes (low collision) |
   353→| nummer | 0 | Yes (low collision) |
   354→| jahrgang | 0 | Yes (low collision) |
   355→
   356→**Conclusion:** Only `werke` (3,212) exceeds the threshold and is genuinely high-collision as a standalone title. All other words are low-frequency as full titles — GND preferred names are typically qualified strings (ISBD-style, see recon 0.4), not bare single-word titles. `GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS` should be revised to contain only `werke`; the others do not need filtering for Pattern C exact-match queries.
   357→
   358→**Decision rule:**
   359→
   360→```python
   361→def select_tokens(tokens: list[str]) -> list[str]:
   362→    # tokens: already stopword-filtered, lowercased, diacritics-stripped
   363→    distinctive = [t for t in tokens if t not in GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS and len(t) > 3]
   364→    return distinctive[:3] if len(distinctive) >= 2 else tokens[:3]
   365→```
   366→
   367→`len(t) > 3` also drops short high-frequency fragments not caught by the stopword list.
   368→
   369→**Why 2–3 tokens specifically:**
   370→
   371→- 1 token: usually sufficient with Pattern A (author constraint already narrows heavily); sometimes sufficient for highly distinctive titles in Pattern B
   372→- 2–3 tokens: the sweet spot for Pattern B — enough to discriminate, low risk of AND-excluding the right Work due to GND/DDB title variation
   373→- >3 tokens: increasing risk of false negatives when GND's preferred form differs from DDB's (e.g., GND has *"Faust. Der Tragödie erster Teil"* but DDB has *"Faust: Erster Teil"* — four tokens from the DDB form likely miss the GND entry)
   374→
   375→**Multi-word handling:**
   376→- Short (1–3 tokens after filtering): pass all remaining tokens
   377→- Long titles: apply `select_tokens()` above
   378→- High-ambiguity titles (single generic word like *Briefe*, *Gedichte* with no distinctive co-tokens): require author constraint (Pattern A); skip title-only search (Pattern B) if no author URI is available
   379→
   380→---
   381→
   382→## Step 3 — Post-retrieval scoring
   383→
   384→QLever's `score()` is used only to rank candidates before string comparison. Match type is assigned in Python:
   385→
   386→| Condition | `match_type` | Triple predicate |
   387→|---|---|---|
   388→| `prefLabel == extracted_title` (case-insensitive) | `exact` | `skos:exactMatch` |
   389→| Normalized match (strip diacritics + punctuation) | `normalized` | `skos:exactMatch` |
   390→| Levenshtein distance ≤ 2 to `prefLabel` or any `variantNameForTheWork` | `fuzzy` | `skos:closeMatch` |
   391→| No candidate above threshold | `unresolved` | — |
   392→
   393→`match_confidence` = normalized edit similarity to `prefLabel` (not QLever score).
   394→
   395→This logic is unchanged from the original plan in `gnd-title-extraction.md`.
   396→
   397→### Predicate justification
   398→
   399→**`skos:exactMatch`** (exact and normalized matches) — asserts two concepts are "an exact match" and can be used interchangeably for all retrieval purposes, without the OWL identity entailments of `owl:sameAs` (Miles & Bechhofer, *SKOS Simple Knowledge Organization System Reference*, W3C Recommendation, 2009, §10). Semantically appropriate here: a DDB ProvidedCHO and a GND Werk URI are not the same individual (CHO = catalog record for a manifestation; Werk URI = authority record for an abstract work), so asserting OWL individual identity would be incorrect.
   400→
   401→**`skos:closeMatch`** (fuzzy matches) — links two concepts "sufficiently similar that they can be used interchangeably in some information retrieval applications" but stops short of asserting full equivalence (Miles & Bechhofer, 2009, §10).
   402→
   403→**Why not `owl:sameAs`:** Halpin et al. show that `owl:sameAs` is routinely misused in linked data to express approximate or cross-type identity, producing unwanted OWL entailments downstream. Their recommendation: reserve `owl:sameAs` for verified same-individual identity; use SKOS mapping properties for concept-level matching (Halpin, Hayes, McCusker, McGuinness & Thompson, *When owl:sameAs Isn't the Same: An Analysis of Identity in Linked Data*, ISWC 2010, DOI: 10.1007/978-3-642-17746-0_16).
   404→
   405→**Match type taxonomy** (exact / normalized / fuzzy) follows the standard candidate generation ladder in entity linking (Shen, Wang & Han, *Entity Linking with a Knowledge Base: Issues, Techniques, and Solutions*, IEEE TKDE 27(2), 2015, §3.1, DOI: 10.1109/TKDE.2014.2327028).
   406→
   407→**Levenshtein distance** (Levenshtein, *Binary Codes Capable of Correcting Deletions, Insertions, and Reversals*, Soviet Physics Doklady 10(8), 1966). The threshold of ≤ 2 is a common empirical choice; validate against the gold set planned in `gnd-title-extraction.md` §Paper §4 quality metrics.
   408→
   409→---
   410→
   411→## Step 4 — Changes to `link_gnd_works.py`
   412→
   413→Replace the lobid-gnd REST call (Steps 3–4 in `gnd-title-extraction.md`) with:
   414→
   415→```python
   416→import httpx
   417→import asyncio
   418→
   419→QLEVER_ENDPOINT = "https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd"
   420→CONCURRENCY = 10  # tune up after rate-limit testing
   421→
   422→_semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(CONCURRENCY)
   423→
   424→async def query_gnd_werk(
   425→    extracted_title: str,
   426→    author_gnd_uri: str | None
   427→) -> list[dict]:
   428→    tokens = tokenize_for_qlever(extracted_title)
   429→    sparql = (
   430→        QUERY_A.format(title_tokens=tokens, author_gnd_uri=author_gnd_uri)
   431→        if author_gnd_uri
   432→        else QUERY_B.format(title_tokens=tokens)
   433→    )
   434→    async with _semaphore:
   435→        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:
   436→            r = await client.post(
   437→                QLEVER_ENDPOINT,
   438→                data={"query": sparql},
   439→                headers={"Accept": "application/sparql-results+json"}
   440→            )
   441→    r.raise_for_status()
   442→    return r.json()["results"]["bindings"]
   443→```
   444→
   445→Pattern selection:
   446→- Pattern A if `author_gnd_uri` is set
   447→- Pattern B otherwise
   448→- Pattern C as fallback if recon 0.2 shows `contains-word` is unavailable
   449→
   450→---
   451→
   452→## Step 5 — What this replaces
   453→
   454→| Old plan | New plan |
   455→|---|---|
   456→| `GET /gnd/search?q=label:"{title}"&filter=type:Work` (lobid REST) | SPARQL POST to QLever endpoint |
   457→| Endpoint TBD / local infrastructure required | Public DNB endpoint, no setup |
   458→| No text relevance ranking | QLever `score()` ranks candidates |
   459→| `link_gnd_works.py` depended on lobid API availability | Depends on `sparql.dnb.de` uptime (DNB-operated) |
   460→| Title-only or title + author_uri via query param | Same semantics; `gndo:firstAuthor` predicate (verify in recon 0.3) |
   461→
   462→---
   463→
   464→## Open questions
   465→

gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .\n  ?work ?authorPred <https://d-nb.info/gnd/118540238> .\n  FILTER(LCASE(STR(?prefLabel)) = \"gotz von berlichingen\")\n}\nLIMIT 10'\n```\n\n**Interpretation:**\n\n| Query A result | Query B result | Conclusion | Impact on FR-05 |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Results returned | No results | Endpoint does **not** normalize Umlauts — FILTER is byte-exact | Strip diacritics from **query string only if GND stores stripped forms**; otherwise keep Umlauts; send both forms via UNION |\n| Results returned | Results returned | Endpoint normalizes Umlauts (accent-insensitive collation) | Stripping is safe; normalized form sufficient for FILTER |\n| No results | No results | Title not stored as `preferredNameForTheWork` in this form | ### User *(2026-03-23T09:12:44.977Z)*

     1→# GeMeA — GND Werk Linking via QLever DNB Endpoint
     2→
     3→Script: `scripts/link_gnd_works.py`
     4→Replaces: lobid-gnd REST API approach (previously planned but endpoint was TBD)
     5→
     6→`link_gnd_works.py` is an **offline ingest step**, not a runtime component. It runs once (or on re-ingest) during Phase 0 to enrich ProvidedCHO records with GND Werk URIs. Its output feeds mocho → QLever → the GeMeA API. The GND linking results are ultimately exposed through the API's `/work/{id}` endpoint and SPARQL proxy — `link_gnd_works.py` itself never serves live requests. Design constraints follow from this: throughput and batch efficiency matter; latency per query does not.
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Knowledge base index
    11→
    12→**Description:** DNB Bibliography and GND (Gemeinsame Normdatei) (`authorities-gnd_lds`, `dnb-all_lds` version 23.02.2026) — updated monthly
    13→
    14→| Metric | Value |
    15→|---|---|
    16→| Triples | 1,242,168,767 |
    17→| Subjects | 186,556,644 |
    18→| Predicates | 591 |
    19→| Objects | 457,761,162 |
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## Endpoint and HTTP API
    24→
    25→**Endpoint:** `https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd`
    26→**Method:** `POST`
    27→**Body:** `query=<URL-encoded SPARQL>` (`application/x-www-form-urlencoded`)
    28→**Accept:** `application/sparql-results+json`
    29→
    30→QLever instance settings:
    31→- Languages: `en`
    32→- Default max rows: 100
    33→- Default mode: 3 — SPARQL & context-sensitive entities
    34→
    35→QLever-specific keywords used: `contains-word`, `textlimit`, `score()`
    36→
    37→---
    38→
    39→## Step 0 — Recon queries (run once before implementation)
    40→
    41→Three things to verify before writing production queries.
    42→
    43→### 0.1 Work type coverage
    44→
    45→IFLA LRM defines a Work (LRM-E2) as "a distinct intellectual or artistic creation" — the abstract entity at the top of the WEMI hierarchy, independent of any particular realization or carrier. The GND ontology (`gnd_20251218.ttl`) models six subclasses of `gndo:Work`. Only three correspond to the IFLA LRM Work concept:
    46→
    47→| GND class | Label (en / de) | MARC21 | IFLA LRM | Include in queries |
    48→|---|---|---|---|---|
    49→| `gndo:Work` | Work / Werk | `079 $v=wit` | LRM-E2 Work — direct match | **Yes** |
    50→| `gndo:MusicalWork` | Musical work / Werk der Musik | `079 $v=wim` | LRM-E2 Work — a musical composition as distinct creative entity | **Yes** |



```

### User *(2026-03-23T09:12:48.645Z)*

**Tool result**
```
   380→---
   381→
   382→## Step 3 — Post-retrieval scoring
   383→
   384→QLever's `score()` is used only to rank candidates before string comparison. Match type is assigned in Python:
   385→
   386→| Condition | `match_type` | Triple predicate |
   387→|---|---|---|
   388→| `prefLabel == extracted_title` (case-insensitive) | `exact` | `skos:exactMatch` |
   389→| Normalized match (strip diacritics + punctuation) | `normalized` | `skos:exactMatch` |
   390→| Levenshtein distance ≤ 2 to `prefLabel` or any `variantNameForTheWork` | `fuzzy` | `skos:closeMatch` |
   391→| No candidate above threshold | `unresolved` | — |
   392→
   393→`match_confidence` = normalized edit similarity to `prefLabel` (not QLever score).
   394→
   395→This logic is unchanged from the original plan in `gnd-title-extraction.md`.
   396→
   397→### Predicate justification
   398→
   399→**`skos:exactMatch`** (exact and normalized matches) — asserts two concepts are "an exact match" and can be used interchangeably for all retrieval purposes, without the OWL identity entailments of `owl:sameAs` (Miles & Bechhofer, *SKOS Simple Knowledge Organization System Reference*, W3C Recommendation, 2009, §10). Semantically appropriate here: a DDB ProvidedCHO and a GND Werk URI are not the same individual (CHO = catalog record for a manifestation; Werk URI = authority record for an abstract work), so asserting OWL individual identity would be incorrect.
   400→
   401→**`skos:closeMatch`** (fuzzy matches) — links two concepts "sufficiently similar that they can be used interchangeably in some information retrieval applications" but stops short of asserting full equivalence (Miles & Bechhofer, 2009, §10).
   402→
   403→**Why not `owl:sameAs`:** Halpin et al. show that `owl:sameAs` is routinely misused in linked data to express approximate or cross-type identity, producing unwanted OWL entailments downstream. Their recommendation: reserve `owl:sameAs` for verified same-individual identity; use SKOS mapping properties for concept-level matching (Halpin, Hayes, McCusker, McGuinness & Thompson, *When owl:sameAs Isn't the Same: An Analysis of Identity in Linked Data*, ISWC 2010, DOI: 10.1007/978-3-642-17746-0_16).
   404→
   405→**Match type taxonomy** (exact / normalized / fuzzy) follows the standard candidate generation ladder in entity linking (Shen, Wang & Han, *Entity Linking with a Knowledge Base: Issues, Techniques, and Solutions*, IEEE TKDE 27(2), 2015, §3.1, DOI: 10.1109/TKDE.2014.2327028).
   406→
   407→**Levenshtein distance** (Levenshtein, *Binary Codes Capable of Correcting Deletions, Insertions, and Reversals*, Soviet Physics Doklady 10(8), 1966). The threshold of ≤ 2 is a common empirical choice; validate against the gold set planned in `gnd-title-extraction.md` §Paper §4 quality metrics.
   408→
   409→---
   410→
   411→## Step 4 — Changes to `link_gnd_works.py`
   412→
   413→Replace the lobid-gnd REST call (Steps 3–4 in `gnd-title-extraction.md`) with:
   414→
   415→```python
   416→import httpx
   417→import asyncio
   418→
   419→QLEVER_ENDPOINT = "https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd"
   420→CONCURRENCY = 10  # tune up after rate-limit testing
   421→
   422→_semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(CONCURRENCY)
   423→
   424→async def query_gnd_werk(
   425→    extracted_title: str,
   426→    author_gnd_uri: str | None
   427→) -> list[dict]:
   428→    tokens = tokenize_for_qlever(extracted_title)
   429→    sparql = (
   430→        QUERY_A.format(title_tokens=tokens, author_gnd_uri=author_gnd_uri)
   431→        if author_gnd_uri
   432→        else QUERY_B.format(title_tokens=tokens)
   433→    )
   434→    async with _semaphore:
   435→        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:
   436→            r = await client.post(
   437→                QLEVER_ENDPOINT,
   438→                data={"query": sparql},
   439→                headers={"Accept": "application/sparql-results+json"}
   440→            )
   441→    r.raise_for_status()
   442→    return r.json()["results"]["bindings"]
   443→```
   444→
   445→Pattern selection:
   446→- Pattern A if `author_gnd_uri` is set
   447→- Pattern B otherwise
   448→- Pattern C as fallback if recon 0.2 shows `contains-word` is unavailable
   449→
   450→---
   451→
   452→## Step 5 — What this replaces
   453→
   454→| Old plan | New plan |
   455→|---|---|
   456→| `GET /gnd/search?q=label:"{title}"&filter=type:Work` (lobid REST) | SPARQL POST to QLever endpoint |
   457→| Endpoint TBD / local infrastructure required | Public DNB endpoint, no setup |
   458→| No text relevance ranking | QLever `score()` ranks candidates |
   459→| `link_gnd_works.py` depended on lobid API availability | Depends on `sparql.dnb.de` uptime (DNB-operated) |
   460→| Title-only or title + author_uri via query param | Same semantics; `gndo:firstAuthor` predicate (verify in recon 0.3) |
   461→
   462→---
   463→
   464→## Open questions
   465→
   466→| ID | Question | Blocking | Status |
   467→|---|---|---|---|
   468→| ~~OQ-00a~~ | ~~Is `preferredNameForTheWork` in the text index?~~ | — | **Resolved** — Recon 0.2: `contains-word` causes SPARQL parse error. Endpoint enforces SPARQL 1.1 only. Pattern C is the sole active query path. |
   469→| ~~OQ-00b~~ | ~~Exact predicate for author link (`gndo:firstAuthor` or `gndo:creator`)?~~ | — | **Resolved** — Recon 0.3: `gndo:author` dominant (8/10); `gndo:firstAuthor` added via `VALUES` (subproperty, SPARQL won't infer); `gndo:poet` for lyrical works; `gndo:composer` for musical works. |
   470→| OQ-01 | Does `sparql.dnb.de` normalize Umlauts in FILTER comparisons? (`ä` vs `a`) | No | Open — see recon 0.5 below |
   471→| OQ-02 | What are the actual rate limits on `sparql.dnb.de`? | No | Open — no published SLA; profile at `CONCURRENCY = 10` during first batch run |
   472→| OQ-03 | Should `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` be added as a UNION branch in Pattern C to increase recall? | No | Open — preferred names are often ISBD-qualified strings (recon 0.4); variant names may carry the clean title form |
   473→| OQ-04 | Does `gndo:composer`/`gndo:firstComposer` behave analogously to `gndo:author`/`gndo:firstAuthor` for MusicalWork records? | No | Open — verify with a musician query analogous to recon 0.3 |
   474→
   475→### Recon 0.5 — Umlaut handling in FILTER comparisons (OQ-01)
   476→
   477→The question is whether `LCASE(STR(?prefLabel)) = "gotz von berlichingen"` (diacritics stripped) matches a record stored as `"Götz von Berlichingen"`. LCASE performs case folding only; it does not decompose or strip combining characters. The hypothesis is that the endpoint does **not** normalize Umlauts, meaning a stripped form will not match an umlaut-bearing literal — and therefore diacritic stripping in FR-05 would *hurt* recall against `preferredNameForTheWork`.
   478→
   479→Test case: Goethe's *Götz von Berlichingen* (`https://d-nb.info/gnd/118540238` as author) — a well-known work with `ö` in the title.
   480→
   481→**Query A — with umlaut (expect: results)**
   482→
   483→```sparql
   484→PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>
   485→SELECT ?work ?prefLabel WHERE {
   486→  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }
   487→  VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }
   488→  ?work a ?wtype .

   492→}
   493→LIMIT 10
   494→```

  "content": "# GeMeA — GND Werk Linking via QLever DNB Endpoint\n\nScript: `scripts/link_gnd_works.py`\nReplaces: lobid-gnd REST API approach (previously planned but endpoint was TBD)\n\n`link_gnd_works.py` is an **offline ingest step**, not a runtime component. It runs once (or on re-ingest) during Phase 0 to enrich ProvidedCHO records with GND Werk URIs. Its output feeds mocho → QLever → the GeMeA API. The GND linking results are ultimately exposed through the API's `/work/{id}` endpoint and SPARQL proxy — `link_gnd_works.py` itself never serves live requests. Design constraints follow from this: throughput and batch efficiency matter; latency per query does not.\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| Section | What it covers |\n|---|---|\n| [Knowledge base index](#knowledge-base-index) | DNB endpoint dataset stats (triples, subjects, predicates) |\n| [Endpoint and HTTP API](#endpoint-and-http-api) | `https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd` — POST format, QLever settings |\n| [Step 0 — Recon queries](#step-0--recon-queries-run-once-before-implementation) | Four one-off queries to validate Work classes, text index, author predicates, literal format before implementation |\n| [Step 1 — Query patterns](#step-1--query-patterns) | Patterns A/B/C with justification; conclusion: Pattern C (FILTER) is the sole active path |\n| [Step 2 — Title token preparation](#step-2--title-token-preparation) | Normalization → tokenization → stopword removal → distinctive token selection; `GENERIC_TITLE_WORDS` validation |\n| [Step 3 — Post-retrieval scoring](#step-3--post-retrieval-scoring) | Match type assignment (exact / normalized / fuzzy); `skos:exactMatch` vs `skos:closeMatch` vs `owl:sameAs` |\n| [Step 4 — Changes to `link_gnd_works.py`](#step-4--changes-to-link_gnd_workspy) | Replace lobid REST with async `httpx` POST; concurrency semaphore |\n| [Step 5 — What this replaces](#step-5--what-this-replaces) | Old lobid plan vs new QLever plan |\n\n---\n\n## Open questions\n\n| ID | Question | Blocking | Status |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| ~~OQ-00a~~ | ~~Is `preferredNameForTheWork` in the text index?~~ | — | **Resolved** — Recon 0.2: `contains-word` causes SPARQL parse error. Pattern C is the sole active query path. |\n| ~~OQ-00b~~ | ~~Exact predicate for author link (`gndo:firstAuthor` or `gndo:creator`)?~~ | — | **Resolved** — Recon 0.3: `gndo:author` dominant (8/10); `gndo:firstAuthor` added via `VALUES` (subproperty, SPARQL won't infer); `gndo:poet` for lyrical works; `gndo:composer` for musical works. |\n| OQ-01 | Does `sparql.dnb.de` normalize Umlauts in FILTER comparisons? (`ä` vs `a`) | No | Open — see [recon 0.5](#recon-05--umlaut-handling-in-filter-comparisons-oq-01) |\n| OQ-02 | What are the actual rate limits on `sparql.dnb.de`? | No | Open — no published SLA; profile at `CONCURRENCY = 10` during first batch run |\n| OQ-03 | Should `gndo:variantNameForTheWork` be added as a UNION branch in Pattern C to increase recall? | No | Open — preferred names are often ISBD-qualified strings (recon 0.4); variant names may carry the clean title form |\n| OQ-04 | Does `gndo:composer`/`gndo:firstComposer` behave analogously to `gndo:author`/`gndo:firstAuthor` for MusicalWork records? | No | Open — verify with a musician query analogous to recon 0.3 |\n\n### Recon 0.5 — Umlaut handling in FILTER comparisons (OQ-01)\n\nThe question is whether `LCASE(STR(?prefLabel)) = \"gotz von berlichingen\"` (diacritics stripped) matches a record stored as `\"Götz von Berlichingen\"`. LCASE performs case folding only; it does not decompose or strip combining characters. The hypothesis is that the endpoint does **not** normalize Umlauts, meaning a stripped form will not match an umlaut-bearing literal — and therefore diacritic stripping in FR-05 would *hurt* recall against `preferredNameForTheWork`.\n\nTest case: Goethe's *Götz von Berlichingen* (`https://d-nb.info/gnd/118540238` as author) — a well-known work with `ö` in the title.\n\n**Query A — with umlaut (expect: results)**\n\n```sparql\nPREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>\nSELECT ?work ?prefLabel WHERE {\n  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }\n  VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }\n  ?work a ?wtype .\n  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .\n  ?work ?authorPred <https://d-nb.info/gnd/118540238> .\n  FILTER(LCASE(STR(?prefLabel)) = \"götz von berlichingen\")\n}\nLIMIT 10\n```\n\n```bash\ncurl -s -X POST \"https://sparql.dnb.de/api/dnbgnd\" \\\n  -H \"Accept: application/sparql-results+json\" \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded\" \\\n  --data-urlencode 'query=PREFIX gndo: <https://d-nb.info/standards/elementset/gnd#>\nSELECT ?work ?prefLabel WHERE {\n  VALUES ?wtype { gndo:Work gndo:MusicalWork gndo:Manuscript }\n  VALUES ?authorPred { gndo:author gndo:firstAuthor gndo:poet gndo:composer }\n  ?work a ?wtype .\n  ?work gndo:preferredNameForTheWork ?prefLabel .\n
