### User *(2026-03-23T20:35:59.973Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→# GeMeA — ISBD Field Rating: Spec and Methodology
     2→
     3→**Script:** `scripts/rate_isbd_fields.py`
     4→**Output:** `data/processed/isbd_field_ratings.csv`, `data/processed/isbd_examples.csv`
     5→**Phase:** 0a — NER Training Data (see [roadmap.md](roadmap.md))
     6→**ADR:** [isbd-field-rating-adr.md](isbd-field-rating-adr.md)
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Objective
    11→
    12→Map the presence of bibliographic fields in each title string across all 4.47M records in `DF_DE_TITLES`. The field presence flags are used to:
    13→
    14→1. **Select silver candidates** for NER training — records where ISBD structure clearly identifies multiple labelable spans
    15→2. **Stratify** the NER evaluation gold set by field combination type
    16→3. **Understand corpus coverage** — which fields are detectable from the title string alone vs. requiring other columns (`dc_publisher`, `dc_creator`, etc.)
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## ISBD Area Structure
    21→
    22→ISBD organises a catalog record into areas separated by ` . - ` (space · period · space · hyphen · space). Within DF_DE_TITLES, the `title` column contains the full ISBD string as catalogued, not just the proper title. A fully structured record looks like:
    23→
    24→```
    25→Title proper : other title information / statement of responsibility
    26→  . - Edition statement
    27→  . - Place of publication : Publisher, Year
    28→  . - (Series title ; volume number)
    29→```
    30→
    31→**Examples from the corpus:**
    32→
    33→```
    34→Die höchstnöthige Sterbe-Schule : Bey Christlicher Beerdigung / Christian Gerber
    35→```
    36→→ Title area only; no `. -`; has ` :` (other title) and ` /` (SoR[^sor])
    37→([UBMFYC2EUGMB3Q5FK65JZMRFMVJXA3E4](https://ddb.de/item/UBMFYC2EUGMB3Q5FK65JZMRFMVJXA3E4))
    38→
    39→```
    40→Erwin : vier Gespräche über das Schöne und die Kunst / von K. W. F. Solger. - Berlin : Realschulbuchhandlung, 1815
    41→```
    42→→ Full structural record; area separator present; PLACE, PUBLISHER, YEAR all detectable
    43→([VQOVBGV47PA25EXTCPCNQ25V736DXDUM](https://ddb.de/item/VQOVBGV47PA25EXTCPCNQ25V736DXDUM))
    44→
    45→```
    46→Beitrag zur Infusorienkunde oder Naturbeschreibung der Zerkarien und Bazillarien : Mit 6 illuminierten Kupfertafeln / von D. Christian Ludwig Nitzsch, Professor zu Halle. - Halle : Hendel, 1817. - (Neue Schriften der Naturforschenden Gesellschaft zu Halle ; 3,1)
    47→```
    48→→ Title, SoR, publication area, series area
    49→([CO5UVCKY6XI5WDLXPZTZ6NDVXL4V2THV](https://ddb.de/item/CO5UVCKY6XI5WDLXPZTZ6NDVXL4V2THV))
    50→
    51→The `. - ` separator is the primary structural signal. Records **without** it are catalogued with incomplete ISBD punctuation — the title string may still contain useful signals (` :`, ` /`, year) but field boundary precision is lower.
    52→
    53→---
    54→
    55→[^sor]: **SoR — Statement of Responsibility** (Verantwortlichkeitsangabe). The ISBD term for the portion of a catalog record that names those responsible for the intellectual content: authors, editors, translators, illustrators, etc. In MARC 21 it is encoded in 245 $c, introduced by ` / ` (trailing mark on 245 $b). Multiple contributors are separated by ` ; `. Example: `Titel / Autor ; Herausgeber ; Übersetzer`. In the rating script, ` /` is the primary cue for detecting `PERSON` (and `TRANSLATOR`) spans. Sources: ISBD consolidated edition (2011), Area 1, §1.4 <https://www.ifla.org/files/assets/cataloguing/isbd/isbd-cons_20110321.pdf>; ThULB Jena, ISBD-Interpunktion <https://koha-wiki.thulb.uni-jena.de/erschliessung/katalogisierung/handbuecher/isbd-interpunktion/>
    56→
    57→---
    58→
    59→## Detection Rules
    60→
    61→Source: ThULB Jena, *ISBD-Interpunktion* (Koha-Wiki, CC BY-SA 4.0). <https://koha-wiki.thulb.uni-jena.de/erschliessung/katalogisierung/handbuecher/isbd-interpunktion/>
    62→
    63→ISBD punctuation marks are **trailing** — each mark appears at the end of the preceding MARC subfield, immediately before the next subfield's content. In a flat ISBD string (as stored in `title`), the mark therefore precedes the field it introduces. The table below maps each NER label to its ISBD punctuation signal, MARC source field, and detection pattern.
    64→
    65→### Structural tier (has `. - `)
    66→
    67→Split on `. - ` (the area separator formed by the trailing `.` of one MARC field group and the ` - ` leader of the next). Parse `area[0]` as the title area; `area[1:]` as subsequent areas.
    68→
    69→**Group 1 — Title and Statement of Responsibility (MARC 245)**
    70→
    71→| Field | MARC subfield | Trailing mark | Signal in flat string | Detection pattern |
    72→|---|---|---|---|---|
    73→| `TITLE` | 245 $a | ` :` (if $b follows) or ` /` (if $c follows) | Always present as leading content | String before first ` :` or ` /` |
    74→| `OTHER_TITLE` | 245 $b | ` /` (if $c follows) | ` :` precedes the subtitle | ` :` in area[0] |
    75→| `PERSON` (SoR[^sor]) | 245 $c | `.` (area close) | ` /` precedes the SoR | ` /` in area[0] |
    76→| `PARALLEL_TITLE` | 246 / 245 | ` =` | ` =` precedes parallel title | ` =` in area[0] |
    77→| `VOLUME` (part number in title) | 245 $n | `,` | `Bd. N,` or `Teil N,` | `\b(Bd\|Teil\|Vol\|Nr)\.\s*\d+` |
    78→
    79→Note: ` :` appears in **both** Group 1 (before subtitle) and Group 4 (before publisher). The area separator `. - ` is used to distinguish them — ` :` before the first `. - ` belongs to the title area; ` :` after it belongs to the publication area.
    80→
    81→**Group 2 — Edition (MARC 250)**
    82→
    83→| Field | MARC subfield | Trailing mark | Detection pattern |
    84→|---|---|---|---|
    85→| `EDITION` | 250 $a | `.` (area close) | Edition keyword regex: `Aufl`, `Ausg`, `ed.`, `überarb`, `erw`, `verb`, `Neuausg` |
    86→
    87→**Group 4 — Publication Area (MARC 264)**
    88→
    89→| Field | MARC subfield | Trailing mark | Detection pattern |
    90→|---|---|---|---|
    91→| `PLACE` | 264 $a | ` :` | Left of ` : ` in area[1+] |
    92→| `PUBLISHER` | 264 $b | `,` | Right of ` : ` up to `,` in area[1+] |
    93→| `YEAR` | 264 $c | `.` (area close) | `\b(1[4-9]\d{2}\|20[012]\d)\b` in area[1+] |
    94→
    95→**Group 6 — Series (MARC 490)**
    96→
    97→| Field | MARC subfield | Trailing mark | Detection pattern |
    98→|---|---|---|---|
    99→| `SERIES` | 490 $a | ` ;` | `\([^)]+;\s*[^)]*\d[^)]*\)` in area[1+] |
   100→| `VOLUME` (series numbering) | 490 $v | `.` | Digit after ` ;` inside parenthetical |
   101→
   102→### Heuristic tier (no `. - `)
   103→
   104→Apply whole-string patterns. Reduced precision for PLACE/PUBLISHER (only `Verlag` keyword → PUBLISHER; PLACE not flagged without area structure).
   105→
   106→| Field | Pattern | Precision note |
   107→|---|---|---|
   108→| `OTHER_TITLE` | ` :` anywhere | May fire on non-ISBD colons |
   109→| `PERSON` | ` /` anywhere | Generally reliable |
   110→| `PARALLEL_TITLE` | ` =` anywhere | Generally reliable |
   111→| `EDITION` | Edition keyword regex | Generally reliable |
   112→| `YEAR` | 4-digit year regex | May fire on non-publication years in text |
   113→| `PUBLISHER` | `\bVerlag\b` or `\bPress\b` | Low recall; misses most publishers |
   114→| `PLACE` | Not detected | Requires `. -` area structure |
   115→| `SERIES` | `\([^)]+;\s*[^)]*\d[^)]*\)` | Generally reliable |
   116→| `VOLUME` | Volume keyword regex | Generally reliable |
   117→
   118→---
   119→
   120→## Output Schema
   121→
   122→`data/processed/isbd_field_ratings.csv` — one row per record.
   123→
   124→| Column | Type | Description |
   125→|---|---|---|
   126→| `obj_id` | str | DDB object ID — link: `https://ddb.de/item/<obj_id>` |
   127→| `title` | str | Original title string |
   128→| `has_dot_dash` | bool | `. -` area separator present |
   129→| `f_title` | int | Always 1 |
   130→| `f_other_title` | int | Subtitle / other title info detected |
   131→| `f_person` | int | Statement of responsibility detected |
   132→| `f_parallel` | int | Parallel title detected |
   133→| `f_edition` | int | Edition statement detected |
   134→| `f_place` | int | Place of publication detected |
   135→| `f_publisher` | int | Publisher detected |
   136→| `f_year` | int | Publication year detected |
   137→| `f_series` | int | Series block detected |
   138→| `f_volume` | int | Volume / part number detected |
   139→| `n_fields` | int | Sum of all f_* flags |
   140→| `silver_tier` | int | 0 / 1 / 2 — see below |
   141→
   142→`data/processed/isbd_examples.csv` (generated with `--examples N`) — N records per ISBD pattern with `ddb_url`.
   143→
   144→---
   145→
   146→## Silver Tier Assignment
   147→
   148→| Tier | Criteria | Intended use in NER training |
   149→|---|---|---|
   150→| **2** | `has_dot_dash AND f_person AND any(f_edition, f_place, f_publisher, f_year, f_series)` | Primary training set — structural annotation enables multi-field span labeling with high confidence |
   151→| **1** | `n_fields ≥ 3` OR `(f_person AND f_year)` | Augmentation set — partial annotation; Work + Expression level |
   152→| **0** | All others | Not selected as silver candidates |
   153→
   154→Tier 2 is the primary source for silver labels. Tier 1 is used for augmentation after tier-2 evaluation.
   155→
   156→---
   157→
   158→## Actual Coverage
   159→
   160→Results from `rate_isbd_fields.py` on `DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl` (4,477,780 records), 2026-03-21.
   161→
   162→| Field | Count | % | Notes |
   163→|---|---|---|---|
   164→| `has_dot_dash` | 53,785 | **1.2%** | ⚠ Much lower than expected — see below |
   165→| `f_title` | 4,477,780 | 100.0% | Always present |
   166→| `f_other_title` | 905,344 | **20.2%** | Consistent with prior analysis (909k) |
   167→| `f_person` | 33,739 | **0.8%** | Consistent with prior analysis (34k) |
   168→| `f_person_compound` | 1,040 | 0.0% | Compound SoR (`/ ... ;`) — very sparse |
   169→| `f_parallel` | 26,084 | **0.6%** | Consistent with prior analysis (26k) |
   170→| `f_edition` | 158,991 | **3.6%** | Lower end of expected range |
   171→| `f_place` | 6,241 | **0.1%** | Structural-only; subset of has_dot_dash |
   172→| `f_publisher` | 8,229 | **0.2%** | Includes `Verlag` keyword hits |
   173→| `f_year` | 652,082 | **14.6%** | Below expected 20–30%; see below |
   174→| `f_series` | 620 | 0.0% | Structural-only; very sparse |
   175→| `f_volume` | 83,772 | **1.9%** | |
   176→
   177→**Silver tiers:**
   178→
   179→| Tier | Count | % |
   180→|---|---|---|
   181→| Tier 2 (structural, multi-field) | 4,613 | 0.1% |
   182→| Tier 1 (heuristic, partial) | 335,524 | 7.5% |
   183→| Tier 0 (not selected) | 4,137,643 | 92.4% |
   184→
   185→### Key findings
   186→
   187→**`has_dot_dash` at 1.2% vs. expected ~28%.** The area separator `. - ` (with spaces on both sides) matches only 53k records. The prior `check_isbd_titles.py` analysis counted 28% as having *any* ISBD marker, not specifically the `. - ` area separator. Most DDB records are catalogued with title-area punctuation (` :`, ` /`) but without full area separation — the `. - ` separator is rare in this dataset. This means structural-tier detection is far more limited than assumed; the heuristic tier carries nearly all the load.
   188→
   189→**`f_year` at 14.6% vs. expected 20–30%.** The year regex `\b(?:1[4-9]\d{2}|20[012]\d)\b` covers 1400–2029. Lower coverage than expected likely reflects that many DDB records store the date in `dc:date` / `dates` column rather than the title string.
   190→
   191→**Tier 2 at 0.1% (4,613 records).** Directly consequent on `has_dot_dash` being 1.2% — tier 2 requires the area separator. Still a usable silver set for fine-tuning; 4,600 structurally annotated records is within normal range for bibliographic NER.
   192→
   193→| # | obj_id | Title (truncated) | Detected fields |
   194→|---|---|---|---|
   195→| 1 | [KPVZSSDOTJWL7PGKA5YX6OOW57MQ4PYR](https://ddb.de/item/KPVZSSDOTJWL7PGKA5YX6OOW57MQ4PYR) | Naturgeschichte und Abbildungen der Säugethiere : Nach den neuesten Systemen … / vo… | PERSON, OTHER_TITLE, YEAR, PLACE, PUBLISHER, EDITION |
   196→| 2 | [I57Y5U5NVXENFX57QU66VNLMZCL3VDXD](https://ddb.de/item/I57Y5U5NVXENFX57QU66VNLMZCL3VDXD) | Pieske, Christa, Bilder für jedermann : Wandbilddrucke 1840–1940 ; [Museum für Dt. Volkskunde…] | PERSON, OTHER_TITLE, PLACE, PUBLISHER, VOLUME |
   197→| 3 | [VQOVBGV47PA25EXTCPCNQ25V736DXDUM](https://ddb.de/item/VQOVBGV47PA25EXTCPCNQ25V736DXDUM) | Erwin : vier Gespräche über das Schöne und die Kunst / von K. W. F. Solger. - Berlin : Realschulbuchhandlung, 1815 | PERSON, OTHER_TITLE, YEAR, PLACE, PUBLISHER |
   198→
   199→**Tier 1 at 7.5% (335k records).** The practical silver candidate pool. Heuristic false positive rate unknown — see `scripts/validate_heuristic_fields.py`.
   200→
   201→| # | obj_id | Title (truncated) | Detected fields |
   202→|---|---|---|---|
   203→| 1 | [UBMFYC2EUGMB3Q5FK65JZMRFMVJXA3E4](https://ddb.de/item/UBMFYC2EUGMB3Q5FK65JZMRFMVJXA3E4) | Die höchstnöthige Sterbe-Schule : Bey Christlicher Beerdigung Der … Fr. Marien Elisabeth von Schönberg… | OTHER_TITLE, YEAR |
   204→| 2 | [PBKMFCEQ2CM622H4SXHBIA5KYYJUQO6U](https://ddb.de/item/PBKMFCEQ2CM622H4SXHBIA5KYYJUQO6U) | Assaphs Und einer jglichen gleubigen Seele Fewrige Liebe gegen Gott … : Bey … Leichbegängnüß … 16. May Anno 1647 | OTHER_TITLE, YEAR |
   205→| 3 | [K74VAL7PLJW2K7J5HPJURCYGPUBK6BUV](https://ddb.de/item/K74VAL7PLJW2K7J5HPJURCYGPUBK6BUV) | Warhafftiger Abtruck gleichlautender Copey Kaysers Rudolffi des Anderen Käys. Mandati … | OTHER_TITLE, YEAR |
   206→
   207→**Tier 0 at 92.4% (4.1M records).** No or insufficient ISBD signals — not selected as silver candidates.
   208→
   209→| # | obj_id | Title | Detected fields |
   210→|---|---|---|---|
   211→| 1 | [5CPBHIPP5PFCDYGZWNJA2OLCZBXDZDXZ](https://ddb.de/item/5CPBHIPP5PFCDYGZWNJA2OLCZBXDZDXZ) | Sitzung 24, 2. Juli 1931 | YEAR only |
   212→| 2 | [A5XNH66CR2PGPDT6D4MBWZV2LKARBZX5](https://ddb.de/item/A5XNH66CR2PGPDT6D4MBWZV2LKARBZX5) | Abbildung und Beschreibung allerhand Menschen | none |
   213→| 3 | [SPWM52CIKUEGY2OVBAUNBXIBWGGGV7OX](https://ddb.de/item/SPWM52CIKUEGY2OVBAUNBXIBWGGGV7OX) | Elegia De Obsidione Magdeburgensi, Klag-Reimen/ Von Beläg- und Eroberung der weitberühmbten Uhralten Stadt Magdeburg | none |
   214→
   215→---
   216→
   217→## Limitations
   218→
   219→- **PLACE** is only detected in the structural tier. Most non-ISBD records will have `f_place = 0` even if place information exists elsewhere (e.g., `dc_publisher` contains "Dresden : Riedel").
   220→- **PUBLISHER** heuristic recall is low — "Verlag" keyword misses many publishers. The `dc_publisher` column could supplement detection but is kept out of scope for field ratings (title-string-only).
   221→
   222→### Silver dataset improvement: auxiliary-guided span lookup
   223→
   224→`rate_isbd_fields.py` intentionally rates only the `title` column (ADR-03). However, for **silver span extraction only**, PLACE and PUBLISHER coverage can be improved by a post-step that uses auxiliary columns as a lookup to find matching text *inside* the title string:
   225→
   226→- Take `dc_publisher` (e.g., `"Leipzig : Insel-Verlag"`) and search for it as a substring in `title`
   227→- If found, the span boundaries come from the title string — inference-consistent
   228→- Same for person names from `dc_creator` / `dc_contributor` vs. `PERSON` spans, and series strings from `agents`
   229→
   230→This approach differs from blind auxiliary-column labeling (ruled out in ADR-03) because the label is only applied when the value actually appears in the title. It is a downstream enrichment step for the silver dataset, not a change to field ratings.
   231→
   232→**Script:** `scripts/build_silver_spans.py` — described in Phase 0a of the roadmap. Inputs: `isbd_field_ratings.csv` + `DF_DE_TITLES` (with auxiliary columns). Output: `data/processed/silver_spans.jsonl` with span-level NER labels per silver-tier record.
   233→- **` ;` ambiguity** — ` ;` in the title area signals a subsequent statement of responsibility (compound SoR); in a series area it separates title from volume number. Mitigated by two complementary patterns: (1) SERIES requires `\([^)]+;\s*[^)]*\d[^)]*\)` — the ` ;` must be inside parentheses with a digit, so a bare ` ;` in the title area will never fire it; (2) `f_person_compound` detects compound SoR via ` /[^(]+;` — ` /` followed by content then ` ;` outside parentheses, distinguishing `Titel / Autor A ; Autor B` (SoR) from `(Series ; Bd. 3)` (series).
   234→- **Multi-volume records** — some records have multiple `. -` separators for volume entries; the script takes only the first split, potentially missing volume-level fields in entries 3+.
   235→- **False positives in heuristic tier** — ` :` fires on non-ISBD colons (URLs, ratios); ` /` fires on fractions or paths. Silver tier 1 should be validated on a ~200-record sample before use.
   236→



```

```
     1→# ISBD Punctuation Analysis — DF_DE_TITLES
     2→
     3→**Dataset:** `data/DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl` — 4,477,780 rows × 27 columns
     4→- Source: German titles from "content" objects.
     5→
     6→**Script:** `scripts/check_isbd_titles.py`
     7→**Date:** 2026-03-17
     8→
     9→## DataFrame Schema
    10→
    11→| Column | Description |
    12→|---|---|
    13→| `obj_id` | DDB object identifier (use: `https://ddb.de/item/<obj_id>`) |
    14→| `lang` | Language code (e.g. `de`) |
    15→| `title` | Title string — primary field for ISBD analysis |
    16→| `title_en` | English title (if available) |
    17→| `valid_htype` | Boolean — record has a valid hierarchical type |
    18→| `agent_uris` | Boolean — at least one agent URI present |
    19→| `agent_literals` | Boolean — at least one agent literal present |
    20→| `agent_both` | Boolean — both URI and literal agent present |
    21→| `date` | Boolean — date field present |
    22→| `both` | Boolean — both agent and date present |
    23→| `blank` | Boolean — blank record |
    24→| `all_tokens` | Token count (all) |
    25→| `content_tokens` | Token count (content tokens only) |
    26→| `dc_type` | DC type string(s), pipe-separated (e.g. `Leichenpredigt\|Monografie`) |
    27→| `agents` | Agent string(s), pipe-separated (names + publisher block) |
    28→| `ner_person` | NER-extracted person name |
    29→| `agent_match` | Agent match score |
    30→| `dates` | Extracted date string(s) |
    31→| `ner_date` | NER-extracted date |
    32→| `agent_approx_match` | Approximate agent match score |
    33→| `date_match` | Date match score |
    34→| `dc_subject` | DC subject field |
    35→| `dc_creator` | DC creator field |
    36→| `dc_contributor` | DC contributor field(s), pipe-separated |
    37→| `dc_publisher` | DC publisher field |
    38→| `model2_q1_answer` | Model 2 Q1 answer |
    39→| `model2_q1_match` | Model 2 Q1 match flag |
    40→
    41→## Summary
    42→
    43→| Metric | Count | % |
    44→|---|---|---|
    45→| Rows total | 4,477,780 | — |
    46→| Empty / null titles | 0 | 0% |
    47→| Titles with ≥1 ISBD pattern (incl. trailing `.`) | 1,993,553 | **44.5%** |
    48→| Titles with ≥1 ISBD pattern (excl. trailing `.`) | 1,272,718 | **28.4%** |
    49→
    50→## Pattern Breakdown
    51→
    52→| Pattern | Signal | Count | % |
    53→|---|---|---|---|
    54→| ` :` | Other title information | 909,869 | 20.3% |
    55→| trailing `.` | Area-end period | 783,752 | 17.5% |
    56→| `[ ]` | Supplied / inferred data | 315,263 | 7.0% |
    57→| `…` / `...` | Ellipsis / truncation | 246,370 | 5.5% |
    58→| ` ;` | Subsequent SoR / series | 174,813 | 3.9% |
    59→| ` /` | Statement of responsibility | 33,907 | 0.8% |
    60→| ` =` | Parallel title | 26,264 | 0.6% |
    61→
    62→## Notes on the Trailing Period
    63→
    64→The trailing period (`.` at end of string) is an **ISBD area-closing mark** — it closes the title area when the field doesn't already end with `?`, `!`, or `)`. Examples:
    65→
    66→- `Harzreise im Winter.` — closes title area
    67→- `Goethes Briefe, Bd. 2.` — closes title + numbering
    68→- `Der neue Amadis.` — simple title close
    69→
    70→**Why it's noisy.** The regex `[^.]\.$` (last char is `.`, preceded by a non-dot) also fires on:
    71→
    72→- **Abbreviations as last token**: `Hrsg.`, `Bd.`, `Nr.`, `u.a.` — very common in German bibliographic data
    73→- **Ordinals**: `4.`, `Bd. 3.`
    74→- **Incomplete titles ending in an abbreviation**: `... et al.`
    75→
    76→The 17.5% / 783k figure is therefore an **upper bound**. True ISBD area-closing periods are a subset. To get a cleaner signal, one would need to either:
    77→1. Strip known German abbreviations before applying the pattern, or
    78→2. Use a more conservative heuristic (e.g., require the title to also contain another ISBD marker, suggesting it was catalogued with full ISBD punctuation).
    79→
    80→For the purposes of GeMeA title extraction, ` :` (20.3%) is the most reliable single ISBD indicator — it almost always signals an `other title information` segment that should be split off from the main title.
    81→



```

{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/isbd-applicability.md",
  "content": "# GeMeA — ISBD Rule Applicability in DF_DE_TITLES\n\nSummary of which ISBD punctuation rules work reliably in the DDB corpus, which fail, and which require era- or type-specific heuristics. Synthesises findings from SR-01–SR-04 and the field rating run on 4,477,780 records.\n\n**Sources:** [isbd-field-rating.md](isbd-field-rating.md), [isbd-title-analysis.md](isbd-title-analysis.md), [silver-label-fp-review.md](silver-label-fp-review.md), [translator-person-disambiguation.md](translator-person-disambiguation.md), [ner-bibliographic.md](ner-bibliographic.md)\n\n---\n\n## 1. ISBD area structure in DDB\n\nA fully conformant ISBD record uses `. -` (trailing period + space-dash-space) to separate areas:\n\n```\nTitle proper : other title information / statement of responsibility\n  . - Edition statement\n  . - Place : Publisher, Year\n  . - (Series ; volume)\n```\n\n**In practice, DDB records rarely follow this fully.** Only **1.2%** (53,785 records) contain the `. -` area separator. Most records are catalogued with title-area punctuation only (` :`, ` /`) — the structural tier is almost absent.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Rule-by-rule applicability\n\n### ✅ Reliable rules\n\n| Rule | Signal | Coverage | FP rate | Notes |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `. -` area separator | Structural tier boundary | 1.2% | Very low | When present, field parsing is high-precision; rare in corpus |\n| ` :` OTHER_TITLE | Subtitle boundary | 20.2% | ~8% | Main false positives: `:YYYY–YYYY` life-date colon, ` :: ` DDB catalog separator |\n| `f_year` publication year | 4-digit year regex | 14.6% | ~6% | False positives: founding years (`gegr.`), life dates, manuscript date ranges, composition dates |\n| `f_publisher` | `Verlag`/`Press` keyword | 0.2% | ~0% | Low recall — misses most publishers; high precision when it fires |\n| `f_series` | Parenthetical + ` ;` + digit | 0.0% | ~0% | Very sparse; reliable when present |\n| `f_volume` | `Bd.`, `Teil`, `Heft`, `Nr.` + digit | 1.9% | ~0% | Reliable |\n\n### ❌ Unreliable rules — excluded from silver labels\n\n| Rule | Signal | Coverage | FP rate | Why it fails in DDB |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| ` =` PARALLEL_TITLE | Parallel title | 0.6% | ~80% | DDB serials use `=` for enumeration equivalences (`= Jg. X`, `= Bd.`, `= N.F.`, `= Quartal`) — not parallel titles in another language |\n| `Ausgabe` EDITION | Edition keyword | 3.6% | ~83% | Newspaper issues use \"Ausgabe vom [date]\" for daily issue labels, not edition statements |\n\n### ⚠️ Rules requiring post-filtering\n\n| Rule | Signal | Coverage | FP rate | Required post-filter |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| ` /` PERSON (SoR) | Statement of responsibility | 0.8% | ~36% | Exclude: single-letter series suffixes (`/ K`, `/ M`), corporate body SoRs, date separators (`/ 1701.`), double-slash manuscript transcriptions (`//`) |\n| ` /…;` PERSON_COMPOUND | Compound SoR | 0.0% | ~29% | Exclude: corporate bodies followed by topic subtitles; volume numbers after `;` |\n\n### ➖ Not detectable from title string alone\n\n| Field | Reason |\n|---|---|\n| `PLACE` | Only detectable in structural tier (after `. -`); 0.1% coverage |\n| `TRANSLATOR` | Zero hits in 100-record sample of ` /`-flagged records; translators absent from title string or in separate metadata fields |\n| `EDITOR` | `(Hg.)` suffix and body-text `bearb.` not captured by SoR-text keyword match |\n\n---\n\n## 3. Era-dependent heuristics\n\n### Pre-1750: early modern titles\n\n| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Long-form title pages** | 42–50% of tokens >14; median 12–15 tokens. Full bibliographic description (subtitle, author, place, printer) folded into title string. | All ISBD fields may be present but without ISBD punctuation — rule-based extraction unreliable; NER is the primary path |\n| **Author before title** | Author name + credentials appear at the start of the string, before the work title. No ` /` present. | `f_person = 0` even when an author is named. Detection requires a name-before-title NER pattern, not SoR heuristic. See [silver-label-fp-review.md §5](silver-label-fp-review.md#5-pre-1750-false-negatives--author-before-title) |\n| **Latin titles** | Unknown proportion; Leichenpredigten and pre-Reformation works frequently Latin or Early Modern German. | Tokenisation and stopword removal behave differently; `de_core_news_sm` is not optimised for Latin |\n| **YEAR false positives** | Manuscript dates, death dates, composition dates common in long ISBD strings from this era | Filter years appearing after `gegr.`, `biß`, `bereit … geschrieben`, or in `Anno YYYY biß YYYY` patterns |\n\n### 19th–early 20th century: serials and newspapers (dc_type = issue|Heft|Zeitung)\n\n| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |\n|---|---|---|\n| **`Ausgabe vom [date]`** | Ordinal + \"Ausgabe\" + weekday/date is a newspaper issue label | Exclude `f_edition` for `dc_type` containing `issue`, `Heft`, or `Zeitung` |\n| **`= Jg.`, `= Bd.`, `= N.F.`** | `=` introduces enumeration equivalences in serial records, not parallel titles | Exclude `f_parallel` for serial dc_types; re-apply only when `=` is followed by a non-German string |\n| **Corporate SoRs** | Statistical offices, government agencies named after ` /` (~19% of `f_person` pool in sample) | Post-filter: if the SoR text matches a known institutional keyword (`Landesamt`, `Bundesamt`, `Ministerium`, `Gesellschaft`, `Institut`) → label as CORPORATE, not PERSON |\n\n### Post-2000: digital-born metadata\n\n| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Short titles, separate subtitle fields** | Only 9% short (≤4 tokens), 62% medium; digital metadata stores subtitle in separate fields — the `title` column may contain only the main title | ` :` recall drops; OTHER_TITLE may not appear in title string even when a subtitle exists |\n| **Richer structured descriptions** | Median token count reverses upward (11 tokens) — structured descriptions reappear but in modern format | ISBD `. -` still rare; heuristic rules apply as before |\n\n---\n\n## 4. Field-level summary by dc_type\n\n| dc_type | Reliable fields | Unreliable / excluded | Special note |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Monografie (pre-1750) | `f_other_title`, `f_year` (with date filter) | `f_person` (false negative), `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | Author-before-title pattern; long-form ISBD strings |\n| Monografie (post-1800) | All accepted fields | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | Standard rules apply |\n| issue / Heft / Zeitung | `f_other_title`, `f_year`, `f_volume` | `f_parallel`, `f_edition`, `f_person` (corporate) | Exclude edition + parallel entirely |\n| Leichenpredigt | `f_other_title`, `f_year` (with filter) | `f_person` (false negative) | Name of deceased and husband's credentials embedded mid-title; no SoR marker |\n| Statistische Berichte | `f_other_title`, `f_year`, `f_volume` | `f_person` (corporate SoR) | Post-filter `f_person` for institutional keywords |\n\n---\n\n## 5. Implications for silver label selection\n\nWhen building the NER silver training set from tier-1 records:\n\n1. **Always apply** post-filter on `f_person`: exclude corporate-body SoRs and series-letter suffixes\n2. **Always exclude** `f_parallel` and `f_edition` from silver labels without `dc_type`-conditional logic\n3. **Filter `f_year` false positives** using date-context patterns (founding years, life dates, manuscript dates)\n4. **Apply dc_type guards** before including `f_person` (newspapers) and `f_edition` (non-newspapers)\n5. **Pre-1750 stratum**: annotate author spans from name-before-title pattern separately; do not rely on `f_person`\n6. **Tier 2 (4,613 records)** remains the highest-confidence silver set — all rules apply with structural precision\n"
}
```

```
     1→# GeMeA — Architecture Decision Record: ISBD Field Rating and Silver Dataset
     2→
     3→**Format:** MADR (Markdown Architecture Decision Record)
     4→**Subject:** Design decisions for `scripts/rate_isbd_fields.py` and NER silver dataset strategy
     5→**Source:** `notes/isbd-field-rating.md`, `notes/ner-bibliographic.md`
     6→**Status:** Accepted
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## ADR-01 — Rate field presence as binary flags rather than implementing a full ISBD parser
    11→
    12→**Status:** Accepted
    13→**Date:** 2026-03-21
    14→
    15→### Context
    16→
    17→`link_gnd_works.py` requires an NER fallback for ~72% of DDB title strings that lack clean ISBD punctuation. Training or evaluating an NER model requires knowing which bibliographic fields (TITLE, PERSON, PUBLISHER, etc.) are present and labelable in each record. Two approaches were considered:
    18→
    19→1. **Full ISBD parser** — extract actual span boundaries for every field in every record; produce labeled span output directly
    20→2. **Field presence rating** — detect which fields are likely present as binary flags; defer span extraction to `link_gnd_works.py`
    21→
    22→A full ISBD parser would need to handle all 8 ISBD areas, AACR2 vs. RDA punctuation variants, abbreviation noise (trailing period), multi-volume records, and edge cases across five centuries of German and Latin bibliographic records (pre-1900 cataloging conventions differ significantly from modern ISBD).
    23→
    24→### Decision
    25→
    26→Implement field presence rating (binary flags) rather than a full parser. The rating script detects structural signals to answer "is this field present?" without extracting spans. Span extraction remains the responsibility of `link_gnd_works.py`.
    27→
    28→### Consequences
    29→
    30→**Positive:**
    31→- Fast and simple — fully vectorised pandas operations; runs on 4.47M records in seconds
    32→- Clean separation of concerns: detection (this script) vs. extraction (`link_gnd_works.py`)
    33→- Output is directly usable for silver tier assignment, gold set stratification, and corpus statistics
    34→- Easier to validate: binary flags on a 200-record sample are quicker to audit than span boundaries
    35→
    36→**Negative:**
    37→- Does not produce training labels directly; still requires a separate span extraction step
    38→- Binary flags do not distinguish multiple instances (e.g., two ` /` segments in a compound SoR)
    39→
    40→**Mitigations:**
    41→- Silver tier 2 criterion (`has_dot_dash AND f_person AND ≥1 manifestation field`) ensures only structurally clean records reach the primary training set, where span extraction is reliable
    42→- Multi-instance cases are rare enough that they do not significantly affect training data quality at the selected tier
    43→
    44→---
    45→
    46→## ADR-02 — Two-tier detection: structural (`. -`) and heuristic (whole-string)
    47→
    48→**Status:** Accepted
    49→**Date:** 2026-03-21
    50→
    51→### Context
    52→
    53→ISBD area structure is only present in ~28% of DF_DE_TITLES records. For the remaining ~72%, no area separator exists and field boundaries are ambiguous. Two options for handling these records:
    54→
    55→1. **Structural-only** — only detect fields in records with `. -`; leave 72% unrated
    56→2. **Two-tier** — apply structural parsing where `. -` is present; fall back to whole-string heuristics for the rest
    57→
    58→### Decision
    59→
    60→Use two-tier detection. For records without `. -`, apply whole-string regex patterns with reduced confidence. PLACE and PUBLISHER are only detected in the structural tier. The `silver_tier` column encodes tier quality: tier 2 requires structural evidence; tier 1 accepts heuristic evidence.
    61→
    62→### Consequences
    63→
    64→**Positive:**
    65→- Heuristic tier covers 72% of the corpus; even low-precision field flags are useful for gold set stratification and corpus statistics
    66→- `silver_tier` field makes confidence explicit — downstream users know which records have structural vs. heuristic support
    67→
    68→**Negative:**
    69→- Heuristic ` :` fires on non-ISBD colons; ` /` fires on fractions or file paths; false positive rate unknown
    70→
    71→**Mitigations:**
    72→- Tier 1 silver candidates require manual validation on ~200-record sample before use in NER training
    73→- PLACE intentionally excluded from heuristic tier to avoid noisy positives
    74→
    75→---
    76→
    77→## ADR-03 — Title-string-only rating; exclude auxiliary columns (dc_publisher, dc_creator)
    78→
    79→**Status:** Accepted
    80→**Date:** 2026-03-21
    81→
    82→### Context
    83→
    84→DF_DE_TITLES contains `dc_publisher`, `dc_creator`, and `dc_contributor` columns that could supplement field detection. For example, `dc_publisher` often contains "Dresden : Riedel" — a clean imprint string that would enable reliable PLACE and PUBLISHER detection even without `. -` in the title. Three options:
    85→
    86→1. **Title-only** — rate only the `title` column
    87→2. **Title + auxiliaries** — use `dc_publisher`, `dc_creator` to fill gaps
    88→3. **Auxiliary-first** — prefer auxiliary columns; fall back to title parsing
    89→
    90→### Decision
    91→
    92→Rate only the `title` column. The goal of this script is to assess what is detectable from the string that `link_gnd_works.py` actually receives at inference time — which is the title string, not the full record. Training the NER model on auxiliary-column labels would create a mismatch between training signal and inference context.
    93→
    94→### Consequences
    95→
    96→**Positive:**
    97→- Ratings faithfully reflect what the NER model will see at inference time
    98→- Avoids mixing detection sources that have different reliability profiles
    99→
   100→**Negative:**
   101→- PUBLISHER and PLACE recall is low for heuristic-tier records; `dc_publisher` could provide high-quality imprint labels for ~3M records without `. -`
   102→
   103→**Mitigations:**
   104→- A future script can join `isbd_field_ratings.csv` with `dc_publisher` for richer corpus statistics (outside the scope of NER training data)
   105→- The paper's evaluation section should note that publisher/place coverage reflects title-string detection only
   106→
   107→---
   108→
   109→## ADR-04 — Silver tier 2 requires has_dot_dash + PERSON + ≥1 manifestation field
   110→
   111→**Status:** Accepted
   112→**Date:** 2026-03-21
   113→
   114→### Context
   115→
   116→Silver labels are auto-generated from ISBD structure; their quality directly determines NER model quality. The tier 2 criterion was chosen to balance three concerns:
   117→
   118→1. **Annotation completeness** — at least two distinct field types must be labelable (TITLE always present + something else)
   119→2. **Structural reliability** — span boundaries must be unambiguous (requires `. -`)
   120→3. **Training signal diversity** — silver set must include both Work-level (TITLE, PERSON) and Manifestation-level (PUBLISHER, PLACE, YEAR) labels
   121→
   122→Alternative tier 2 criteria considered:
   123→- `has_dot_dash AND n_fields ≥ 3` — too broad; includes records where only ` :` + YEAR are detected (no PERSON)
   124→- `has_dot_dash AND f_person AND f_year` — misses records with PUBLISHER/PLACE but no explicit year in title string
   125→
   126→### Decision
   127→
   128→Require `has_dot_dash AND f_person AND any(f_edition, f_place, f_publisher, f_year, f_series)`. This guarantees:
   129→- Work-level signal (PERSON from ` /`)
   130→- At least one Manifestation-level signal
   131→- Structural area separation (unambiguous span boundaries)
   132→
   133→### Consequences
   134→
   135→**Positive:**
   136→- Tier 2 records are the richest for multi-field NER training — TITLE, PERSON, and ≥1 Manifestation label per record
   137→- `has_dot_dash` requirement filters out most heuristic false positives
   138→
   139→**Negative:**
   140→- Tier 2 will cover only a small fraction of the corpus (~1–5% estimated); most ISBD-structured records lack ` /`
   141→- PERSON (` /`) appears in only 0.8% of DF_DE_TITLES; tier 2 will be a small but high-quality set
   142→
   143→**Mitigations:**
   144→- Tier 1 (`n_fields ≥ 3` or `f_person AND f_year`) supplements tier 2 for augmentation
   145→- The small size of tier 2 is expected and acceptable — 5–50K structurally clean records is sufficient for fine-tuning if needed
   146→



```

Reduced precision for PLACE/PUBLISHER (only `Verlag` keyword → PUBLISHER; PLACE not flagged without area structure).\n\nFP rates from SR-03 validation (200-record stratified sample). See [silver-label-fp-review.md](silver-label-fp-review.md).\n\n| Field | Pattern | FP rate | Silver label? | Notes |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| `OTHER_TITLE` | ` :` anywhere | ~8% | ✅ Accept | FPs: `:YYYY–YYYY` life-date colon; ` :: ` DDB catalog separator |\n| `PERSON` | ` /` anywhere | ~36% | ⚠️ Post-filter | FPs: series letter suffixes, corporate SoRs, date separators — see SoR sub-classification below |\n| `PERSON_COMPOUND` | ` /…;` | ~29% | ⚠️ Post-filter | FPs: corporate body + topic subtitles; volume numbers after `;` |\n| `PARALLEL_TITLE` | ` =` anywhere | ~80% | ❌ Exclude | DDB serials use `=` for enumeration (`= Jg. X`, `= N.F.`), not parallel titles |\n| `EDITION` | Edition keyword regex | ~83% | ❌ Exclude | \"Ausgabe vom [date]\" in newspapers is an issue-date label, not edition; exclude for `dc_type` = issue/Heft/Zeitung |\n| `YEAR` | 4-digit year regex | ~6% | ✅ Accept | FPs: founding years (`gegr.`), life dates, manuscript date ranges, composition dates |\n| `PUBLISHER` | `\\bVerlag\\b` or `\\bPress\\b` | ~0% | ✅ Accept | Low recall; high precision when fires |\n| `PLACE` | Not detected | — | ➖ N/A | Requires `. -` area structure |\n| `SERIES` | `\\([^)]+;\\s*[^)]*\\d[^)]*\\)` | ~0% | ✅ Accept | Very sparse but reliable |\n| `VOLUME` | Volume keyword regex | ~0% | ✅ Accept | Reliable |\n\n### SoR sub-classification (f_person post-filter)\n\nSR-04 validation (100-record sample of `f_person = 1`, heuristic tier) found that ` /` fires on four distinct content types, not only personal name SoRs. A post-classification step is required before using `f_person` as a silver label. See [translator-person-disambiguation.md](translator-person-disambiguation.md).\n\n| Sub-class | flag | Count in sample | Detection heuristic |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Individual person (author) | `f_sor_person` | 35% | SoR text matches personal name pattern; no corporate or editor keyword |\n| Corporate body | `f_sor_corp` | 19% | SoR text matches institutional keyword: `Landesamt`, `Bundesamt`, `Ministerium`, `Gesellschaft`, `Institut`, `Universität`, `Akademie`, `Verband`, `Amt`, `Behörde` |\n| Editor / adaptor | `f_sor_editor` | 5% | SoR text matches: `Hrsg.`, `herausgegeben`, `(Hg.)`, `bearb.`, `Bearbeitung`, `edited by`, `zusammengestellt` |\n| Non-SoR false positive | `f_sor_other` | 41% | None of the above; ` /` is a series suffix, fraction, date separator, etc. |\n| Translator | `f_sor_translator` | 0% | Not detectable from title strings in this corpus — translators absent from title or in separate metadata fields |\n| Family name | `f_sor_family` | — | Not validated; ISBD family entries are rare in DDB; `Familie`, `Nachlass` may be weak signals |\n\n**Alignment with ISBD/MARC entity types:** MARC 21 / RDA encode the responsible entity type in the indicator of 1xx/7xx fields: individual (ind1=0/1), corporate body (ind1=2), family (ind1=3 in RDA). The `f_sor_*` sub-classification mirrors this at the heuristic level.\n\n**Note on TRANSLATOR:** Zero true translators found in the 100-record sample. `DF_DE_TITLES` contains very few translated works with explicit SoR markers in the title string — do not use as a silver label target."
}

See [translator-person-disambiguation.md](translator-person-disambiguation.md).\n\n**SR-10: title length by era.** Pre-1750 titles are 42–50% long (>14 tokens); post-1775 shift to median 6–9 tokens; 2000–2024 reversal. `all_tokens` includes stopwords and punctuation (spaCy `de_core_news_sm`). See [de-titles-distribution.md](de-titles-distribution.md) and [title-length-thresholds.md](title-length-thresholds.md)."

### Assistant *(2026-03-23T20:47:39.347Z)*

**Tool call:** `Edit`
```json
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/isbd-field-rating-adr.md",
  "old_string": "**Mitigations:**\n- Tier 1 (`n_fields ≥ 3` or `f_person AND f_year`) supplements tier 2 for augmentation\n- The small size of tier 2 is expected and acceptable — 5–50K structurally clean records is sufficient for fine-tuning if needed\n",
  "new_string": "**Mitigations:**\n- Tier 1 (`n_fields ≥ 3` or `f_person AND f_year`) supplements tier 2 for augmentation\n- The small size of tier 2 is expected and acceptable — 5–50K structurally clean records is sufficient for fine-tuning if needed\n\n---\n\n## ADR-05 — Exclude f_parallel and f_edition from heuristic silver labels\n\n**Status:** Accepted\n**Date:** 2026-03-23\n**Amends:** ADR-02 (heuristic tier reliability assumptions)\n**Evidence:** SR-03 — [silver-label-fp-review.md](silver-label-fp-review.md)\n\n### Context\n\nADR-02 listed ` =` (PARALLEL_TITLE) and edition keywords (EDITION) as \"generally reliable\" in the heuristic tier. SR-03 validation on a 200-record stratified sample found FP rates of ~80% and ~83% respectively — far exceeding the 15% acceptance threshold.\n\n- **`f_parallel`** (~80% FP): DDB serial records systematically use ` =` for enumeration equivalences (`= Jg. X`, `= Bd.`, `= N.F.`, `= Quartal`) rather than parallel titles in another language. Parallel titles in two languages are rare in a German-language corpus.\n- **`f_edition`** (~83% FP): Newspaper and periodical records use \"Ausgabe vom [weekday, date]\" as an issue-date label, not an edition statement. The edition keyword regex (`Ausgabe`, `Aufl`) fires on this pattern throughout the serials stratum.\n\n### Decision\n\nExclude `f_parallel` and `f_edition` from heuristic-tier silver labels entirely. In the structural tier (`. -` present), both fields remain valid — the area separator disambiguates the edition area from title-area colons, and parallel titles in the edition area are genuine. Apply `dc_type` guard for `f_edition` in future: exclude for `dc_type` containing `issue`, `Heft`, or `Zeitung`; accept for `Monografie`.\n\n### Consequences\n\n**Positive:**\n- Eliminates the two highest-FP fields from the silver training set\n- Consistent with the `isbd-applicability.md` rule table\n\n**Negative:**\n- Reduces field diversity in tier-1 silver labels (EDITION and PARALLEL_TITLE spans unavailable from heuristic tier)\n- PARALLEL_TITLE may still be detectable via `dc_type`-conditional logic for non-serial records — left as future work\n\n---\n\n## ADR-06 — Sub-classify f_person SoR into f_resp_* entity types\n\n**Status:** Accepted\n**Date:** 2026-03-23\n**Amends:** ADR-04 (tier 2 PERSON requirement)\n**Evidence:** SR-04 — [translator-person-disambiguation.md](translator-person-disambiguation.md)\n\n### Context\n\nADR-04 treats `f_person` (` /` present) as equivalent to a PERSON (individual author) SoR. SR-04 validation on a 100-record sample of heuristic `f_person` records found that only 35% are true individual-person SoRs. The remaining 65% are:\n\n- **Corporate bodies** (19%) — government agencies, statistical offices, research institutions\n- **Non-SoR false positives** (41%) — series letter suffixes (`/ K`), date separators, manuscript slashes\n- **Editors** (5%) — `Hrsg.`, `(Hg.)`, `bearb.`\n- **Translators** (0%) — not detectable from title strings in this corpus\n\nThis corresponds to the MARC 21 / RDA distinction between responsible entity types: individual (ind1=0/1), corporate body (ind1=2), family (ind1=3).\n\n### Decision\n\nExtend the output schema with `f_resp_*` sub-classification flags derived from post-filtering `f_person`:\n\n| Flag | Entity type | Detection heuristic |\n|---|---|---|\n| `f_resp_person` | Individual person (author) | No corporate/editor keyword; personal name pattern |\n| `f_resp_corp` | Corporate body | Institutional keyword: `Landesamt`, `Bundesamt`, `Ministerium`, `Gesellschaft`, `Institut`, `Universität`, `Akademie`, `Verband`, `Amt`, `Behörde` |\n| `f_resp_editor` | Editor / adaptor | `Hrsg.`, `herausgegeben`, `(Hg.)`, `bearb.`, `edited by`, `zusammengestellt` |\n| `f_resp_family` | Family name | Not yet validated; candidate signals: `Familie`, `Nachlass` |\n| `f_resp_other` | Non-SoR false positive | None of the above |\n\nThe existing `f_person` flag is retained unchanged for backward compatibility. The `f_resp_*` flags are a post-processing layer, not a replacement.\n\nFor tier 2 silver (ADR-04), the PERSON criterion is tightened: require `f_resp_person = 1` rather than `f_person = 1` to ensure individual-person SoRs only.\n\n### Consequences\n\n**Positive:**\n- Silver PERSON labels are restricted to individual-person SoRs — eliminates corporate-body contamination from NER training data\n- Corporate body SoRs (19%) surface as a distinct class for future `CORPORATE` label work\n- Aligns flag naming with ISBD/MARC entity-type conventions\n\n**Negative:**\n- Requires implementing the `f_resp_*` post-filter in `rate_isbd_fields.py` or a new downstream script\n- `f_resp_family` is unvalidated — keep out of silver labels until a dedicated sample is reviewed\n\n**Mitigations:**\n- The keyword lists for `f_resp_corp` and `f_resp_editor` are drawn directly from SR-04 findings and can be extended incrementally\n- `f_resp_other` acts as a catch-all that keeps false positives out of any silver label category\n"
}
```

https://ddb.de/item/JD76UJ72HGLPBD57MJL4X5TDNTT6VQIL

https://ddb.de/item/MBL5TP4QQTJE42U3LVAACUXAIUSMKTFI

https://ddb.de/item/JBIOMGMUG5PXUG7RIS44KJYCY553B34U

"Jeversches Wochenblatt : Friesisches Tageblatt ; gegr. 1791"
https://ddb.de/item/AGZJAK7XYRNH3IWXEWFVELM4OFBJARLL
1791 is the founding year (gegr. = gegründet), not the publication year; fp_fields = f_year.

"Porträt Georg Philipp Wucherer (1734 - 1805) :Kupferstich ; Radierung"
https://ddb.de/item/7EG6MNM55XRFKT63ZIUZN35OZAZMMY2B
The year range 1734–1805 consists of life dates of the portrayed person in parentheses; fp_fields = f_year.

"Verlag von Q. Spamer" is a genuine publisher name embedded in the title; f_publisher fires correctly, fp_fields empty.

https://ddb.de/item/QFX2RP37W4IUCIGM3VE3FAUSYERTN5OY

https://ddb.de/item/EFXCKGZWS446BTD22OVYS4FNACIZR2RR

https://ddb.de/item/O6J6EQJNBQ7DW2RGCJQNBGLFYZ5DNNRZ

https://ddb.de/item/HBGATPAF5KAQZFMSNA5FUZ3OQC2ZS3KH

---

**11. `f_person` editor**

"Ueber den Austritt aus dem Judenthume : ein aufgefundener Briefwechsel / hrsg. von Abraham Geiger"
https://ddb.de/item/HGCH7UKPABAHS46CGTA6D4V5CQOELPOY
"hrsg. von" keyword clearly identifies the SoR as an editor, not an author; true_class = EDITOR.

"Friedrich Lübkers Reallexikon des klassischen Altertums / Hrsg. von J. Geffcken ; E. Ziebarth"
https://ddb.de/item/Z5C64JC4HY2HVJK7BOPOFQJL4U2YN6KC
"Hrsg. von" with two named editors separated by semicolon; true_class = EDITOR.

---

**12. `f_person` non-SoR false positive (OTHER)**

"Ah fuggi il traditor / O flieh den Bösewicht"
https://ddb.de/item/6GPNJKG4RDEVFEDHMGU5F7OKLWG2N2GT
` /` separates the Italian title from its German parallel title, not a person SoR; true_class = OTHER.

"Große Monden=Finsternüß / Auff das 1667. Jahr Christi /"
https://ddb.de/item/V6ETCXEEFF3VP3TAJCI73J3IQIJOGGQF
` /` introduces a date qualifier appended to the title ("Auff das 1667. Jahr Christi"), not a person; true_class = OTHER.

---

**13. `f_other_title` false positive**

"Johann Ludwig Böhner :7. Januar 1787 - 28. März 1860 ; [Katalog]"
https://ddb.de/item/THF6HTNRUTSYTYBY377JLKXHCWVHNYQP
` :` precedes the person's life dates (7 Jan 1787–28 Mar 1860), not an ISBD subtitle; fp_fields contains f_other_title and f_year.

"Feldmarschall Ludwig Andreas Graf von Khevenhüller-Frankenburg seiner großen Herrscherin 'treuer Vasall und Beschützer' :1683-1744 ; eine Lebensskizze"
https://ddb.de/item/SKUC2ZDHLCDGXDWQ2NMGSDXZ5U6GLRPC
` :` introduces a life-date range (1683–1744), not a subtitle; the colon follows the person's name in a biographical entry format; fp_fields contains f_other_title and f_year.

```
     1→# GeMeA — ISBD Rule Applicability in DF_DE_TITLES
     2→
     3→Summary of which ISBD punctuation rules work reliably in the DDB corpus, which fail, and which require era- or type-specific heuristics. Synthesises findings from SR-01–SR-04 and the field rating run on 4,477,780 records.
     4→
     5→**Sources:** [isbd-field-rating.md](isbd-field-rating.md), [isbd-title-analysis.md](isbd-title-analysis.md), [silver-label-fp-review.md](silver-label-fp-review.md), [translator-person-disambiguation.md](translator-person-disambiguation.md), [ner-bibliographic.md](ner-bibliographic.md)
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1. ISBD area structure in DDB
    10→
    11→A fully conformant ISBD record uses `. -` (trailing period + space-dash-space) to separate areas:
    12→
    13→```
    14→Title proper : other title information / statement of responsibility
    15→  . - Edition statement
    16→  . - Place : Publisher, Year
    17→  . - (Series ; volume)
    18→```
    19→
    20→**In practice, DDB records rarely follow this fully.** Only **1.2%** (53,785 records) contain the `. -` area separator. Most records are catalogued with title-area punctuation only (` :`, ` /`) — the structural tier is almost absent.
    21→
    22→---
    23→
    24→## 2. Rule-by-rule applicability
    25→
    26→### ✅ Reliable rules
    27→
    28→| Rule | Signal | Coverage | FP rate | Notes |
    29→|---|---|---|---|---|
    30→| `. -` area separator | Structural tier boundary | 1.2% | Very low | When present, field parsing is high-precision; rare in corpus |
    31→| ` :` OTHER_TITLE | Subtitle boundary | 20.2% | ~8% | Main false positives: `:YYYY–YYYY` life-date colon, ` :: ` DDB catalog separator |
    32→| `f_year` publication year | 4-digit year regex | 14.6% | ~6% | False positives: founding years (`gegr.`), life dates, manuscript date ranges, composition dates |
    33→| `f_publisher` | `Verlag`/`Press` keyword | 0.2% | ~0% | Low recall — misses most publishers; high precision when it fires |
    34→| `f_series` | Parenthetical + ` ;` + digit | 0.0% | ~0% | Very sparse; reliable when present |
    35→| `f_volume` | `Bd.`, `Teil`, `Heft`, `Nr.` + digit | 1.9% | ~0% | Reliable |
    36→
    37→### ❌ Unreliable rules — excluded from silver labels
    38→
    39→| Rule | Signal | Coverage | FP rate | Why it fails in DDB |
    40→|---|---|---|---|---|
    41→| ` =` PARALLEL_TITLE | Parallel title | 0.6% | ~80% | DDB serials use `=` for enumeration equivalences (`= Jg. X`, `= Bd.`, `= N.F.`, `= Quartal`) — not parallel titles in another language |
    42→| `Ausgabe` EDITION | Edition keyword | 3.6% | ~83% | Newspaper issues use "Ausgabe vom [date]" for daily issue labels, not edition statements |
    43→
    44→### ⚠️ Rules requiring post-filtering
    45→
    46→| Rule | Signal | Coverage | FP rate | Required post-filter |
    47→|---|---|---|---|---|
    48→| ` /` PERSON (SoR) | Statement of responsibility | 0.8% | ~36% | Exclude: single-letter series suffixes (`/ K`, `/ M`), corporate body SoRs, date separators (`/ 1701.`), double-slash manuscript transcriptions (`//`) |
    49→| ` /…;` PERSON_COMPOUND | Compound SoR | 0.0% | ~29% | Exclude: corporate bodies followed by topic subtitles; volume numbers after `;` |
    50→
    51→### ➖ Not detectable from title string alone
    52→
    53→| Field | Reason |
    54→|---|---|
    55→| `PLACE` | Only detectable in structural tier (after `. -`); 0.1% coverage |
    56→| `TRANSLATOR` | Zero hits in 100-record sample of ` /`-flagged records; translators absent from title string or in separate metadata fields |
    57→| `EDITOR` | `(Hg.)` suffix and body-text `bearb.` not captured by SoR-text keyword match |
    58→
    59→---
    60→
    61→## 3. Era-dependent heuristics
    62→
    63→### Pre-1750: early modern titles
    64→
    65→| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |
    66→|---|---|---|
    67→| **Long-form title pages** | 42–50% of tokens >14; median 12–15 tokens. Full bibliographic description (subtitle, author, place, printer) folded into title string. | All ISBD fields may be present but without ISBD punctuation — rule-based extraction unreliable; NER is the primary path |
    68→| **Author before title** | Author name + credentials appear at the start of the string, before the work title. No ` /` present. | `f_person = 0` even when an author is named. Detection requires a name-before-title NER pattern, not SoR heuristic. See [silver-label-fp-review.md §5](silver-label-fp-review.md#5-pre-1750-false-negatives--author-before-title) |
    69→| **Latin titles** | Unknown proportion; Leichenpredigten and pre-Reformation works frequently Latin or Early Modern German. | Tokenisation and stopword removal behave differently; `de_core_news_sm` is not optimised for Latin |
    70→| **YEAR false positives** | Manuscript dates, death dates, composition dates common in long ISBD strings from this era | Filter years appearing after `gegr.`, `biß`, `bereit … geschrieben`, or in `Anno YYYY biß YYYY` patterns |
    71→
    72→### 19th–early 20th century: serials and newspapers (dc_type = issue|Heft|Zeitung)
    73→
    74→| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |
    75→|---|---|---|
    76→| **`Ausgabe vom [date]`** | Ordinal + "Ausgabe" + weekday/date is a newspaper issue label | Exclude `f_edition` for `dc_type` containing `issue`, `Heft`, or `Zeitung` |
    77→| **`= Jg.`, `= Bd.`, `= N.F.`** | `=` introduces enumeration equivalences in serial records, not parallel titles | Exclude `f_parallel` for serial dc_types; re-apply only when `=` is followed by a non-German string |
    78→| **Corporate SoRs** | Statistical offices, government agencies named after ` /` (~19% of `f_person` pool in sample) | Post-filter: if the SoR text matches a known institutional keyword (`Landesamt`, `Bundesamt`, `Ministerium`, `Gesellschaft`, `Institut`) → label as CORPORATE, not PERSON |
    79→
    80→### Post-2000: digital-born metadata
    81→
    82→| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |
    83→|---|---|---|
    84→| **Short titles, separate subtitle fields** | Only 9% short (≤4 tokens), 62% medium; digital metadata stores subtitle in separate fields — the `title` column may contain only the main title | ` :` recall drops; OTHER_TITLE may not appear in title string even when a subtitle exists |
    85→| **Richer structured descriptions** | Median token count reverses upward (11 tokens) — structured descriptions reappear but in modern format | ISBD `. -` still rare; heuristic rules apply as before |
    86→
    87→---
    88→
    89→## 4. Field-level summary by dc_type
    90→
    91→| dc_type | Reliable fields | Unreliable / excluded | Special note |
    92→|---|---|---|---|
    93→| Monografie (pre-1750) | `f_other_title`, `f_year` (with date filter) | `f_person` (false negative), `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | Author-before-title pattern; long-form ISBD strings |
    94→| Monografie (post-1800) | All accepted fields | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | Standard rules apply |
    95→| issue / Heft / Zeitung | `f_other_title`, `f_year`, `f_volume` | `f_parallel`, `f_edition`, `f_person` (corporate) | Exclude edition + parallel entirely |
    96→| Leichenpredigt | `f_other_title`, `f_year` (with filter) | `f_person` (false negative) | Name of deceased and husband's credentials embedded mid-title; no SoR marker |
    97→| Statistische Berichte | `f_other_title`, `f_year`, `f_volume` | `f_person` (corporate SoR) | Post-filter `f_person` for institutional keywords |
    98→
    99→---
   100→
   101→## 5. Implications for silver label selection
   102→
   103→When building the NER silver training set from tier-1 records:
   104→
   105→1. **Always apply** post-filter on `f_person`: exclude corporate-body SoRs and series-letter suffixes
   106→2. **Always exclude** `f_parallel` and `f_edition` from silver labels without `dc_type`-conditional logic
   107→3. **Filter `f_year` false positives** using date-context patterns (founding years, life dates, manuscript dates)
   108→4. **Apply dc_type guards** before including `f_person` (newspapers) and `f_edition` (non-newspapers)
   109→5. **Pre-1750 stratum**: annotate author spans from name-before-title pattern separately; do not rely on `f_person`
   110→6. **Tier 2 (4,613 records)** remains the highest-confidence silver set — all rules apply with structural precision
   111→



```

{

  "content": "# GeMeA — ISBD Rule Applicability in DF_DE_TITLES\n\nSummary of which ISBD punctuation rules work reliably in the DDB corpus, which fail, and which require era- or type-specific heuristics. Synthesises findings from SR-01–SR-04 and the field rating run on 4,477,780 records.\n\n**Sources:** [isbd-field-rating.md](isbd-field-rating.md), [isbd-title-analysis.md](isbd-title-analysis.md), [silver-label-fp-review.md](silver-label-fp-review.md), [translator-person-disambiguation.md](translator-person-disambiguation.md), [ner-bibliographic.md](ner-bibliographic.md)\n\n---\n\n## 1. ISBD area structure in DDB\n\nA fully conformant ISBD record uses `. -` (trailing period + space-dash-space) to separate areas:\n\n```\nTitle proper : other title information / statement of responsibility\n  . - Edition statement\n  . - Place : Publisher, Year\n  . - (Series ; volume)\n```\n\n**In practice, DDB records rarely follow this fully.** Only **1.2%** (53,785 records) contain the `. -` area separator. Most records are catalogued with title-area punctuation only (` :`, ` /`) — the structural tier is almost absent.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Rule-by-rule applicability\n\n### ✅ Reliable rules\n\n#### `. -` area separator — structural tier boundary\n\nCoverage: 1.2% | FP rate: very low\n\nWhen present, field parsing is high-precision; the separator unambiguously marks area boundaries. Rare in corpus — the structural tier covers only 53k records.\n\n| Title | URL |\n|---|---|\n| `Kriegstagebuch der Seekriegsleitung 1939 - 1945. - Teil A ; Band 65. Januar 1945` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/UR75EQHR72BNWYVZG3XKMUHOAKSLJ2XO) |\n| `Ohne Grenzen :XXXII. Deutscher Kunsthistorikertag … - 24. März 2013; Tagungsband` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/JD76UJ72HGLPBD57MJL4X5TDNTT6VQIL) |\n\n---\n\n#### ` :` OTHER_TITLE — subtitle boundary\n\nCoverage: 20.2% | FP rate: ~8%\n\nMain false positives: `:YYYY–YYYY` life-date colon; ` :: ` DDB catalog-field separator.\n\n**True positives:**\n\n| Title | URL |\n|---|---|\n| `Der Weltverkehr und seine Mittel :Rundschau über Schiffahrt und Welthandel …` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/5LOLZLJ77E2OZ3FXXWICQGVZJEXFQ542) |\n| `Modulhandbuch Bachelor Angewandte Medien- und Kommunikationswissenschaft :Studienordnungsversion: 2012` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/MBL5TP4QQTJE42U3LVAACUXAIUSMKTFI) |\n\n**False positives:**\n\n| Title | URL | Reason |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Johann Ludwig Böhner :7. Januar 1787 - 28. März 1860 ; [Katalog]` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/THF6HTNRUTSYTYBY377JLKXHCWVHNYQP) | `:` precedes life dates, not subtitle |\n| `Feldmarschall … Khevenhüller-Frankenburg … :1683-1744 ; eine Lebensskizze` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/SKUC2ZDHLCDGXDWQ2NMGSDXZ5U6GLRPC) | `:` precedes life-date range |\n\n---\n\n#### `f_year` — publication year (4-digit regex 1400–2029)\n\nCoverage: 14.6% | FP rate: ~6%\n\n**True positives:**\n\n| Title | URL |\n|---|---|\n| `Allgemeine Deutsche Krankenkasse … : IV. Quartal 1922` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/JBIOMGMUG5PXUG7RIS44KJYCY553B34U) |\n| `Programm des Evangelischen Gymnasiums … 1876/77` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/PRFIMYOD64K5YG73YHJWSV6N7CRAOYCC) |\n\n**False positives:**\n\n| Title | URL | Reason |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Jeversches Wochenblatt : Friesisches Tageblatt ; gegr. 1791` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/AGZJAK7XYRNH3IWXEWFVELM4OFBJARLL) | Founding year (`gegr.`) |\n| `Porträt Georg Philipp Wucherer (1734 - 1805) :Kupferstich ; Radierung` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/7EG6MNM55XRFKT63ZIUZN35OZAZMMY2B) | Life dates in parentheses |\n\n---\n\n#### `f_publisher` — `Verlag`/`Press` keyword\n\nCoverage: 0.2% | FP rate: ~0%\n\nLow recall — misses most publishers; high precision when fires.\n\n| Title | URL |\n|---|---|\n| `Jugendschriften : aus dem Verlag von Q. Spamer in Leipzig` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/2RIK6UPHKWTOU65R3S5GOUR5GYQULL4I) |\n| `Information des Bereiches FDJ-Presse im Verlag Junge Welt: 19/66 vom 20. Mai 1966 ; [Mitteilungen]` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/QFX2RP37W4IUCIGM3VE3FAUSYERTN5OY) |\n\n---\n\n#### `f_series` — parenthetical + ` ;` + digit\n\nCoverage: 0.0% | FP rate: ~0%\n\nVery sparse; reliable when present.\n\n---\n\n#### `f_volume` — `Bd.`, `Teil`, `Heft`, `Nr.` + digit\n\nCoverage: 1.9% | FP rate: ~0%\n\n| Title | URL |\n|---|---|\n| `Ärztliches Vereinsblatt für Deutschland. Jg. 22, Nr. 269` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/EFXCKGZWS446BTD22OVYS4FNACIZR2RR) |\n| `Das Echo. Jg. 17, Nr. 819` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/O6J6EQJNBQ7DW2RGCJQNBGLFYZ5DNNRZ) |\n\n---\n\n### ❌ Unreliable rules — excluded from silver labels\n\n#### ` =` PARALLEL_TITLE\n\nCoverage: 0.6% | FP rate: ~80%\n\nDDB serial records systematically use `=` for enumeration equivalences (`= Jg. X`, `= Bd.`, `= N.F.`, `= Quartal`), not parallel titles in another language.\n\n| Title | URL | Reason |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Allgemeine Zeitung. 1898, 1898 = Jg. 101, 4 - 5` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/ZUE6UGELN66IR7NQR3QEZSGEHRPKDHD6) | `=` introduces volume enumeration |\n| `Verzeichnis der Vorlesungen … 1833/34 (1833) = Winter-Halbjahr` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/HBGATPAF5KAQZFMSNA5FUZ3OQC2ZS3KH) | `=` introduces semester designation |\n\n---\n\n#### `Ausgabe` EDITION keyword\n\nCoverage: 3.6% | FP rate: ~83%\n\nNewspaper and periodical records use \"Ausgabe vom [weekday, date]\" as an issue-date label, not an edition statement.\n\n| Title | URL | Reason |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Erste Ausgabe vom Dienstag, den 18. Mai 1937.` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/YASRD5RWR6SXOLRMJ24A66FL4EWJ5ZNP) | Newspaper print-run label |\n| `Dritte Ausgabe vom Samstag, den 21. Dezember 1895.` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/G474LKFU2M4HUG6SNJJFK2CPUNEBQIOC) | Same pattern — ordinal + issue date |\n\n---\n\n### ⚠️ Rules requiring SoR sub-classification\n\n#### ` /` Statement of Responsibility\n\nCoverage: 0.8% | FP rate: ~36% for PERSON label\n\n` /` detects the presence of a responsible entity but does not distinguish entity type. From SR-04 (100-record sample):\n\n| Entity type | `f_resp_*` flag | % of ` /` pool | Example |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **Individual person** | `f_resp_person` | 35% | `… / von M. Rosenstock` [↗](https://ddb.de/item/ZGN4C7ZJ7NBZV65ZSIZHC5N2SM3VGIOB) |\n| **Corporate body** | `f_resp_corp` | 19% | `… / Privates Katholisches Lyzeum Magdeburg` [↗](https://ddb.de/item/OROZ7FMM4X4XDYVYBJSJP33NZKIFPUD5) |\n| **Editor / adaptor** | `f_resp_editor` | 5% | `… / hrsg. von Abraham Geiger` [↗](https://ddb.de/item/HGCH7UKPABAHS46CGTA6D4V5CQOELPOY) |\n| **Non-SoR false positive** | `f_resp_other` | 41% | `Ah fuggi il traditor / O flieh den Bösewicht` [↗](https://ddb.de/item/6GPNJKG4RDEVFEDHMGU5F7OKLWG2N2GT) |\n| **Translator** | `f_resp_translator` | 0% | Not detectable from title strings in this corpus |\n| **Family** | `f_resp_family` | — | Not yet validated |\n\nCorporate body SoRs are a **recognised entity class** (19% of the ` /` pool), not noise — they map to the MARC 21 corporate name entry (1xx/7xx ind1=2) and may warrant a `CORPORATE` NER label in future. They should be sub-classified with `f_resp_corp`, not silently discarded.\n\nAdditional corporate body example:\n`Jahresbericht … / Hochschule für Angewandte Wissenschaften Würzburg-Schweinfurt` [↗](https://ddb.de/item/NC2UQJ4O4RF76SG3G2DPJ4DNEC6KTOSM)\n\n---\n\n#### ` /…;` PERSON_COMPOUND\n\nCoverage: 0.0% | FP rate: ~29%\n\n| Title | URL | Reason |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Jahrbuch / Deutsche Shakespeare-Gesellschaft; 3` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/BDMEHSHZCBPUG6NL3OKG4FMKKGL4VHMH) | `; 3` is a volume number |\n| `Statistische Berichte / Hessisches Statistisches Landesamt … ; Ergebnisse nach Verwaltungsbezirken …` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/GKSDCS5H4ERC4ZTPNRBOBMH5ZDU6WQN2) | Corporate SoR; `;` separates topic subtitles |\n\n---\n\n### ➖ Not detectable from title string alone\n\n| Field | Reason |\n|---|---|\n| `PLACE` | Only detectable in structural tier (after `. -`); 0.1% coverage |\n| `TRANSLATOR` | Zero hits in 100-record sample; translators absent from title string or in separate metadata fields |\n| `EDITOR` | `(Hg.)` suffix and body-text `bearb.` not reliably captured by SoR-text keyword match; partial detection only |\n\n---\n\n## 3. Era-dependent heuristics\n\n### Pre-1750: early modern titles\n\n| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Long-form title pages** | 42–50% long (>14 tokens); median 12–15 tokens. Full bibliographic description folded into title string — the title page functioned as a table of contents | All ISBD fields may be present but without ISBD punctuation; NER is the primary path |\n| **Author before title** | Author name + credentials appear at the start of the string, before the work title. No ` /` present → `f_person = 0` even when an author is named | Requires name-before-title NER pattern; do not rely on `f_resp_person`. See [silver-label-fp-review.md §5](silver-label-fp-review.md#5-pre-1750-false-negatives--author-before-title) |\n| **Latin titles** | Unknown proportion; Leichenpredigten and pre-Reformation works frequently Latin or Early Modern German | `de_core_news_sm` not optimised for Latin; tokenisation and stopword removal unreliable |\n| **YEAR false positives** | Manuscript dates, death dates, composition dates common | Filter years appearing after `gegr.`, `biß`, `bereit … geschrieben`, `Anno YYYY biß YYYY` |\n\n### 19th–early 20th century: serials and newspapers (dc_type = issue|Heft|Zeitung)\n\n| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |\n|---|---|---|\n| **`Ausgabe vom [date]`** | Ordinal + \"Ausgabe\" + weekday/date is a newspaper issue label, not edition statement | Exclude `f_edition` entirely for serial dc_types |\n| **`= Jg.`, `= Bd.`, `= N.F.`** | `=` introduces enumeration equivalences, not parallel titles | Exclude `f_parallel` for serial dc_types |\n| **Corporate body SoRs** | Government agencies, statistical offices, universities named after ` /` (~19% of ` /` pool) | Sub-classify as `f_resp_corp`; treat as CORPORATE entity, not PERSON |\n\n### Post-2000: digital-born metadata\n\n| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Short titles, separate subtitle fields** | Only 9% short; digital metadata stores subtitle separately — `title` may contain only the main title | ` :` recall drops; OTHER_TITLE may not appear in title string even when a subtitle exists |\n| **Richer structured descriptions** | Median 11 tokens — structured descriptions reappear but in modern format | ISBD `. -` still rare; heuristic rules apply as before |\n\n---\n\n## 4. Field-level summary by dc_type\n\n| dc_type | Reliable fields | Excluded | Special note |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| Monografie (pre-1750) | `f_other_title`, `f_year` (date filter) | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | `f_person` is false negative (author before title); long-form strings |\n| Monografie (post-1800) | All accepted fields | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | Standard rules apply |\n| issue / Heft / Zeitung | `f_other_title`, `f_year`, `f_volume` | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | `f_person` present but sub-classify: mostly `f_resp_corp` |\n| Leichenpredigt | `f_other_title`, `f_year` (date filter) | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | `f_person` is false negative; deceased + husband credentials embedded mid-title |\n| Statistische Berichte | `f_other_title`, `f_year`, `f_volume` | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | `f_person` present → sub-classify as `f_resp_corp` |\n\n---\n\n## 5. Implications for silver label selection\n\n1. **Sub-classify `f_person`** into `f_resp_person`, `f_resp_corp`, `f_resp_editor`, `f_resp_other` before assigning NER labels — do not treat ` /` as synonymous with individual authorship\n2. **Treat `f_resp_corp` as a distinct entity class** (CORPORATE), not a false positive — 19% of ` /` records; maps to MARC corporate name entry\n3. **Always exclude** `f_parallel` and `f_edition` from heuristic silver labels\n4. **Filter `f_year` false positives** using date-context patterns (founding years, life dates, manuscript dates)\n5. **Apply dc_type guards**: exclude `f_edition` and `f_parallel` for serial types; sub-classify `f_person` for institutional dc_types\n6. **Pre-1750 stratum**: annotate author spans from name-before-title pattern separately; do not rely on `f_resp_person`\n7. **Tier 2 (4,613 records)** remains the highest-confidence silver set — structural tier with unambiguous span boundaries\n"
}
```
