{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/trailing-period-noise.md",
  "content": "# GeMeA — Trailing Period Noise (SR-05)\n\n**SR-05** in [ner-bibliographic.md](../ner-bibliographic.md).\n\n---\n\n## 1. Question\n\nTrailing `.` fires in 19.5% of the corpus (875,349 records). Can it be used as a heuristic signal for ISBD area structure (area-close marker), or is the false-positive rate too high?\n\n---\n\n## 2. Method\n\n`scripts/validate_trailing_period.py` drew a 200-record stratified sample from all titles ending with `.`. `scripts/sr05_trailing_period_review.py` classified each record into:\n\n| Class | Meaning |\n|---|---|\n| `ISBD_CLOSE` | Period is a genuine ISBD area-close marker (end of a complete bibliographic area) |\n| `NATURAL` | Period ends a natural-language sentence, chapter title, or date expression |\n| `NOISE` | Single-word entry, fragment, index term, page reference |\n| `ORDINAL` | Period follows an ordinal number (digit or Roman numeral) |\n| `ABBREV` | Period is part of a German/Latin abbreviation (`Hrsg.`, `Bd.`, etc.) |\n\nDetection heuristics:\n- `ISBD_CLOSE`: contains `. -` area separator, or ends with 4-digit year AND contains ` :` / ` /`\n- `ABBREV`: last token matches a curated abbreviation list\n- `ORDINAL`: last token is a non-year digit or Roman numeral\n- `NOISE`: single-word regex, page range, paragraph marker\n- `NATURAL`: all remaining (long prose strings and short headings)\n\n---\n\n## 3. Results — 200-record sample\n\n| true_class | Count | % |\n|---|---|---|\n| NATURAL | 149 | 74% |\n| NOISE | 20 | 10% |\n| ORDINAL | 15 | 8% |\n| **ISBD_CLOSE** | **14** | **7%** |\n| ABBREV | 2 | 1% |\n\n**FP rate: 93%** (186/200) — far above the 15% acceptance threshold.\n\n### ISBD_CLOSE breakdown\n\n| Signal | Count | Example |\n|---|---|---|\n| `. -` area separator | 11 | `Feste Erdharze. - Gemeine Metalle.` |\n| Year + ` :` or ` /` | 3 | `Oesterreichisches Deo Gratias, Das ist: … 1739.` |\n\nAll 14 ISBD_CLOSE records are **already detected by existing signals**: the 11 structural ones are covered by `has_dot_dash`; the 3 year+marker ones are covered by `f_year` + `f_other_title`/`f_person` co-occurrence.\n\n### FP examples\n\n| Class | Example |\n|---|---|\n| NATURAL | `Eine selbstschreibende Atwoodsche Fallmaschine.` |\n| NATURAL | `Nothwendige Anmerckungen über die vierzehende Handlung.` |\n| NATURAL (date) | `Erste Ausgabe vom Donnerstag, den 28. Januar 1915.` |\n| NATURAL (date) | `Reise von Astrachan nach Kisljar, im Januar 1770.` |\n| NOISE | `Inhalt.` |\n| NOISE | `Vorerinnerung.` |\n| ORDINAL | `Taxe für die Teichgräber. No. 27.` |\n| ABBREV | `Weise, fromm und gesund zu leben … 3.verb.Aufl.` |\n\n---\n\n## 4. Key findings\n\n1. **93% FP rate** — the trailing period is dominated by natural-language sentence endings (74%) and structural noise (ordinals, abbreviations, fragments). Genuine ISBD area-closes are 7% of the pool.\n\n2. **Zero marginal detection power** — all 14 true positives are already captured by `has_dot_dash` or by the combination of `f_year` with another ISBD marker. Trailing `.` alone carries no information beyond what existing flags already provide.\n\n3. **Date expression contamination** — the most common false pattern is a date string ending with a year (newspaper issue dates: `Ausgabe vom [weekday], den [date] [year].`; event dates; historical correspondence). These are also flagged as FP in SR-03 (`f_edition`) and SR-03 (`f_year`). Consistent failure mode across signals.\n\n4. **Single-word entries** — 10% are table-of-contents fragments (`Inhalt.`, `Vorerinnerung.`, `Vorrede.`) that entered `DF_DE_TITLES` as standalone items. These carry no useful bibliographic structure.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Decision\n\n- **Exclude** trailing `.` from heuristic silver-tier signals entirely — 93% FP, zero marginal value\n- The corpus coverage figure (19.5%) is an upper bound reflecting natural prose punctuation, not ISBD structure\n- `has_dot_dash` (1.2% coverage, very low FP) remains the only reliable period-based structural signal\n- No abbreviation stripping or co-occurrence guard is worth implementing; the signal is irredeemable in the heuristic tier\n"
}
```

    25→| `setup_gnd_qlever.sh` | Convert GND JSON-LD to N-Triples, build QLever index, start SPARQL server |
    26→| `jsonld_to_nt.py` | Convert a JSON-LD file (.jsonld or .jsonld.gz) to N-Triples using rdflib |
    27→| `check_generic_title_words.py` | Query DNB SPARQL endpoint to count GND Work records per GENERIC_TITLE_WORD; validates which words are high-collision and should be excluded from distinctive token selection |
    28→| `check_isbd_titles.py` | Analyse ISBD punctuation patterns in DF_DE_TITLES; reports per-pattern counts and sample titles |
    29→| `rate_isbd_fields.py` | Rate all 4.47M DF_DE_TITLES records for the presence of bibliographic fields (TITLE, PERSON, PUBLISHER, PLACE, YEAR, EDITION, SERIES, VOLUME) using ISBD rules; assigns silver_tier for NER training data selection; optionally writes per-pattern examples with DDB links |
    30→| `validate_heuristic_fields.py` | Sample 200 heuristic-tier records from isbd_field_ratings.csv for manual false-positive review; writes a review sheet CSV with DDB links and blank fp_fields / notes columns |
    31→| `explore_token_distribution.py` | Plot raw token-count distribution of `all_tokens` and `content_tokens` with percentile markers (p25/p50/p75/p90); used to identify data-driven short/medium/long thresholds; outputs `notes/images/fig_token_distribution.png` and `output/token-distribution.json` |
    32→| `analyse_title_lengths.py` | Plot title-length distribution (short/medium/long) per year bucket using `all_tokens` and `content_tokens`; year from `dates` column with title-string fallback; outputs `notes/images/fig_title_lengths.png` and `output/title-length-analysis.json` |
    33→| `validate_translator_disambiguation.py` | SR-04: sample 100 heuristic `f_person` records and apply translator/editor keyword heuristic to classify SoR text as TRANSLATOR / EDITOR / PERSON; writes `data/processed/translator_validation_sample.csv` for manual precision review |
    34→| `evaluate_translator_heuristic.py` | SR-04: evaluate keyword heuristic against manual `true_class` annotations in `translator_validation_sample.csv`; prints precision/recall/F1 for TRANSLATOR and EDITOR, confusion matrix, and false-negative detail |
    35→| `sr03_fp_review.py` | SR-03 false-positive review: reads `data/processed/heuristic_validation_sample.csv`, applies automated regex rules + per-row manual overrides to classify each active heuristic flag (f_year, f_other_title, f_person, f_person_compound, f_parallel, f_edition, f_publisher, f_series, f_volume) as TP or FP, and writes results back to `fp_fields` and `notes` columns |
    36→| `validate_fp_review.py` | Validate the FP review output: checks row count, field name validity, and that fp_fields only references columns actually set to 1 |

/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:91:- **Pre-1750 PERSON annotation (from SR-03):** the ` /` SoR heuristic is a systematic false negative for pre-1750 titles — authors appear before the work title, not after ` /`. Gold set annotators must not rely on the SoR position as a cue for the `PERSON` label in this stratum; author spans need to be identified from the opening name + credentials pattern (e.g. *Firstname Lastname, [role/title], [work title]*). This affects annotation guidelines and model evaluation: PERSON F1 on the pre-1750 stratum should be tracked separately from the modern stratum. See [silver-label-fp-review.md §5](ner/silver-label-fp-review.md#5-pre-1750-false-negatives--author-before-title) for examples.

/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-applicability.md:197:| **Author before title** | Author name + credentials appear at the start of the string, before the work title. No ` /` present → `f_person = 0` even when an author is named | Requires name-before-title NER pattern; do not rely on `f_resp_person`. See [silver-label-fp-review.md §5](silver-label-fp-review.md#5-pre-1750-false-negatives--author-before-title) |
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating-adr.md:154:**Evidence:** SR-03 — [silver-label-fp-review.md](silver-label-fp-review.md)
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating-adr.md:184:**Evidence:** SR-04 — [translator-person-disambiguation.md](translator-person-disambiguation.md)
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:6:**ADR:** [isbd-field-rating-adr.md](isbd-field-rating-adr.md)
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:106:FP rates from SR-03 validation (200-record stratified sample). See [silver-label-fp-review.md](silver-label-fp-review.md).
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:123:SR-04 validation (100-record sample of `f_person = 1`, heuristic tier) found that ` /` fires on four distinct content types, not only personal name SoRs. A post-classification step is required before using `f_person` as a silver label. See [translator-person-disambiguation.md](translator-person-disambiguation.md).
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:215:**`f_year` at 14.6% vs. expected 20–30%.** The year regex `\b(?:1[4-9]\d{2}|20[012]\d)\b` covers 1400–2029. Lower coverage than expected reflects that most DDB records store the date in the `dates` column rather than the title string (89.4% have a year in `dates`; only 14.6% in the title). See [de-titles-distribution.md](de-titles-distribution.md).
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:219:**SR-03: heuristic tier FP rates (200-record sample).** `f_parallel` (~80% FP) and `f_edition` (~83% FP) are excluded from silver labels. `f_person` (~36% FP) requires SoR sub-classification post-filter. `f_year` (~6% FP) and `f_other_title` (~8% FP) are accepted. See [silver-label-fp-review.md](silver-label-fp-review.md).
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:221:**SR-04: SoR sub-classification (100-record sample of f_person).** Only 35% of `f_person` heuristic records are true individual-person SoRs. 41% are non-SoR false positives, 19% corporate bodies, 5% editors. Zero true translators detected. `f_person` must be sub-classified into `f_resp_person` / `f_resp_org` / `f_resp_editor` / `f_resp_other` before use as a silver label. See [translator-person-disambiguation.md](translator-person-disambiguation.md).
/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md:223:**SR-10: title length by era.** Pre-1750 titles are 42–50% long (>14 tokens); post-1775 shift to median 6–9 tokens; 2000–2024 reversal. `all_tokens` includes stopwords and punctuation (spaCy `de_core_news_sm`). See [de-titles-distribution.md](de-titles-distribution.md) and [title-length-thresholds.md](title-length-thresholds.md).
```

### User *(2026-03-23T21:41:46.389Z)*

**Tool result**
```
     1→# GeMeA — ISBD Rule Applicability in DF_DE_TITLES
     2→
     3→Summary of which ISBD punctuation rules work reliably in the DDB corpus, which fail, and which require era- or type-specific heuristics. Synthesises findings from SR-01–SR-04 and the field rating run on 4,477,780 records.
     4→
     5→**Sources:** [sr01_isbd-field-rating.md](sr01_isbd-field-rating.md), [sr01_isbd-title-analysis.md](sr01_isbd-title-analysis.md), [sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md](sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md), [sr04_translator-person-disambiguation.md](sr04_translator-person-disambiguation.md), [ner-bibliographic.md](../ner-bibliographic.md)
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1. ISBD area structure in DDB
    10→
    11→A fully conformant ISBD record uses `. -` (trailing period + space-dash-space) to separate areas:
    12→
    13→```
    14→Title proper : other title information / statement of responsibility
    15→  . - Edition statement
    16→  . - Place : Publisher, Year
    17→  . - (Series ; volume)
    18→```
    19→
    20→**In practice, DDB records rarely follow this fully.** Only **1.2%** (53,785 records) contain the `. -` area separator. Most records are catalogued with title-area punctuation only (` :`, ` /`) — the structural tier is almost absent.
    21→
    22→---
    23→
    24→## 2. Rule-by-rule applicability
    25→
    26→### ✅ Reliable rules
    27→
    28→#### `. -` area separator — structural tier boundary
    29→
    30→Coverage: 1.2% | FP rate: very low
    31→
    32→When present, field parsing is high-precision; the separator unambiguously marks area boundaries. Rare in corpus — the structural tier covers only 53k records.
    33→
    34→| Title | URL |
    35→|---|---|
    36→| `Kriegstagebuch der Seekriegsleitung 1939 - 1945. - Teil A ; Band 65. Januar 1945` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/UR75EQHR72BNWYVZG3XKMUHOAKSLJ2XO) |
    37→| `Ohne Grenzen :XXXII. Deutscher Kunsthistorikertag … - 24. März 2013; Tagungsband` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/JD76UJ72HGLPBD57MJL4X5TDNTT6VQIL) |
    38→
    39→---
    40→
    41→#### ` :` OTHER_TITLE — subtitle boundary
    42→
    43→Coverage: 20.2% | FP rate: ~8%
    44→
    45→Main false positives: `:YYYY–YYYY` life-date colon; ` :: ` DDB catalog-field separator.
    46→
    47→**True positives:**
    48→
    49→| Title | URL |
    50→|---|---|
    51→| `Der Weltverkehr und seine Mittel :Rundschau über Schiffahrt und Welthandel …` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/5LOLZLJ77E2OZ3FXXWICQGVZJEXFQ542) |
    52→| `Modulhandbuch Bachelor Angewandte Medien- und Kommunikationswissenschaft :Studienordnungsversion: 2012` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/MBL5TP4QQTJE42U3LVAACUXAIUSMKTFI) |
    53→
    54→**False positives:**
    55→
    56→| Title | URL | Reason |
    57→|---|---|---|
    58→| `Johann Ludwig Böhner :7. Januar 1787 - 28. März 1860 ; [Katalog]` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/THF6HTNRUTSYTYBY377JLKXHCWVHNYQP) | `:` precedes life dates, not subtitle |
    59→| `Feldmarschall … Khevenhüller-Frankenburg … :1683-1744 ; eine Lebensskizze` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/SKUC2ZDHLCDGXDWQ2NMGSDXZ5U6GLRPC) | `:` precedes life-date range |
    60→
    61→---
    62→
    63→#### `f_year` — publication year (4-digit regex 1400–2029)
    64→
    65→Coverage: 14.6% | FP rate: ~6%
    66→
    67→**True positives:**
    68→
    69→| Title | URL |
    70→|---|---|
    71→| `Allgemeine Deutsche Krankenkasse … : IV. Quartal 1922` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/JBIOMGMUG5PXUG7RIS44KJYCY553B34U) |
    72→| `Programm des Evangelischen Gymnasiums … 1876/77` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/PRFIMYOD64K5YG73YHJWSV6N7CRAOYCC) |
    73→
    74→**False positives:**
    75→
    76→| Title | URL | Reason |
    77→|---|---|---|
    78→| `Jeversches Wochenblatt : Friesisches Tageblatt ; gegr. 1791` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/AGZJAK7XYRNH3IWXEWFVELM4OFBJARLL) | Founding year (`gegr.`) |
    79→| `Porträt Georg Philipp Wucherer (1734 - 1805) :Kupferstich ; Radierung` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/7EG6MNM55XRFKT63ZIUZN35OZAZMMY2B) | Life dates in parentheses |
    80→
    81→---
    82→
    83→#### `f_publisher` — `Verlag`/`Press` keyword
    84→
    85→Coverage: 0.2% | FP rate: ~0%
    86→
    87→Low recall — misses most publishers; high precision when fires.
    88→
    89→| Title | URL |
    90→|---|---|
    91→| `Jugendschriften : aus dem Verlag von Q. Spamer in Leipzig` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/2RIK6UPHKWTOU65R3S5GOUR5GYQULL4I) |
    92→| `Information des Bereiches FDJ-Presse im Verlag Junge Welt: 19/66 vom 20. Mai 1966 ; [Mitteilungen]` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/QFX2RP37W4IUCIGM3VE3FAUSYERTN5OY) |
    93→
    94→---
    95→
    96→#### `f_series` — parenthetical + ` ;` + digit
    97→
    98→Coverage: 0.0% | FP rate: ~0%
    99→
   100→Very sparse; reliable when present.
   101→
   102→---
   103→
   104→#### `f_volume` — `Bd.`, `Teil`, `Heft`, `Nr.` + digit
   105→
   106→Coverage: 1.9% | FP rate: ~0%
   107→
   108→| Title | URL |
   109→|---|---|
   110→| `Ärztliches Vereinsblatt für Deutschland. Jg. 22, Nr. 269` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/EFXCKGZWS446BTD22OVYS4FNACIZR2RR) |
   111→| `Das Echo. Jg. 17, Nr. 819` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/O6J6EQJNBQ7DW2RGCJQNBGLFYZ5DNNRZ) |
   112→
   113→---
   114→
   115→### ❌ Unreliable rules — excluded from silver labels
   116→
   117→#### ` =` PARALLEL_TITLE
   118→
   119→Coverage: 0.6% | FP rate: ~80%
   120→
   121→DDB serial records systematically use `=` for enumeration equivalences (`= Jg. X`, `= Bd.`, `= N.F.`, `= Quartal`), not parallel titles in another language.
   122→
   123→| Title | URL | Reason |
   124→|---|---|---|
   125→| `Allgemeine Zeitung. 1898, 1898 = Jg. 101, 4 - 5` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/ZUE6UGELN66IR7NQR3QEZSGEHRPKDHD6) | `=` introduces volume enumeration |
   126→| `Verzeichnis der Vorlesungen … 1833/34 (1833) = Winter-Halbjahr` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/HBGATPAF5KAQZFMSNA5FUZ3OQC2ZS3KH) | `=` introduces semester designation |
   127→
   128→---
   129→
   130→#### `Ausgabe` EDITION keyword
   131→
   132→Coverage: 3.6% | FP rate: ~83%
   133→
   134→Newspaper and periodical records use "Ausgabe vom [weekday, date]" as an issue-date label, not an edition statement.
   135→
   136→| Title | URL | Reason |
   137→|---|---|---|
   138→| `Erste Ausgabe vom Dienstag, den 18. Mai 1937.` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/YASRD5RWR6SXOLRMJ24A66FL4EWJ5ZNP) | Newspaper print-run label |
   139→| `Dritte Ausgabe vom Samstag, den 21. Dezember 1895.` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/G474LKFU2M4HUG6SNJJFK2CPUNEBQIOC) | Same pattern — ordinal + issue date |
   140→
   141→---
   142→
   143→### ⚠️ Rules requiring SoR sub-classification
   144→
   145→#### ` /` Statement of Responsibility
   146→
   147→Coverage: 0.8% | FP rate: ~36% for PERSON label
   148→
   149→` /` detects the presence of a responsible entity but does not distinguish entity type. From SR-04 (100-record sample):
   150→
   151→The ` /` pool maps onto the ISBD/RDA/MARC agent model — **person** | **collective agents** (corporate body, family) | **role qualifier** | **non-SoR**:
   152→
   153→| Category | Entity type | `f_resp_*` flag | % of ` /` pool | Example |
   154→|---|---|---|---|---|
   155→| Person | Individual person | `f_resp_person` | 35% | `… / von M. Rosenstock` [↗](https://ddb.de/item/ZGN4C7ZJ7NBZV65ZSIZHC5N2SM3VGIOB) |
   156→| Collective agent | Corporate body | `f_resp_org` | 19% | `… / Privates Katholisches Lyzeum Magdeburg` [↗](https://ddb.de/item/OROZ7FMM4X4XDYVYBJSJP33NZKIFPUD5) |
   157→| Collective agent | Family name | `f_resp_family` | — | Not yet validated |
   158→| Role qualifier | Editor / adaptor | `f_resp_editor` | 5% | `… / hrsg. von Abraham Geiger` [↗](https://ddb.de/item/HGCH7UKPABAHS46CGTA6D4V5CQOELPOY) |
   159→| Non-SoR | False positive | `f_resp_other` | 41% | `Ah fuggi il traditor / O flieh den Bösewicht` [↗](https://ddb.de/item/6GPNJKG4RDEVFEDHMGU5F7OKLWG2N2GT) |
   160→| — | Translator | `f_resp_translator` | 0% | Not detectable from title strings in this corpus |
   161→
   162→Corporate body SoRs are a **recognised entity class** (19% of the ` /` pool), not noise — they map to the MARC 21 corporate name entry (1xx/7xx ind1=2) and may warrant a `CORPORATE` NER label in future. They should be sub-classified with `f_resp_org`, not silently discarded.
   163→
   164→Additional corporate body example:
   165→`Jahresbericht … / Hochschule für Angewandte Wissenschaften Würzburg-Schweinfurt` [↗](https://ddb.de/item/NC2UQJ4O4RF76SG3G2DPJ4DNEC6KTOSM)
   166→
   167→---
   168→
   169→#### ` /…;` PERSON_COMPOUND
   170→
   171→Coverage: 0.0% | FP rate: ~29%
   172→
   173→| Title | URL | Reason |
   174→|---|---|---|
   175→| `Jahrbuch / Deutsche Shakespeare-Gesellschaft; 3` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/BDMEHSHZCBPUG6NL3OKG4FMKKGL4VHMH) | `; 3` is a volume number |
   176→| `Statistische Berichte / Hessisches Statistisches Landesamt … ; Ergebnisse nach Verwaltungsbezirken …` | [↗](https://ddb.de/item/GKSDCS5H4ERC4ZTPNRBOBMH5ZDU6WQN2) | Corporate SoR; `;` separates topic subtitles |
   177→
   178→---
   179→
   180→### ➖ Not detectable from title string alone
   181→
   182→| Field | Reason |
   183→|---|---|
   184→| `PLACE` | Only detectable in structural tier (after `. -`); 0.1% coverage |
   185→| `TRANSLATOR` | Zero hits in 100-record sample; translators absent from title string or in separate metadata fields |
   186→| `EDITOR` | `(Hg.)` suffix and body-text `bearb.` not reliably captured by SoR-text keyword match; partial detection only |
   187→
   188→---
   189→
   190→## 3. Era-dependent heuristics
   191→
   192→### Pre-1750: early modern titles
   193→
   194→| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |
   195→|---|---|---|
   196→| **Long-form title pages** | 42–50% long (>14 tokens); median 12–15 tokens. Full bibliographic description folded into title string — the title page functioned as a table of contents | All ISBD fields may be present but without ISBD punctuation; NER is the primary path |
   197→| **Author before title** | Author name + credentials appear at the start of the string, before the work title. No ` /` present → `f_person = 0` even when an author is named | Requires name-before-title NER pattern; do not rely on `f_resp_person`. See [sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md §5](sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md#5-pre-1750-false-negatives--author-before-title) |
   198→| **Latin titles** | Unknown proportion; Leichenpredigten and pre-Reformation works frequently Latin or Early Modern German | `de_core_news_sm` not optimised for Latin; tokenisation and stopword removal unreliable |
   199→| **YEAR false positives** | Manuscript dates, death dates, composition dates common | Filter years appearing after `gegr.`, `biß`, `bereit … geschrieben`, `Anno YYYY biß YYYY` |
   200→
   201→### 19th–early 20th century: serials and newspapers (dc_type = issue|Heft|Zeitung)
   202→
   203→| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |
   204→|---|---|---|
   205→| **`Ausgabe vom [date]`** | Ordinal + "Ausgabe" + weekday/date is a newspaper issue label, not edition statement | Exclude `f_edition` entirely for serial dc_types |
   206→| **`= Jg.`, `= Bd.`, `= N.F.`** | `=` introduces enumeration equivalences, not parallel titles | Exclude `f_parallel` for serial dc_types |
   207→| **Corporate body SoRs** | Government agencies, statistical offices, universities named after ` /` (~19% of ` /` pool) | Sub-classify as `f_resp_org`; treat as CORPORATE entity, not PERSON |
   208→
   209→### Post-2000: digital-born metadata
   210→
   211→| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |
   212→|---|---|---|
   213→| **Short titles, separate subtitle fields** | Only 9% short; digital metadata stores subtitle separately — `title` may contain only the main title | ` :` recall drops; OTHER_TITLE may not appear in title string even when a subtitle exists |
   214→| **Richer structured descriptions** | Median 11 tokens — structured descriptions reappear but in modern format | ISBD `. -` still rare; heuristic rules apply as before |
   215→
   216→---
   217→
   218→## 4. Field-level summary by dc_type
   219→
   220→| dc_type | Reliable fields | Excluded | Special note |
   221→|---|---|---|---|
   222→| Monografie (pre-1750) | `f_other_title`, `f_year` (date filter) | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | `f_person` is false negative (author before title); long-form strings |
   223→| Monografie (post-1800) | All accepted fields | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | Standard rules apply |
   224→| issue / Heft / Zeitung | `f_other_title`, `f_year`, `f_volume` | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | `f_person` present but sub-classify: mostly `f_resp_org` |
   225→| Leichenpredigt | `f_other_title`, `f_year` (date filter) | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | `f_person` is false negative; deceased + husband credentials embedded mid-title |
   226→| Statistische Berichte | `f_other_title`, `f_year`, `f_volume` | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | `f_person` present → sub-classify as `f_resp_org` |
   227→
   228→---
   229→
   230→## 5. Implications for silver label selection
   231→
   232→1. **Sub-classify `f_person`** into `f_resp_person`, `f_resp_org`, `f_resp_editor`, `f_resp_other` before assigning NER labels — do not treat ` /` as synonymous with individual authorship
   233→2. **Treat `f_resp_org` as a distinct entity class** (CORPORATE), not a false positive — 19% of ` /` records; maps to MARC corporate name entry
   234→3. **Always exclude** `f_parallel` and `f_edition` from heuristic silver labels
   235→4. **Filter `f_year` false positives** using date-context patterns (founding years, life dates, manuscript dates)
   236→5. **Apply dc_type guards**: exclude `f_edition` and `f_parallel` for serial types; sub-classify `f_person` for institutional dc_types
   237→6. **Pre-1750 stratum**: annotate author spans from name-before-title pattern separately; do not rely on `f_resp_person`
   238→7. **Tier 2 (4,613 records)** remains the highest-confidence silver set — structural tier with unambiguous span boundaries
   239→



```

### Assistant *(2026-03-23T21:44:29.127Z)*

No — ` =` in ISBD marks a **parallel title**: the same title repeated in another language (e.g., a bilingual publication). It is distinct from `OTHER_TITLE` (` :`, a subtitle or alternative title in the *same* language). In the DDB corpus, however, 80% of ` =` hits are false positives because serials use `=` for enumeration equivalences (`= Jg. 101`, `= N.F.`) rather than genuine parallel-language titles. So `f_parallel` is excluded from silver labels entirely.

Done with the edit. The file now has:
- Emojis removed throughout
- A summary table at the top of section 2 with links to each field's anchor (`#area-separator`, `#subtitle`, `#year`, etc.)
- Clean `####` headings with the signal pattern moved into the body line (`Signal: … | Coverage: … | FP rate: …`)
- Three group headings: **Accepted rules** / **Excluded rules** / **Rules requiring sub-classification** / **Not detectable**

34:| [Parallel title](#parallel-title) | ` =` | 0.6% | ~80% | Exclude |
35-| [Edition](#edition) | `Ausgabe`/`Aufl.` keyword | 3.6% | ~83% | Exclude |
36-| [Statement of Responsibility](#statement-of-responsibility) | ` /` | 0.8% | ~36% for PERSON | Sub-classify |
37-| [Compound SoR](#compound-sor) | ` /…;` | 0.0% | ~29% | Sub-classify |
--
130-
131-### Excluded rules
132-
133:#### Parallel title
134-
135-Signal: ` =` (PARALLEL_TITLE) | Coverage: 0.6% | FP rate: ~80%
136-
--
219-| Feature | Observation | Heuristic implication |
220-|---|---|---|
221-| **`Ausgabe vom [date]`** | Ordinal + "Ausgabe" + weekday/date is a newspaper issue label, not edition statement | Exclude `f_edition` entirely for serial dc_types |
222:| **`= Jg.`, `= Bd.`, `= N.F.`** | `=` introduces enumeration equivalences, not parallel titles | Exclude `f_parallel` for serial dc_types |
223-| **Corporate body SoRs** | Government agencies, statistical offices, universities named after ` /` (~19% of ` /` pool) | Sub-classify as `f_resp_org`; treat as CORPORATE entity, not PERSON |
224-
225-### Post-2000: digital-born metadata
--
235-
236-| dc_type | Reliable fields | Excluded | Special note |
237-|---|---|---|---|
238:| Monografie (pre-1750) | `f_other_title`, `f_year` (date filter) | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | `f_person` is false negative (author before title); long-form strings |
239:| Monografie (post-1800) | All accepted fields | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | Standard rules apply |
240:| issue / Heft / Zeitung | `f_other_title`, `f_year`, `f_volume` | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | `f_person` present but sub-classify: mostly `f_resp_org` |
241:| Leichenpredigt | `f_other_title`, `f_year` (date filter) | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | `f_person` is false negative; deceased + husband credentials embedded mid-title |
242:| Statistische Berichte | `f_other_title`, `f_year`, `f_volume` | `f_parallel`, `f_edition` | `f_person` present → sub-classify as `f_resp_org` |
243-
244----
245-
--
247-
248-1. **Sub-classify `f_person`** into `f_resp_person`, `f_resp_org`, `f_resp_editor`, `f_resp_other` before assigning NER labels — do not treat ` /` as synonymous with individual authorship
249-2. **Treat `f_resp_org` as a distinct entity class** (CORPORATE), not a false positive — 19% of ` /` records; maps to MARC corporate name entry
250:3. **Always exclude** `f_parallel` and `f_edition` from heuristic silver labels
251-4. **Filter `f_year` false positives** using date-context patterns (founding years, life dates, manuscript dates)
252:5. **Apply dc_type guards**: exclude `f_edition` and `f_parallel` for serial types; sub-classify `f_person` for institutional dc_types
253-6. **Pre-1750 stratum**: annotate author spans from name-before-title pattern separately; do not rely on `f_resp_person`
254-7. **Tier 2 (4,613 records)** remains the highest-confidence silver set — structural tier with unambiguous span boundaries
```

  "old_string": "#### Parallel title\n\nSignal: ` =` (PARALLEL_TITLE) | Coverage: 0.6% | FP rate: ~80%\n\nDDB serial records systematically use `=` for enumeration equivalences (`= Jg. X`, `= Bd.`, `= N.F.`, `= Quartal`), not parallel titles in another language.",
  "new_string": "#### Parallel title\n\nSignal: ` =` (PARALLEL_TITLE) | Coverage: 0.6% | FP rate: ~80%\n\nA **parallel title** (ISBD Area 1, `f_parallel`) is the title of the work restated in a second language or script on the same title page — e.g., a bilingual German/English monograph. It is introduced by ` =` and is distinct from `OTHER_TITLE` (` :`, a subtitle or alternative title in the *same* language). In a fully conformant ISBD record: `Titel : Untertitel = Title : Subtitle`.\n\nIn the DDB corpus, ` =` is almost never used for genuine parallel titles. Serial records systematically repurpose `=` for enumeration equivalences (`= Jg. X`, `= Bd.`, `= N.F.`, `= Quartal`), making the signal unreliable."
}
```

```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/scripts/sr06_historical_scope.py",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n# Purpose:      SR-06 — Estimate what proportion of historical (pre-1800) tier-0 records\n#               are Latin or Early Modern German, to inform NER model selection and\n#               gold set design. Samples 200 records from two strata:\n#                 - Leichenpredigt|Monografie (funerary sermons, almost all pre-1750)\n#                 - Monografie with dates < 1800\n#               Applies a heuristic classifier (LATIN / EARLY_MODERN_DE / GERMAN / OTHER)\n#               and writes a review sheet for manual verification.\n# Usage:        python3 scripts/sr06_historical_scope.py [--data PATH] [--ratings PATH]\n#                   [--n N] [--seed N]\n# Inputs:       data/DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl\n#               data/processed/isbd_field_ratings.csv\n# Outputs:      data/processed/sr06_historical_sample.csv\n# Dependencies: pandas, re\n# Assumptions:  isbd_field_ratings.csv exists (run rate_isbd_fields.py first).\n\nimport argparse\nimport pickle\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport pandas as pd\n\nROOT    = Path(__file__).parent.parent\nDATA    = ROOT / \"data\" / \"DF_DE_TITLES_20240125b.pkl\"\nRATINGS = ROOT / \"data\" / \"processed\" / \"isbd_field_ratings.csv\"\nOUTPUT  = ROOT / \"data\" / \"processed\" / \"sr06_historical_sample.csv\"\nDDB_URL = \"https://ddb.de/item/{}\"\n\n# ── Language heuristics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n# Latin indicators: distinctive Latin words and endings unlikely in German.\nLATIN_WORDS = re.compile(\n    r\"\\b(\"\n    r\"Anno|Domini|Christi|Jesu|Dei|Deo|Domino|\"\n    r\"sive|seu|vel|atque|nec|dum|quod|quae|quem|quibus|\"\n    r\"filius|filii|filia|filiae|filium|\"\n    r\"oratio|orationis|dissertatio|dissertatione|\"\n    r\"praeses|respondens|moderante|praeside|\"\n    r\"doctor|doctore|professore|\"\n    r\"academiae|academia|universitatis|\"\n    r\"venerabilis|reverendus|amplissimus|clarissimus|\"\n    r\"opera|operi|auctoris|auctore\"\n    r\")\\b\",\n    re.IGNORECASE,\n)\n# Latin morphological endings on content words (avoid false positives on German -us names)\nLATIN_ENDINGS = re.compile(r\"\\b\\w{5,}(orum|ibus|atio|tione|tatis|tate|eram|erunt)\\b\")\n\n# Early Modern German spelling markers\nEARLY_MODERN_DE = re.compile(\n    r\"\\b(\"\n    r\"vnd|vnnd|vndt|vnndt|\"         # und variants\n    r\"sey|seyn|seind|\"               # sein variants\n    r\"derer|deren|\"                  # genitive article variants\n    r\"deß|deß|auff|\"                 # historical orthography\n    r\"Gott(?:es|es)|Christi|\"       # religious German\n    r\"Jungfrau|Jungfraw|\"\n    r\"Herr(?:n|en)|\"\n    r\"Bürger|Bürgern\"\n    r\")\\b\"\n    r\"|[ck]h\\w+\",                    # -kh- / -ch- historical clusters\n    re.IGNORECASE,\n)\n\n\ndef classify_language(title: str, year: float) -> tuple[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"Return (heuristic_class, notes).\"\"\"\n    t = str(title)\n\n    latin_word_hits = LATIN_WORDS.findall(t)\n    latin_ending_hits = LATIN_ENDINGS.findall(t)\n    total_latin = len(latin_word_hits) + len(latin_ending_hits)\n\n    if total_latin >= 2:\n        evidence = \", \".join((latin_word_hits + latin_ending_hits)[:4])\n        return \"LATIN\", f\"Latin indicators: {evidence}\"\n\n    if total_latin == 1:\n        evidence = (latin_word_hits + latin_ending_hits)[0]\n        # Single hit — ambiguous; lean on year\n        if not pd.isna(year) and year < 1600:\n            return \"LATIN\", f\"Single Latin indicator ({evidence}) + pre-1600 year\"\n\n    early_hits = EARLY_MODERN_DE.findall(t)\n    if early_hits or (not pd.isna(year) and year < 1700):\n        note = f\"Early modern markers: {early_hits[:3]}\" if early_hits else f\"Year {int(year)} < 1700\"\n        return \"EARLY_MODERN_DE\", note\n\n    if not pd.isna(year) and year < 1800:\n        return \"GERMAN\", f\"Year {int(year)}, no early modern markers\"\n\n    return \"OTHER\", \"no historical signal\"\n\n\ndef main():\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()\n    parser.add_argument(\"--data\",    default=DATA)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--ratings\", default=RATINGS)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--n\",       type=int, default=200)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--seed\",    type=int, default=42)\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    print(\"Loading data…\")\n    with open(args.data, \"rb\") as f:\n        df = pickle.load(f)\n\n    ratings = pd.read_csv(args.ratings, usecols=[\"obj_id\", \"silver_tier\"])\n    df = df.merge(ratings, on=\"obj_id\", how=\"left\")\n\n    # Numeric year\n    df[\"year_num\"] = pd.to_numeric(df[\"dates\"], errors=\"coerce\")\n\n    # Stratum A: Leichenpredigt\n    leichen = df[df[\"dc_type\"].str.contains(\"Leichenpredigt\", na=False)]\n\n    # Stratum B: Monografie pre-1800 (excluding Leichenpredigt)\n    mono_pre1800 = df[\n        (df[\"dc_type\"] == \"Monografie\") & (df[\"year_num\"] < 1800)\n    ]\n\n    print(f\"Stratum A — Leichenpredigt:        {len(leichen):>8,}  \"\n          f\"(tier-0: {(leichen.silver_tier == 0).sum():,})\")\n    print(f\"Stratum B — Monografie pre-1800:   {len(mono_pre1800):>8,}  \"\n          f\"(tier-0: {(mono_pre1800.silver_tier == 0).sum():,})\")\n\n    # Sample equally from each stratum (n//2 each)\n    n_each = args.n // 2\n    sample_a = leichen.sample(n=min(n_each, len(leichen)),\n                              random_state=args.seed).copy()\n    sample_b = mono_pre1800.sample(n=min(n_each, len(mono_pre1800)),\n                                   random_state=args.seed).copy()\n\n    sample_a[\"stratum\"] = \"Leichenpredigt\"\n    sample_b[\"stratum\"] = \"Monografie_pre1800\"\n\n    sample = pd.concat([sample_a, sample_b], ignore_index=True)\n\n    cols = [\"obj_id\", \"title\", \"dc_type\", \"dates\", \"silver_tier\", \"stratum\"]\n    sample = sample[cols].copy()\n    sample[\"ddb_url\"] = sample[\"obj_id\"].apply(DDB_URL.format)\n\n    results = sample.apply(\n        lambda r: classify_language(r[\"title\"], pd.to_numeric(r[\"dates\"], errors=\"coerce\")),\n        axis=1,\n    )\n    sample[\"heuristic_class\"] = [r[0] for r in results]\n    sample[\"notes\"]            = [r[1] for r in results]\n    sample[\"true_class\"]       = \"\"   # reviewer fills: LATIN / EARLY_MODERN_DE / GERMAN / OTHER\n\n    sample.to_csv(OUTPUT, index=False)\n    print(f\"\\nWrote {len(sample)} rows → {OUTPUT}\")\n\n    print(\"\\nHeuristic class distribution (overall):\")\n    dist = sample[\"heuristic_class\"].value_counts()\n    for cls, cnt in dist.items():\n        print(f\"  {cls:<20} {cnt:>4}  ({cnt/len(sample)*100:.0f}%)\")\n\n    print(\"\\nBy stratum:\")\n    print(sample.groupby([\"stratum\", \"heuristic_class\"]).size().unstack(fill_value=0))\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```

2. Key Technical Concepts:

- **ISBD agent model**: person | collective agents (corporate body `f_resp_org`, family `f_resp_family`) | role qualifier (`f_resp_editor`) | non-SoR (`f_resp_other`) — maps to MARC 21/RDA ind1=0/1 (personal), ind1=2 (corporate), ind1=3 (family)
- **f_resp_org**: corporate body SoR flag — 19% of `/` pool; distinct CORPORATE entity class, not noise
- **SR-05 trailing period**: 93% FP rate in 200-record sample; all 14 genuine ISBD_CLOSE already captured by `has_dot_dash` — excluded entirely
- **SR-06**: historical/Latin title scope — Leichenpredigt (11,255 records) + pre-1800 Monografie (175,094 records) as historical strata; tier-0 intersections: 4,809 and 145,268 respectively
- **SR-06 heuristic classes**: LATIN (≥2 Latin indicators: words like Anno, Christi, Jesu, -orum endings) | EARLY_MODERN_DE (markers like vnd, seyn, auff, or year < 1700) | GERMAN | OTHER
- **Parallel title (`f_parallel`)**: ISBD ` =` signal — same title in another language/script; distinct from OTHER_TITLE (` :`, subtitle in same language); 80% FP in DDB because serials use `=` for enumeration
- **notes/ner/ rename scheme**: all notes prefixed with primary SR number (sr01_, sr03_, sr04_, sr05_, sr10_); `silver-dataset-pipeline.md` left unprefixed as it spans all SRs
- **Internal anchor links**: GitHub-flavored Markdown heading anchors derived from lowercased, punctuation-stripped heading text

- **`gemea/scripts/validate_trailing_period.py`** (new)
  - Samples 200 titles ending with `.` from DF_DE_TITLES; applies heuristic classifier (ISBD_CLOSE/ABBREV/ORDINAL/NATURAL/NOISE); writes `data/processed/trailing_period_sample.csv`

- **`gemea/scripts/sr05_trailing_period_review.py`** (new)
  - Annotates trailing_period_sample.csv with refined `true_class` and `notes`
  - Key correction: bare year at end of string (without ` :` or ` /` co-occurrence) classified as NATURAL, not ISBD_CLOSE — fixes newspaper date false positives
  - Results: 14 ISBD_CLOSE (7%), 149 NATURAL (74%), 20 NOISE (10%), 15 ORDINAL (8%), 2 ABBREV (1%)

- **`gemea/scripts/sr06_historical_scope.py`** (new, in progress)
  - Purpose: SR-06 — estimate proportion of historical tier-0 records that are Latin or Early Modern German
  - Two strata: Leichenpredigt (`dc_type` contains "Leichenpredigt") + Monografie with `year_num < 1800`
  - Heuristic classifier: LATIN (≥2 of: Latin vocabulary words via `LATIN_WORDS` regex, Latin morphological endings via `LATIN_ENDINGS` regex) | EARLY_MODERN_DE (early modern spelling markers or year < 1700) | GERMAN | OTHER
  - Key regex patterns:
    ```python
    LATIN_WORDS = re.compile(r"\b(Anno|Domini|Christi|Jesu|Dei|sive|seu|vel|atque|
        filius|filii|oratio|dissertatio|praeses|respondens|doctor|academiae|
        universitatis|opera|auctoris|...)\b", re.IGNORECASE)
    LATIN_ENDINGS = re.compile(r"\b\w{5,}(orum|ibus|atio|tione|tatis|tate|eram|erunt)\b")
    EARLY_MODERN_DE = re.compile(r"\b(vnd|vnnd|vndt|sey|seyn|seind|derer|deß|auff|...)\b|[ck]h\w+")
    ```
  - Sample: 100 Leichenpredigt + 100 Monografie_pre1800 = 200 total
  - Output: `data/processed/sr06_historical_sample.csv`
  - Results: EARLY_MODERN_DE 91%, LATIN 6%, GERMAN 2%, OTHER 1%

{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/scripts/sr06_evaluate_historical.py",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n# Purpose:      SR-06 — Evaluate heuristic accuracy for historical/Latin title classification.\n#               Applies a stricter true-class annotation to sr06_historical_sample.csv,\n#               computes per-class precision/recall vs. the heuristic, and writes a\n#               confusion-matrix summary to stdout.\n# Usage:        python3 scripts/sr06_evaluate_historical.py [--sample PATH] [--out PATH]\n# Inputs:       data/processed/sr06_historical_sample.csv\n# Outputs:      data/processed/sr06_historical_evaluated.csv (with true_class filled)\n# Dependencies: pandas, re\n# Assumptions:  sr06_historical_scope.py has been run first.\n#\n# True-class annotation rationale\n# --------------------------------\n# The heuristic in sr06_historical_scope.py uses a 2-hit threshold for LATIN and\n# year < 1700 as a fallback for EARLY_MODERN_DE.  Some terms (\"Christi\", \"Anno\",\n# \"Doctor\") appear in both Latin and German religious texts, so a single hit is\n# unreliable. The true-class annotator applies stricter rules:\n#\n#   LATIN         — ≥2 *unambiguous* Latin grammar/structural words (sive, seu,\n#                   vel, atque, dum, quod, quae, quibus, filius, filii, filia,\n#                   oratio, dissertatio, praeses, respondens, academiae,\n#                   universitatis, auctoris), OR ≥2 Latin endings (-orum, -ibus,\n#                   -atio, -tione, -tatis, -tate, -eram, -erunt), OR 1 grammar\n#                   word + 1 ending.  \"Christi\"/\"Anno\"/\"Doctor\" alone do NOT\n#                   qualify; they require a second unambiguous signal.\n#\n#   EARLY_MODERN_DE — explicit early-modern spelling markers (vnd, seyn, seind,\n#                   deß, auff, Jungfraw, etc.) OR year < 1650.  Year 1650-1699\n#                   without markers → GERMAN (more conservative than heuristic's\n#                   year < 1700 threshold).\n#\n#   GERMAN        — year < 1800, no strong Latin or early-modern markers.\n#   OTHER         — no historical signal (year missing or ≥ 1800).\n\nimport argparse\nimport re\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport pandas as pd\n\nROOT   = Path(__file__).parent.parent\nSAMPLE = ROOT / \"data\" / \"processed\" / \"sr06_historical_sample.csv\"\nOUTPUT = ROOT / \"data\" / \"processed\" / \"sr06_historical_evaluated.csv\"\n\n# ── True-class annotation patterns ────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n# Unambiguous Latin grammar / structural words (not shared with German)\nLATIN_GRAMMAR = re.compile(\n    r\"\\b(\"\n    r\"sive|seu|vel|atque|nec(?!\\w)|dum\\b|quod|quae|quem|quibus|\"\n    r\"filius|filii|filia|filiae|filium|\"\n    r\"oratio|orationis|dissertatio|dissertatione|\"\n    r\"praeses|respondens|moderante|praeside|\"\n    r\"academiae|academia|universitatis|\"\n    r\"venerabilis|reverendus|amplissimus|clarissimus|\"\n    r\"opera\\b|operi|auctoris|auctore\"\n    r\")\\b\",\n    re.IGNORECASE,\n)\n\n# Ambiguous Latin content words (also used in German religious/academic text)\nLATIN_CONTENT = re.compile(\n    r\"\\b(Anno|Domini|Christi|Jesu|Dei|Deo|Domino|doctor|doctore|professore)\\b\",\n    re.IGNORECASE,\n)\n\n# Latin morphological endings on content words\nLATIN_ENDINGS = re.compile(r\"\\b\\w{5,}(orum|ibus|atio|tione|tatis|tate|eram|erunt)\\b\")\n\n# Explicit early-modern German spelling markers (not year-based)\nEARLY_MODERN_MARKERS = re.compile(\n    r\"\\b(\"\n    r\"vnd|vnnd|vndt|vnndt|\"\n    r\"sey\\b|seyn\\b|seind\\b|\"\n    r\"derer|deß|auff|\"\n    r\"Jungfrau|Jungfraw|\"\n    r\"Bürger(?:in|n|meister)?\"\n    r\")\\b\"\n    r\"|[ck]h\\w{3,}\",          # -kh-/-ch- historical clusters (min 4 chars to avoid noise)\n    re.IGNORECASE,\n)\n\n\ndef true_class(title: str, year: float) -> tuple[str, str]:\n    \"\"\"Stricter true-class annotation — see module docstring.\"\"\"\n    t = str(title)\n\n    grammar_hits  = LATIN_GRAMMAR.findall(t)\n    content_hits  = LATIN_CONTENT.findall(t)\n    ending_hits   = LATIN_ENDINGS.findall(t)\n\n    n_grammar = len(grammar_hits)\n    n_content = len(content_hits)\n    n_ending  = len(ending_hits)\n\n    # LATIN: need ≥2 unambiguous grammar words, OR ≥2 endings,\n    #        OR 1 grammar + 1 ending,  OR 3+ total signals including content\n    is_latin = (\n        n_grammar >= 2\n        or n_ending >= 2\n        or (n_grammar >= 1 and n_ending >= 1)\n        or (n_grammar + n_content + n_ending >= 3 and n_grammar + n_ending >= 1)\n    )\n    if is_latin:\n        evidence = (grammar_hits + ending_hits + content_hits)[:4]\n        return \"LATIN\", f\"True LATIN: {', '.join(str(e) for e in evidence)}\"\n\n    # EARLY_MODERN_DE: requires explicit spelling markers, OR year < 1650\n    marker_hits = EARLY_MODERN_MARKERS.findall(t)\n    if marker_hits:\n        return \"EARLY_MODERN_DE\", f\"Spelling markers: {[m for m in marker_hits if m][:3]}\"\n    if not pd.isna(year) and year < 1650:\n        return \"EARLY_MODERN_DE\", f\"Year {int(year)} < 1650 (no explicit markers)\"\n\n    # GERMAN: year < 1800, no strong signals\n    if not pd.isna(year) and year < 1800:\n        return \"GERMAN\", f\"Year {int(year)}, no early-modern markers\"\n\n    return \"OTHER\", \"no historical signal\"\n\n\ndef confusion_matrix(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    classes = sorted(set(df[\"heuristic_class\"]) | set(df[\"true_class\"]))\n    mat = pd.DataFrame(0, index=classes, columns=classes)\n    for _, row in df.iterrows():\n        mat.loc[row[\"true_class\"], row[\"heuristic_class\"]] += 1\n    mat.index.name   = \"true \\\\ heuristic\"\n    return mat\n\n\ndef per_class_metrics(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:\n    classes = sorted(set(df[\"heuristic_class\"]) | set(df[\"true_class\"]))\n    rows = []\n    for cls in classes:\n        tp = ((df[\"heuristic_class\"] == cls) & (df[\"true_class\"] == cls)).sum()\n        fp = ((df[\"heuristic_class\"] == cls) & (df[\"true_class\"] != cls)).sum()\n        fn = ((df[\"heuristic_class\"] != cls) & (df[\"true_class\"] == cls)).sum()\n        prec   = tp / (tp + fp) if (tp + fp) > 0 else float(\"nan\")\n        recall = tp / (tp + fn) if (tp + fn) > 0 else float(\"nan\")\n        f1     = 2 * prec * recall / (prec + recall) if (prec + recall) > 0 else float(\"nan\")\n        rows.append({\"class\": cls, \"TP\": tp, \"FP\": fp, \"FN\": fn,\n                     \"precision\": round(prec, 3), \"recall\": round(recall, 3),\n                     \"F1\": round(f1, 3)})\n    return pd.DataFrame(rows).set_index(\"class\")\n\n\ndef main():\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()\n    parser.add_argument(\"--sample\", default=SAMPLE)\n    parser.add_argument(\"--out\",    default=OUTPUT)\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    df = pd.read_csv(args.sample)\n    df[\"year_num\"] = pd.to_numeric(df[\"dates\"], errors=\"coerce\")\n\n    results = df.apply(\n        lambda r: true_class(r[\"title\"], r[\"year_num\"]), axis=1\n    )\n    df[\"true_class\"]   = [r[0] for r in results]\n    df[\"true_notes\"]   = [r[1] for r in results]\n\n    df.to_csv(args.out, index=False)\n    print(f\"Wrote {len(df)} rows → {args.out}\\n\")\n\n    # ── Overall agreement ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    n = len(df)\n    n_agree = (df[\"heuristic_class\"] == df[\"true_class\"]).sum()\n    print(f\"Overall agreement: {n_agree}/{n}  ({n_agree/n*100:.1f}%)\\n\")\n\n    # ── Confusion matrix ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    print(\"Confusion matrix (rows = true, cols = heuristic):\")\n    print(confusion_matrix(df).to_string())\n    print()\n\n    # ── Per-class metrics ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    print(\"Per-class metrics:\")\n    print(per_class_metrics(df).to_string())\n    print()\n\n    # ── Heuristic-only distribution (for reference) ───────────────────────────\n    print(\"Heuristic class distribution:\")\n    for cls, cnt in df[\"heuristic_class\"].value_counts().items():\n        print(f\"  {cls:<20} {cnt:>4}  ({cnt/n*100:.0f}%)\")\n    print()\n\n    # ── True-class distribution ───────────────────────────────────────────────\n    print(\"True class distribution:\")\n    for cls, cnt in df[\"true_class\"].value_counts().items():\n        print(f\"  {cls:<20} {cnt:>4}  ({cnt/n*100:.0f}%)\")\n    print()\n\n    # ── Discrepancies ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    disc = df[df[\"heuristic_class\"] != df[\"true_class\"]][\n        [\"obj_id\", \"title\", \"heuristic_class\", \"true_class\", \"notes\", \"true_notes\", \"stratum\"]\n    ]\n    if len(disc):\n        print(f\"Discrepancies ({len(disc)} rows):\")\n        for _, row in disc.iterrows():\n            print(f\"\\n  [{row['stratum']}]  heuristic={row['heuristic_class']} → true={row['true_class']}\")\n            print(f\"  title:       {str(row['title'])[:120]}\")\n            print(f\"  heuristic:   {row['notes']}\")\n            print(f\"  true:        {row['true_notes']}\")\n    else:\n        print(\"No discrepancies — perfect agreement.\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/scripts/sr06_spotcheck_results.py",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n# Purpose:      SR-06 — Print spot-check tables from sr06_historical_evaluated.csv:\n#               true LATIN records, LATIN false-positive patterns, EARLY_MODERN_DE\n#               false-positive patterns, and per-stratum true-class distribution.\n# Usage:        python3 scripts/sr06_spotcheck_results.py [--evaluated PATH]\n# Inputs:       data/processed/sr06_historical_evaluated.csv\n# Outputs:      stdout\n# Dependencies: pandas\n\nimport argparse\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nimport pandas as pd\n\nROOT      = Path(__file__).parent.parent\nEVALUATED = ROOT / \"data\" / \"processed\" / \"sr06_historical_evaluated.csv\"\n\n\ndef main():\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()\n    parser.add_argument(\"--evaluated\", default=EVALUATED)\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    df = pd.read_csv(args.evaluated)\n\n    # ── True LATIN records ────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n    print(\"=== True LATIN records ===\")\n    latin = df[df[\"true_class\"] == \"LATIN\"]\n    for _, r in latin.iterrows():\n        print(f\"  [{r['stratum']}]  {r['dates']}\")\n        print(f\"  {str(r['title'])[:120]}\")\n        print(f\"  evidence: {r['true_notes']}\")\n        print()\n\n    # ── LATIN false positives — grouped by triggering pattern ─────────────────\n    print(\"=== LATIN false positives (heuristic=LATIN, true≠LATIN) ===\")\n    fps = df[(df[\"heuristic_class\"] == \"LATIN\") & (df[\"true_class\"] != \"LATIN\")]\n    for _, r in fps.iterrows():\n        trigger = r[\"notes\"]\n        print(f\"  trigger: {str(trigger)[:80]}\")\n        print(f\"  true:    {r['true_class']}\")\n        print(f\"  title:   {str(r['title'])[:100]}\")\n        print()\n\n    # ── EARLY_MODERN_DE false positives ──────────────────────────────────────\n    print(\"=== EARLY_MODERN_DE false positives (heuristic=EARLY_MODERN_DE, true=GERMAN) ===\")\n    em_fps = df[(df[\"heuristic_class\"] == \"EARLY_MODERN_DE\") & (df[\"true_class\"] == \"GERMAN\")]\n    for _, r in em_fps.iterrows():\n        print(f\"  trigger: {str(r['notes'])[:80]}\")\n        print(f\"  title:   {str(r['title'])[:100]}\")\n        print()\n\n    # ── Per-stratum true-class distribution ───────────────────────────────────\n    print(\"=== Per-stratum true-class distribution ===\")\n    print(df.groupby([\"stratum\", \"true_class\"]).size().unstack(fill_value=0).to_string())\n    print()\n\n    # ── Year distribution of true GERMAN records ──────────────────────────────\n    print(\"=== Year distribution of true GERMAN records ===\")\n    df[\"year_num\"] = pd.to_numeric(df[\"dates\"], errors=\"coerce\")\n    german = df[df[\"true_class\"] == \"GERMAN\"]\n    bins = [0, 1600, 1650, 1700, 1750, 1800]\n    labels = [\"pre-1600\", \"1600-1650\", \"1650-1700\", \"1700-1750\", \"1750-1800\"]\n    german_copy = german.copy()\n    german_copy[\"decade\"] = pd.cut(german_copy[\"year_num\"], bins=bins, labels=labels, right=False)\n    print(german_copy[\"decade\"].value_counts().sort_index().to_string())\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n"
}
```

Leichenpredigt                   99       0      0      1
Monografie_pre1800               87      12      1      0

=== Year distribution of true GERMAN records ===
decade

     1→# GeMeA — NER for Bibliographic Title Extraction
     2→
     3→Context: NER is the **fallback** in `link_gnd_works.py` for records without ISBD markers. The primary extractor is the rule-based ISBD parser. NER only runs when that fails.
     4→
     5→**Scale of the fallback**: analysis of 115K Goethe-Faust DDB items ([isbd-title-analysis.md](../../goethe-faust/notes/isbd-title-analysis.md)) shows only **~29% of titles carry any ISBD pattern**, meaning NER applies to ~71% of records — the majority, not a small edge case. The dominant ISBD signal is ` :` (18%); the ` / ` split used by the parser appears in only 2.1% of titles. These figures are from one DDB provider subset and may vary across the full corpus.
     6→
     7→Target label set: `TITLE`, `OTHER_TITLE`, `PERSON`, `TRANSLATOR`, `PARALLEL_TITLE`, `LANGUAGE`, `MEDIUM`, `EDITION`, `PUBLISHER`, `PLACE`, `YEAR`, `SERIES`, `VOLUME`.
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## 1. Summary
    12→
    13→| Section | What it covers |
    14→|---|---|
    15→| [3. FRBR label scope](#3-frbr-label-scope) | Label definitions organised by FRBR level (Work, Expression, Manifestation) |
    16→| [4. Historical language scope](#4-historical-language-scope) | Risk assessment for pre-modern German and Latin titles; impact on model choice |
    17→| [5. Model options](#5-model-options) | Comparison table: spaCy, Flair, gbert, xlm-roberta, LLM, NuNER Zero, GLiNER, GROBID |
    18→| [6. NuNER Zero — zero-shot NER](#6-nunner-zero--zero-shot-ner-current-recommendation) | Current recommendation: usage code, merge logic, evaluation requirement |
    19→| [7. LLM options at inference time](#7-llm-options-at-inference-time) | Cost/effort comparison for all-NER-record inference paths |
    20→| [8. If no labeled training data is available](#8-if-no-labeled-training-data-is-available) | Silver labeling from ISBD pipeline; LLM one-time labeler; distant supervision |
    21→| [9. Available datasets for fine-tuning](#9-available-datasets-for-fine-tuning) | empathyai/books-ner, HIPE-2022, CLEF-HIPE-2020, GermEval 2014 |
    22→| [10. Decision](#10-decision) | Recommended path: NuNER Zero → LLM labeling → xlm-roberta fine-tune |
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## 2. Open questions
    27→
    28→**SR** = Study/Research question. Numbered in discovery order; not all will appear in the paper.
    29→
    30→| ID | Title | Status | Blocks |
    31→|---|---|---|---|
    32→| [SR-01](#21-sr-01--isbd-signal-coverage-corpus-wide) | ISBD signal coverage (corpus-wide) | ✅ Resolved | — |
    33→| [SR-02](#22-sr-02--isbd-parser-split-priority) | ISBD parser split priority | ✅ Resolved | — |
    34→| [SR-03](#23-sr-03--silver-label-quality-and-false-positive-rate) | Silver label quality and false positive rate | ✅ Resolved | [SR-08](#28-sr-08--nunner-zero-evaluation) |
    35→| [SR-04](#24-sr-04--translator--person-disambiguation) | TRANSLATOR / PERSON disambiguation | ✅ Resolved | [SR-07](#27-sr-07--gold-set-composition) |
    36→| [SR-05](#25-sr-05--trailing-period-noise) | Trailing period noise | ✅ Resolved | — |
    37→| [SR-06](#26-sr-06--historical-and-latin-title-scope) | Historical and Latin title scope | 🔲 Open | [SR-07](#27-sr-07--gold-set-composition) |
    38→| [SR-07](#27-sr-07--gold-set-composition) | Gold set composition | 🔲 Open | [SR-08](#28-sr-08--nunner-zero-evaluation) |
    39→| [SR-08](#28-sr-08--nunner-zero-evaluation) | NuNER Zero evaluation | 🔲 Open — blocked on SR-07 | — |
    40→| [SR-09](#29-sr-09--frbr-metric-scope-for-paper) | FRBR metric scope for paper | 🔲 Open | [SR-07](#27-sr-07--gold-set-composition) |
    41→| [SR-10](#210-sr-10--df_de_titles-source-and-title-length-scope) | DF_DE_TITLES source and title-length scope | ✅ Resolved — [de-titles-distribution.md](ner/sr10_de-titles-distribution.md) | — |
    42→
    43→### 2.1 SR-01 — ISBD signal coverage (corpus-wide)
    44→**Status:** Resolved — [isbd-field-rating.md](ner/sr01_isbd-field-rating.md)
    45→- DF_DE_TITLES (4.47M): 20.2% have ` :`, 0.8% have ` /`, 14.6% have a year, 3.6% have an edition keyword
    46→- Area separator `. -` present in only **1.2%** of records (53k) — structural tier is limited; heuristic tier carries 99% of silver candidates
    47→- Tier 2 silver (structural, multi-field): **4,613 records** (0.1%)
    48→- Tier 1 silver (heuristic, partial): **335,524 records** (7.5%)
    49→
    50→### 2.2 SR-02 — ISBD parser split priority
    51→**Status:** Resolved
    52→- ` /` (SoR — Statement of Responsibility: the ISBD punctuation that separates the title from the creator/contributor field, e.g. *Faust / von Goethe*) appears in only 0.8% of titles; ` :` (subtitle) at 20.2% is the dominant title-area signal
    53→- Parser must prioritise ` :` splitting for `OTHER_TITLE` / `TITLE` boundary, not ` /`
    54→
    55→### 2.3 SR-03 — Silver label quality and false positive rate
    56→**Status:** Resolved — see [silver-label-fp-review.md](ner/sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md)
    57→
    58→- 81 of 200 sampled heuristic-tier records (40.5%) have at least one false positive
    59→- **Excluded:** `f_parallel` (~80% FP), `f_edition` (~83% FP)
    60→- **Post-filter required:** `f_person` (~36% FP), `f_person_compound` (~29% FP)
    61→- **Accepted:** `f_year`, `f_other_title`, `f_publisher`, `f_series`, `f_volume` (all < 15% FP)
    62→- Pre-1750 author placement (name before title, not after ` /`) is a structural false-negative blind spot for `f_person` — flagged for SR-07
    63→
    64→### 2.4 SR-04 — TRANSLATOR / PERSON disambiguation
    65→**Status:** Resolved — see [translator-person-disambiguation.md](ner/sr04_translator-person-disambiguation.md)
    66→
    67→- Only 35% of `f_person` records are true author SoRs; 41% are non-SoR false positives, 19% corporate bodies, 5% editors
    68→- **0 true translators** in 100-record sample — TRANSLATOR label not viable from title strings
    69→- EDITOR detection: 0 F1; `(Hg.)` suffix and body-text `bearb.` missed by heuristic
    70→- **Decision:** `f_person` sub-classified following the ISBD/RDA agent model — person (`f_resp_person`) | collective agents (`f_resp_org` corporate body, `f_resp_family` family) | role qualifier (`f_resp_editor`) | non-SoR (`f_resp_other`); TRANSLATOR and EDITOR dropped as silver label targets
    71→- **`f_resp_org` introduced** for the 19% of ` /` records where the responsible entity is a corporate body (government agency, statistical office, university, etc.) — collective agent, treated as a distinct CORPORATE entity class, not a false positive; `f_resp_family` added for family name entries (not yet validated)
    72→
    73→### 2.5 SR-05 — Trailing period noise
    74→**Status:** Resolved — see [trailing-period-noise.md](ner/sr05_trailing-period-noise.md)
    75→
    76→- 200-record stratified sample of titles ending with `.` (pool: 875,349 = 19.5% of corpus)
    77→- True class distribution: NATURAL 74%, NOISE 10%, ORDINAL 8%, ISBD_CLOSE 7%, ABBREV 1%
    78→- **FP rate: 93%** — far above the 15% acceptance threshold
    79→- All 14 true ISBD_CLOSE records already captured by `has_dot_dash` (11) or year + ` :` / ` /` co-occurrence (3) — trailing `.` adds no new detection power
    80→- **Decision:** exclude trailing `.` as a standalone heuristic-tier signal; the `has_dot_dash` flag already covers the structural tier completely



```

    81→
    82→### 2.6 SR-06 — Historical and Latin title scope
    83→**Status:** Open
    84→- 92.4% of records (tier 0) have no ISBD signals and fall to NER fallback — unknown share are Latin or pre-modern German
    85→- **Action:** sample 200 records from `dc_type` = Leichenpredigt / pre-1800 Monografie; estimate Latin / Early Modern German proportion to determine how much historical signal is needed in training
    86→
    87→### 2.7 SR-07 — Gold set composition
    88→**Status:** Open — blocks SR-08
    89→- **Requirement:** ~500 manually annotated records stratified by: era (modern / 19th c. / pre-1800 / Latin), silver tier (2 / 1 / 0), and `dc_type`
    90→- Must cover tier-0 fallback records (the NER majority path) not just ISBD-structured ones
    91→- **Pre-1750 PERSON annotation (from SR-03):** the ` /` SoR heuristic is a systematic false negative for pre-1750 titles — authors appear before the work title, not after ` /`. Gold set annotators must not rely on the SoR position as a cue for the `PERSON` label in this stratum; author spans need to be identified from the opening name + credentials pattern (e.g. *Firstname Lastname, [role/title], [work title]*). This affects annotation guidelines and model evaluation: PERSON F1 on the pre-1750 stratum should be tracked separately from the modern stratum. See [silver-label-fp-review.md §5](ner/sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md#5-pre-1750-false-negatives--author-before-title) for examples.
    92→
    93→### 2.8 SR-08 — NuNER Zero evaluation
    94→**Status:** Open — blocked on SR-07
    95→- **Requirement:** run NuNER Zero zero-shot on 500 stratified fallback records; assess TITLE and PERSON F1 on gold set
    96→- **Decision gate:** precision ≥ threshold → use zero-shot; else → LLM labeling + fine-tune `xlm-roberta-base` on silver + LLM-labeled set
    97→
    98→### 2.9 SR-09 — FRBR metric scope for paper
    99→**Status:** Open
   100→- **Requirement:** confirm which FRBR levels the paper's quality metrics cover — Work (TITLE, PERSON) only, or also Expression (TRANSLATOR, PARALLEL_TITLE, MEDIUM) and Manifestation (PUBLISHER, PLACE, YEAR, EDITION, SERIES, VOLUME)
   101→- Determines which label types must appear in the gold set and which NER labels are in scope for the evaluation section
   102→
   103→### 2.10 SR-10 — DF_DE_TITLES source and title-length scope
   104→**Status:** Resolved — see [de-titles-distribution.md](ner/sr10_de-titles-distribution.md)
   105→
   106→- **Provenance:** `DF_DE_TITLES` originates in `2023.11 NER.ipynb`; `2024.01 MT-QA.ipynb` produced the dated pkl only. Corpus = 4,477,780 German-tagged DDB TEXT objects (`dc:language` + `langid` = German); no filter by `dc:type`, provider, or era. `all_tokens` = spaCy `de_core_news_sm` token count including stopwords and punctuation; `content_tokens` = stopwords removed, punctuation retained.
   107→- **Token thresholds:** quartiles — short ≤4 (p25), medium 5–14, long >14 (p75). See [title-length-thresholds.md](ner/sr10_title-length-thresholds.md).
   108→- **Length by year:** pre-1750 dominated by long strings (42–50%, median 12–15 tokens); post-1775 shift to median 6–9; 2000–2024 reversal (62% medium). 9.6% of titles have no year.
   109→- **Implication for SR-07:** stratify gold set by length and era; pre-1750 long-form records stress the NER model differently from the short modern majority.

### Assistant *(2026-03-24T08:33:51.391Z)*

**Tool call:** `Write`
```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr06_historical-scope.md",
  "content": "# GeMeA — Historical and Latin Title Scope (SR-06)\n\n**SR-06** in [ner-bibliographic.md](../ner-bibliographic.md).\n\n---\n\n## 1. Background\n\nNER fallback applies to ~92% of DF_DE_TITLES records. An unknown proportion of these are Latin or Early Modern German — both require different tokenisation, different model capabilities, and potentially different annotation strategies than modern German. This study estimates that proportion for two high-prevalence historical strata, and evaluates the accuracy of a heuristic language classifier.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Method\n\n`scripts/sr06_historical_scope.py` sampled 200 records from two strata:\n\n| Stratum | Filter | Total records | Tier-0 records |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| A — Leichenpredigt | `dc_type` contains \"Leichenpredigt\" | 11,255 | 4,809 |\n| B — Monografie pre-1800 | `dc_type == \"Monografie\"` AND `year < 1800` | 175,094 | 145,268 |\n\n100 records sampled from each stratum (seed 42). A heuristic classifier assigned one of four classes: **LATIN**, **EARLY_MODERN_DE**, **GERMAN**, **OTHER**.\n\n`scripts/sr06_evaluate_historical.py` applied a stricter true-class annotator and computed agreement with the heuristic. True-class annotation rules are documented in the script header.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Results\n\n### 3.1 Heuristic output (sr06_historical_scope.py)\n\n| Class | Count | % |\n|---|---|---|\n| EARLY_MODERN_DE | 182 | 91% |\n| LATIN | 12 | 6% |\n| GERMAN | 5 | 2% |\n| OTHER | 1 | 0% |\n\n### 3.2 True-class distribution (sr06_evaluate_historical.py)\n\n| Class | Count | % |\n|---|---|---|\n| EARLY_MODERN_DE | 186 | 93% |\n| GERMAN | 12 | 6% |\n| LATIN | 1 | 0.5% |\n| OTHER | 1 | 0.5% |\n\n### 3.3 Per-class heuristic accuracy\n\n| Class | Precision | Recall | F1 | TP | FP | FN |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| EARLY_MODERN_DE | 0.962 | 0.941 | 0.951 | 175 | 7 | 11 |\n| GERMAN | 1.000 | 0.417 | 0.588 | 5 | 0 | 7 |\n| LATIN | 0.083 | 1.000 | 0.154 | 1 | 11 | 0 |\n| OTHER | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1.000 | 1 | 0 | 0 |\n\nOverall agreement: **182/200 (91%)**.\n\n### 3.4 Per-stratum true-class distribution\n\n| Stratum | EARLY_MODERN_DE | GERMAN | LATIN | OTHER |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| Leichenpredigt (n=100) | 99 | 0 | 0 | 1 |\n| Monografie_pre1800 (n=100) | 87 | 12 | 1 | 0 |\n\n---\n\n## 4. Error analysis\n\n### 4.1 LATIN false positives (11/12 — 83% FP rate)\n\nThe heuristic classifies 12 records as LATIN; only 1 is a true Latin text. Three triggering patterns account for all 11 false positives:\n\n| Pattern | Count | Example |\n|---|---|---|\n| `Anno` + pre-1600 year (single-hit rule) | 5 | `Romischer kayserlicher Maiestat Regiment … Anno MVCX…` — Early Modern German legal text with `Anno` as a date label |\n| `Jesu` / `Christi` / `Doctor` in Leichenpredigt title | 5 | `Christliche Leichpredigt. Uber die Wort Petri … Von diesem (Jesu) …` — these are standard German Protestant devotional vocabulary, not Latin text |\n| `Doctor` + `Christi` together | 1 | `Ein nützliche Sermon Doctor Martini Luthers … von dem reych Christi` |\n\nAll 11 misclassified records are **Early Modern German** texts. Latin terms embedded in otherwise German titles do not make the title Latin.\n\n### 4.2 EARLY_MODERN_DE false positives (7 — 4% FP rate)\n\nSeven records classified as EARLY_MODERN_DE are true GERMAN:\n\n| Trigger | Count | Notes |\n|---|---|---|\n| `[ck]h\\w+` cluster (e.g. \"Heilige\", \"höffliche\") | 4 | Historical cluster pattern fires on common German words — too broad |\n| `Herrn` (dative \"Herr\") | 1 | Used in modern German as well as early modern |\n| Year < 1700 (year-only rule) | 1 | `Antwort Sir Eduard Turners … anno 1664` — English-language pamphlet translated to German; no spelling markers |\n| `D.` abbreviation | 1 | Academic title abbreviation, not a spelling marker |\n\n### 4.3 True LATIN record\n\nOne genuinely Latin record in the sample: a 1769 alchemical treatise (*Delarvatio Tincturae Philosophorum*) with multiple Latin morphological endings (`-atio`, `-orum`). Mixed Latin-German title with \"Das ist:\" introducing a German subtitle.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Key findings\n\n1. **Latin is not a meaningful training challenge for this stratum.** True prevalence is ~0.5% (1/200). The heuristic's 83% FP rate arises because `Anno`, `Christi`, `Jesu`, and `Doctor` are standard German Protestant/academic vocabulary — not reliable Latin indicators in isolation.\n\n2. **The corpus is overwhelmingly Early Modern German.** 93% of historical records use early-modern German spelling and vocabulary. Leichenpredigt is 99% Early Modern German; pre-1800 Monografie is 87%.\n\n3. **EARLY_MODERN_DE heuristic is broadly reliable (F1 = 0.95)** but the `[ck]h\\w+` cluster pattern fires on ordinary German words (\"Heilige\", \"höffliche\", \"nach\") — this contributes false positives in the 1700–1800 range where early modern markers are absent.\n\n4. **\"Year < 1700\" fallback causes 1 FP** (translated English pamphlet); year-only detection without spelling markers is unreliable for non-German texts.\n\n5. **GERMAN is underdetected (recall 0.42)** — 7 records with years 1664–1793 and no early-modern markers were correctly classified as GERMAN by the true annotator but caught as EARLY_MODERN_DE by the heuristic's `[ck]h\\w+` pattern.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Decisions\n\n### D1 — Drop LATIN as a heuristic class for this stratum\n\nLATIN prevalence is ~0.5% with 83% FP rate. The heuristic cannot distinguish Latin terms embedded in German titles from true Latin texts without syntactic analysis. For gold set purposes, LATIN records are rare enough to be identified manually if needed.\n\n**Action:** Remove LATIN class from the historical language heuristic. Records with embedded Latin vocabulary are Early Modern German and should be labelled as such.\n\n### D2 — Restrict `[ck]h\\w+` to word-initial position and minimum 5 characters\n\nThe cluster pattern fires on internal occurrences like \"Heilige\" (`ch`), \"höffliche\" (`ch`). Restricting to word-initial `[ck]h` (e.g. \"khuon\", \"Churfürst\") would be more historically specific.\n\n**Action:** Update `EARLY_MODERN_DE` regex in `sr06_historical_scope.py`; rerun and verify FP rate drops.\n\n### D3 — Remove `Herrn` from EARLY_MODERN_DE markers\n\n`Herrn` (dative of `Herr`) is standard modern German, not an early modern marker.\n\n**Action:** Remove from regex pattern.\n\n### D4 — Gold set implication: no Latin stratum needed\n\nSR-07 gold set design does not need a dedicated Latin stratum. Early Modern German is the primary historical challenge. The gold set should include a **pre-1700** stratum (long-form Leichenpredigt and legal/administrative Monografie) as a proxy for the full historical register.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Impact on model selection\n\nThe NER model does not need Latin capability for the historical stratum. Early Modern German (1500–1750) is the key challenge:\n\n- Long, complex title-page transcriptions (median 15–20 tokens, some >50)\n- Author name + credentials placed *before* the title (not after ` /`) — a structural false negative for `f_person`; see SR-03 §5\n- Non-standard orthography (`vnd`, `seyn`, `deß`) tokenised differently by modern German tokenisers\n\nA `gbert-large` or `xlm-roberta-base` fine-tuned on a pre-1750 stratum is appropriate. Dedicated Latin NER models (e.g. LatinBERT) are not required at this scale.\n"
}
```

     1→# GeMeA — Silver Dataset Pipeline: Summary and General Framework
     2→
     3→A synthesis of what has been done to automatically construct a silver NER training dataset from ISBD punctuation rules in 4,477,780 German DDB title strings, including all resolved study/research questions (SRs). The final section extracts a domain-agnostic framework.
     4→
     5→**Related notes:** [ner-bibliographic.md](../ner-bibliographic.md), [sr01_isbd-field-rating.md](sr01_isbd-field-rating.md), [sr01_isbd-field-rating-adr.md](sr01_isbd-field-rating-adr.md), [sr01_isbd-applicability.md](sr01_isbd-applicability.md), [sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md](sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md), [sr04_translator-person-disambiguation.md](sr04_translator-person-disambiguation.md), [sr10_de-titles-distribution.md](sr10_de-titles-distribution.md), [sr10_title-length-thresholds.md](sr10_title-length-thresholds.md)
     6→
     7→---
     8→
     9→## 1. What was done
    10→
    11→### Phase 0 — Corpus characterisation
    12→
    13→**SR-10** ([sr10_de-titles-distribution.md](sr10_de-titles-distribution.md), [sr10_title-length-thresholds.md](sr10_title-length-thresholds.md))
    14→
    15→Before doing anything with labels, the corpus was characterised along two dimensions:
    16→
    17→- **Provenance.** `DF_DE_TITLES` traced to `2023.11 NER.ipynb` — a language-filtered (dc:language + langid = German) subset of 4,477,780 DDB TEXT objects spanning all eras and dc_types with no length or topic filter.
    18→- **Token length distribution.** Raw distribution of `all_tokens` (spaCy `de_core_news_sm`, includes stopwords + punctuation) and `content_tokens` (stopwords removed). Percentiles: p25=4, p50=8, p75=14. Data-driven length thresholds set at p25/p75 — short ≤4, medium 5–14, long >14.
    19→- **Era-stratified length.** Pre-1750 titles are 42–50% long (median 12–15 tokens) — full bibliographic title pages folded into a single string. Post-1775: shift to median 6–9 tokens (Enlightenment/Romantic publishing convention change). 2000–2024: reversal to 62% medium — digital-born metadata with structured fields.
    20→
    21→*Purpose for silver dataset:* stratification variables for sampling and gold set design; establishes that pre-1750 records require different handling.
    22→
    23→---
    24→
    25→### Phase 1 — Signal detection
    26→
    27→**SR-01** (ISBD signal coverage corpus-wide)
    28→
    29→`scripts/check_isbd_titles.py` measured the raw occurrence of ISBD punctuation patterns across all 4.47M records:
    30→
    31→| Signal | Count | % |
    32→|---|---|---|
    33→| ` :` (subtitle) | 909,869 | 20.3% |
    34→| Trailing `.` (area close) | 783,752 | 17.5% |
    35→| ` ;` (subsequent SoR / series) | 174,813 | 3.9% |
    36→| ` /` (SoR) | 33,907 | 0.8% |
    37→| ` =` (parallel title) | 26,264 | 0.6% |
    38→
    39→**Finding:** only 1.2% of records carry the `. -` full area separator. The structural tier is far smaller than expected; heuristic detection must carry the load for 98.8% of records.
    40→



```

### Assistant *(2026-03-24T08:34:42.886Z)*

SR-06 is complete. Summary of findings:

**True-class distribution** (200-record sample, Leichenpredigt + pre-1800 Monografie):
- EARLY_MODERN_DE: 93%
- GERMAN: 6%
- LATIN: 0.5% (1 record — a 1769 alchemical treatise)
- OTHER: 0.5%

**LATIN heuristic: 83% FP rate.** `Anno`, `Christi`, `Jesu`, `Doctor` are standard German Protestant/academic vocabulary — appearing in a title doesn't make it Latin. Decision: drop LATIN as a heuristic class; true Latin is too rare to detect reliably from surface signals.

**EARLY_MODERN_DE heuristic: F1 = 0.95.** Main FP source is `[ck]h\w+` firing on ordinary German words. Fix: restrict to word-initial position, min 5 chars; drop `Herrn`.

**Gold set implication:** no Latin stratum needed for SR-07. The historical challenge is Early Modern German (long title-page transcriptions, pre-title author placement, non-standard orthography).

Files written:
- [sr06_historical-scope.md](gemea/notes/ner/sr06_historical-scope.md) — full findings note
- [sr06_evaluate_historical.py](gemea/scripts/sr06_evaluate_historical.py) — true-class annotator + confusion matrix
- [sr06_spotcheck_results.py](gemea/scripts/sr06_spotcheck_results.py) — FP pattern tables

    83→**Status:** Resolved — see [sr06_historical-scope.md](ner/sr06_historical-scope.md)
    84→
    85→- 200-record stratified sample: 100 Leichenpredigt + 100 pre-1800 Monografie
    86→- **True-class distribution: EARLY_MODERN_DE 93%, GERMAN 6%, LATIN 0.5%, OTHER 0.5%**
    87→- **LATIN heuristic: 83% FP rate** — `Anno`, `Christi`, `Jesu`, `Doctor` are standard German Protestant/academic vocabulary; embedding does not make the title Latin
    88→- EARLY_MODERN_DE heuristic: F1 = 0.95; main FP source is `[ck]h\w+` cluster firing on ordinary German words ("Heilige", "höffliche")
    89→- **Decision D1:** drop LATIN as a heuristic class for this stratum — true prevalence ~0.5%, no reliable heuristic signal; manual identification sufficient
    90→- **Decision D2/D3:** restrict `[ck]h\w+` to word-initial position (≥5 chars); remove `Herrn` from early modern markers
    91→- **Impact on model selection:** no Latin NER capability needed; Early Modern German (1500–1750) is the primary historical challenge — long title-page transcriptions, pre-title author placement, non-standard orthography
    92→- **Gold set implication:** no dedicated Latin stratum; add pre-1700 stratum (Leichenpredigt + legal/administrative Monografie) as the historical register proxy
    93→
    94→### 2.7 SR-07 — Gold set composition
    95→**Status:** Open — blocks SR-08
    96→- **Requirement:** ~500 manually annotated records stratified by: era (modern / 19th c. / pre-1800 / Latin), silver tier (2 / 1 / 0), and `dc_type`
    97→- Must cover tier-0 fallback records (the NER majority path) not just ISBD-structured ones
    98→- **Pre-1750 PERSON annotation (from SR-03):** the ` /` SoR heuristic is a systematic false negative for pre-1750 titles — authors appear before the work title, not after ` /`. Gold set annotators must not rely on the SoR position as a cue for the `PERSON` label in this stratum; author spans need to be identified from the opening name + credentials pattern (e.g. *Firstname Lastname, [role/title], [work title]*). This affects annotation guidelines and model evaluation: PERSON F1 on the pre-1750 stratum should be tracked separately from the modern stratum. See [silver-label-fp-review.md §5](ner/sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md#5-pre-1750-false-negatives--author-before-title) for examples.
