```json
{
  "file_path": "/Users/mta/Documents/claude/gemea/notes/ner/sr09_gold-set-composition.md",
  "content": "# GeMeA — Gold Set Composition (SR-09)\n\n**SR-09** in [ner-bibliographic.md](../ner-bibliographic.md).\nBlocks: SR-08 (NuNER Zero / GLiNER evaluation), SR-11 (LLM annotation batch), SR-12 (field-level weighting calibration).\n\n---\n\n## 1. Purpose\n\nThe gold set is the single authoritative evaluation corpus for the GeMeA NER pipeline. It has three roles:\n\n1. **Model evaluation** — compute span-level F1 per label type on a held-out human-annotated sample; drives SR-08 (NuNER Zero / GLiNER zero-shot benchmark vs. fine-tuned baselines)\n2. **LLM annotation quality gate** — the 50-record manually annotated seed is used as few-shot examples and as the reference for the ≥85% agreement threshold check before running the full 4k–5k batch (SR-11 §2.3)\n3. **Silver label calibration** — NER F1 at different tier cutoffs lets SR-12 set the weighted-score boundary between tier-1 and tier-0 treatment\n\n---\n\n## 2. Composition requirements\n\n### 2.1 Target size\n\n| Phase | Size | Rationale |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Seed** (manual, immediate) | 50–100 records | Minimum for SR-11 few-shot bootstrap; annotation takes ~2–4 hours manually |\n| **Full gold set** | ~500 records | Sufficient for stable F1 estimates; 500 × p(1−p)/n ≈ ±4.4% margin at 95% CI (worst-case p=0.5) |\n\nThe 50-record seed is a subset of the full gold set — do not annotate separately. Annotate the full 500 in one pass; use the first 50–100 as the SR-11 prompt seed.\n\n### 2.2 Stratification\n\nGold set records must be stratified across four dimensions simultaneously:\n\n| Dimension | Strata | Reason |\n|---|---|---|\n| **Era** | modern (≥1900), 19th c. (1800–1899), 1700–1800, pre-1700 | NER difficulty and register vary sharply by era; PERSON recall drops for pre-1750 author-before-title structure |\n| **Silver tier** | tier-2 (structural), tier-1 (heuristic), tier-0 (no labels) | Evaluation must cover all inference paths; tier-0 is the main fallback (92.4% of corpus) |\n| **`dc_type`** | Leichenpredigt, Monografie, Einblattdruck (and modern residual) | Genre-specific structure differences affect label distribution; Leichenpredigt has the highest pre-1750 density |\n| **Title length** | short (≤4 tokens), medium (5–14), long (>14) | Pre-1750 long strings (42–50% of era) stress boundary detection differently than short modern titles |\n\n**Allocation target (500 records):**\n\n| Era | Tier-2 | Tier-1 | Tier-0 | Total |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| Modern (≥1900) | 20 | 40 | 20 | 80 |\n| 19th c. (1800–1899) | 15 | 30 | 15 | 60 |\n| 1700–1800 | 10 | 20 | 30 | 60 |\n| Pre-1700 | 5 | 15 | 80 | 100 |\n| Modern/19th residual | — | — | 100 | 100 |\n| dc_type oversampling | 40 (Leichenpredigt + Einblattdruck) | | | 100 |\n| **Total** | **~50** | **~105** | **~245** | **~500** |\n\n> Note: tier-0 is over-represented in the pre-1700 strata because that is where the LLM annotation (SR-11) applies — the gold set must evaluate model behaviour on the primary inference path.\n\nLeichenpredigt and Einblattdruck records are oversampled to 40–50 each (drawn from pre-1700/1700–1800 strata) because these genres have the most structurally distinctive title-page conventions and are the highest-risk failure modes for the NER model.\n\n### 2.3 No Latin stratum\n\nTrue Latin prevalence in DF_DE_TITLES is ~0.5% (SR-06). Latin records are not a separate stratum — identify them manually during annotation if encountered and mark `lang=la` in the annotation metadata. Do not attempt Latin NER evaluation in Phase 1.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Annotation schema\n\n### 3.1 Phase 1 labels (required for evaluation)\n\n| Label | What it marks | Notes |\n|---|---|---|\n| `TITLE` | Main work title; primary intellectual content identifier | May include subtitle if no `OTHER_TITLE` separator is present |\n| `OTHER_TITLE` | Subtitle or alternative title | Introduced by `Das ist:`, `oder`, `:`, `nämlich`, `welches handelt von` |\n| `PERSON` | Author, editor, responsible person; includes credentials and role descriptions forming a single naming unit | Pre-1750: includes opening credential sequence and post-name role phrases; see §4 |\n\n### 3.2 Phase 2 labels (include in schema, skip for Phase 1 evaluation)\n\nAnnotate these in the same pass to avoid re-annotation when SR-07 Phase 2 is reached:\n\n| Label | What it marks |\n|---|---|\n| `TRANSLATOR` | Translator; only when a translation keyword is present (`übersetzt`, `Übers.`, `transl.`, `traduit`) |\n| `PARALLEL_TITLE` | Title in a second language, typically after `=` |\n| `MEDIUM` | Statement of medium or format for music (e.g. `für Klavier und Violine`) |\n\nPhase 2 labels need not be evaluated in Phase 1. Annotate them anyway so the gold set does not need to be extended when Phase 2 evaluation is due.\n\n### 3.3 Span boundary convention\n\n- Spans are **character-level** offsets into the raw `dc:title` string (no pre-tokenization)\n- Spans are **non-overlapping** and **non-nested** — if a PERSON span contains a title fragment, the PERSON boundary stops before that fragment\n- Spans **must** be contiguous substrings of the input — no gap spans\n- For pre-1750 records: the PERSON span includes degree abbreviations, full name, and role phrases up to (but not including) the first content noun of the title; see §4 for examples\n\n---\n\n## 4. Annotation guidelines for pre-1750 records\n\nThese records are the highest-risk stratum for annotation error and model failure. Annotators must not apply modern bibliographic conventions.\n\n### 4.1 Author-before-title structure\n\nIn pre-1750 titles, the author's name and credentials appear **before** the main title. There is no ` /` separator. The typical structure is:\n\n```\n[credential + name + role phrase | PERSON] [main title | TITLE] [subtitle | OTHER_TITLE]\n```\n\nExample:\n```\nInput:  \"D. Johann Gerhard, Professoris zu Jena, Erklärung der Historien des Leidens\"\nOutput: [D. Johann Gerhard, Professoris zu Jena | PERSON] [Erklärung der Historien des Leidens | TITLE]\n```\n\n### 4.2 PERSON span boundaries\n\n**Include in the PERSON span:**\n- Academic degree abbreviations immediately before the name: `D.` (Doktor), `M.` (Magister), `Lic.`, `Mag.`\n- Full personal name (first name + surname)\n- Role or position phrases following the name: `Pfarrers zu X`, `der H. Schrifft Lehrers`, `Professoris`, `Pastoris`, `Superintendenten`\n- Genitive or prepositional phrases identifying the post: `zu Jena`, `in Leipzig`, `bey der Gemeine zu X`\n\n**Stop the PERSON span** at the first token that is clearly part of the work title (a content noun, verb phrase, or `Das ist:`).\n\n### 4.3 Common errors to avoid\n\n| Error | Example | Correct annotation |\n|---|---|---|\n| Annotating the credential sequence as TITLE | `D. Johann Gerhard` marked as TITLE | Mark as PERSON; title begins after role phrase |\n| Splitting credentials from name | `D. Johann Gerhard` → only `Johann Gerhard` as PERSON | Include degree abbreviation in the PERSON span |\n| Marking dedicatees as PERSON | `Herrn N.N. gewidmet` | Only label named dedicatees if also the author; generic dedications are not labeled |\n| Marking embedded Latin as a separate entity | `Anno MDXLVI` | Part of the TITLE span; do not extract separately |\n| Labeling `durch` / `von` phrases as TRANSLATOR | `durch Johann Schmidt` | Label PERSON unless a translation keyword is present |\n\n---\n\n## 5. Sampling procedure\n\n### 5.1 Draw the sample\n\n```python\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\n\nrng = np.random.default_rng(seed=42)\n\n# Load corpus\ndf = pd.read_parquet(\"data/processed/df_de_titles.parquet\")\n\n# Derive era strata\ndf[\"era\"] = pd.cut(\n    pd.to_numeric(df[\"dates\"], errors=\"coerce\"),\n    bins=[-np.inf, 1700, 1800, 1900, np.inf],\n    labels=[\"pre-1700\", \"1700-1800\", \"19th-c\", \"modern\"],\n    right=False,\n)\ndf[\"era\"] = df[\"era\"].cat.add_categories(\"unknown\").fillna(\"unknown\")\n\n# Draw per stratum\nstrata = [\n    (\"modern\",    \"2\",  20), (\"modern\",    \"1\",  40), (\"modern\",    \"0\",  20),\n    (\"19th-c\",    \"2\",  15), (\"19th-c\",    \"1\",  30), (\"19th-c\",    \"0\",  15),\n    (\"1700-1800\", \"2\",  10), (\"1700-1800\", \"1\",  20), (\"1700-1800\", \"0\",  30),\n    (\"pre-1700\",  \"2\",   5), (\"pre-1700\",  \"1\",  15), (\"pre-1700\",  \"0\",  80),\n]\n\nframes = []\nfor era, tier, n in strata:\n    pool = df[(df[\"era\"] == era) & (df[\"silver_tier\"] == tier)]\n    k = min(n, len(pool))\n    frames.append(pool.sample(n=k, random_state=rng.integers(1e6)))\n\n# Oversample Leichenpredigt + Einblattdruck\nfor dc_type in [\"Leichenpredigt\", \"Einblattdruck\"]:\n    pool = df[\n        df[\"dc_type\"].str.contains(dc_type, na=False) &\n        df[\"silver_tier\"].isin([\"0\", \"1\"])\n    ]\n    frames.append(pool.sample(n=min(50, len(pool)), random_state=rng.integers(1e6)))\n\ngold_sample = (\n    pd.concat(frames)\n    .drop_duplicates(subset=[\"identifier\"])\n    .sample(frac=1, random_state=42)\n    .reset_index(drop=True)\n)\ngold_sample.to_csv(\"data/annotation/sr09_gold_sample.csv\", index=False)\nprint(f\"Gold sample size: {len(gold_sample)}\")\n```\n\n### 5.2 Output format\n\nEach annotated record should produce a JSON Lines file (`data/annotation/sr09_gold.jsonl`) with entries of the form:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"identifier\": \"de/...\",\n  \"dc_title\": \"D. Johann Gerhard, Professoris zu Jena, ...\",\n  \"era\": \"pre-1700\",\n  \"dc_type\": \"Leichenpredigt\",\n  \"silver_tier\": \"0\",\n  \"spans\": [\n    {\"start\": 0, \"end\": 35, \"label\": \"PERSON\", \"text\": \"D. Johann Gerhard, Professoris zu Jena\"},\n    {\"start\": 37, \"end\": 82, \"label\": \"TITLE\",  \"text\": \"Erklärung der Historien des Leidens...\"}\n  ],\n  \"annotator\": \"manual\",\n  \"annotation_date\": \"2026-XX-XX\",\n  \"notes\": \"\"\n}\n```\n\nPhase 2 labels (`TRANSLATOR`, `PARALLEL_TITLE`, `MEDIUM`) use the same span format — add them to the `spans` array if present.\n\n---\n\n## 6. Evaluation metrics\n\nRun after annotation is complete; use the held-out portion (full 500) against model predictions.\n\n### 6.1 Span-level exact match F1\n\nStandard NER evaluation: a span is correct if **both** the character offsets and the label match exactly.\n\n```python\nfrom seqeval.metrics import classification_report  # or spacy scorer\n\n# Convert spans to BIO tags per token, then compute\n```\n\nReport separately:\n- Per-label F1: `TITLE`, `OTHER_TITLE`, `PERSON` (Phase 1); `TRANSLATOR`, `PARALLEL_TITLE`, `MEDIUM` (Phase 2 when ready)\n- Per-era F1: `modern`, `19th-c`, `1700-1800`, `pre-1700` — PERSON F1 on pre-1700 must be tracked separately (author-before-title failure mode)\n- Per-tier F1: tier-2, tier-1, tier-0 — primary interest is tier-0 (NER fallback path)\n\n### 6.2 Thresholds and decision gates\n\n| Metric | Threshold | Action if below |\n|---|---|---|\n| Overall span F1 (all labels) | ≥ 0.80 | Do not advance to production; extend fine-tuning data |\n| TITLE F1 (all eras) | ≥ 0.85 | Minimum for reliable title extraction feeding GND linking |\n| PERSON F1 (pre-1700 stratum) | ≥ 0.75 | Below this: author-before-title prompt rule is not learned; revise SR-11 prompt and re-run LLM labeling |\n| LLM annotation agreement (SR-11 spot-check) | ≥ 0.85 span F1 vs. gold | Below this: revise system prompt before full 4k–5k batch |\n\nThese thresholds are consistent with the \"practical minimum for reliable boundary learning\" framing in SR-11 §1 and with the SR-08 decision gate in ner-bibliographic.md §2.8.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Relationship to other SRs\n\n| SR | Dependency on SR-09 |\n|---|---|\n| SR-08 (NuNER Zero / GLiNER evaluation) | Needs full 500-record gold set to run zero-shot benchmarks |\n| SR-11 (LLM annotation) | Needs 50-record seed for few-shot prompt; needs spot-check agreement against gold sample |\n| SR-12 (field-level weighting) | Needs NER F1 vs. score-cutoff to calibrate tier-1/0 boundary |\n\nThe 50-record manual seed annotated first also serves as the SR-11 prompt seed — annotate these from the pre-1700 tier-0 stratum (the highest-risk and least-covered stratum) to maximize value of the seed.\n\n---\n\n## 8. Blockers and open questions\n\n| Item | Status |\n|---|---|\n| Annotation tool selection | Unresolved — `doccano`, `Label Studio`, or JSON Lines in a spreadsheet are all viable for 500 records; Label Studio preferred for span annotations |\n| Inter-annotator agreement (IAA) | Desirable but not required for Phase 1 — a single trained annotator is sufficient for the 500-record set; flag ambiguous cases in `notes` field |\n| Pre-1750 annotation examples | 5 examples in SR-11 §4.3 serve as annotator training; review against real DDB records before starting |\n| `silver_tier` column name in corpus | Check actual column name in `df_de_titles.parquet` — may be `tier`, `label_tier`, or `silver_tier` |\n"
}
```

   111→**Status:** Open — blocks SR-08

gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:64:- Pre-1750 author placement (name before title, not after ` /`) is a structural false-negative blind spot for `f_person` — flagged for SR-09
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:103:- **Implication for SR-09:** gold set Phase 1 annotation covers `TITLE`, `OTHER_TITLE`, `PERSON` only. Expression labels (`TRANSLATOR`, `PARALLEL_TITLE`) should be included in the annotation schema from the start (to avoid re-annotation) but are not required for the initial evaluation pass.
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:105:### 2.8 SR-08 — NuNER Zero evaluation
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:106:**Status:** Open — blocked on SR-09
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:110:### 2.9 SR-09 — Gold set composition
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:111:**Status:** Open — blocks SR-08
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:128:- **Implication for SR-09:** stratify gold set by length and era; pre-1750 long-form records stress the NER model differently from the short modern majority.
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:131:**Status:** Open — blocked on SR-09 seed (50-record manual annotation)
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:137:- **Bootstrapping:** annotate 50–100 records manually as prompt seed (these also seed SR-09 gold set); spot-check 200 LLM-labeled records (~5%); re-run if agreement < 85%
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:141:**Status:** Future — blocked on SR-03 extension, SR-04 completion, SR-09 completion
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:147:- **Calibration:** tier boundary requires SR-09 gold set to measure NER F1 at different score cutoffs (external calibration preferred over internal proxy)
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:240:**On DeBERTa vs XLM-R:** `deberta-v3-large` outperforms `xlm-roberta-large` on English NER benchmarks, but is English-only. The multilingual DeBERTa (`mdeberta-v3-base`) is only available at base size — so the choice for this task is effectively `xlm-roberta-large` (560M, multilingual large) vs `mdeberta-v3-base` (86M, multilingual base). XLM-R large is the stronger model and the documented choice for historical multilingual NER. Both should be benchmarked in SR-08 if fine-tuning is pursued.
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:286:BERT-sized — runs on CPU, same deployment footprint as GLiNER. Zero-shot precision on DDB strings is unknown; **evaluate on ~500 stratified fallback records before committing** (see [SR-08](#28-sr-08--nunner-zero-evaluation)).
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:368:[Omitted long matching line]
gemea/notes/ner-bibliographic.md:374:1. **Try NuNER Zero first** — compact zero-shot encoder-based NER, no training data, runs locally, handles arbitrarily long spans. Evaluate on 500 stratified fallback records (see [SR-08](#28-sr-08--nunner-zero-evaluation)). Zero-shot LLM NER is a known step down from fine-tuned models (~88 vs ~93 F1 on CoNLL2003; Zhan et al. 2026) — expect similar or larger gap on out-of-distribution historical German.
gemea/notes/roadmap-spinoff.md:19:- [ ] SR-07 FRBR scope, SR-09 gold set, SR-08 NuNER Zero evaluation — SR-09 blocked on SR-07
gemea/notes/roadmap.md:53:- [ ] SR-07 — FRBR metric scope for paper: confirm which FRBR levels (Work only, or also Expression/Manifestation) the evaluation section covers; determines gold set label scope. Blocks SR-09.
gemea/notes/roadmap.md:54:- [ ] SR-09 — Gold set composition: ~500 manually annotated records stratified by era (modern / 19th c. / 1700–1800 / pre-1700), silver tier (2 / 1 / 0), `dc_type`, and title length; annotation guidelines must address pre-1750 author-before-title placement (systematic `f_person` false negative); no dedicated Latin stratum (SR-06). Blocked on SR-07. Blocks SR-08.
gemea/notes/roadmap.md:55:- [ ] SR-08 — NuNER Zero evaluation: run `numind/NuNerZero` zero-shot on 500 stratified fallback records; assess F1 per label and per era stratum on gold set. Blocked on SR-09.
gemea/notes/roadmap.md:56:- [ ] **Decision gate** (SR-08 output): NuNER Zero F1 ≥ threshold → use zero-shot; else LLM-label silver candidates and fine-tune `xlm-roberta-large` (primary) and `mdeberta-v3-base` (benchmark). See `notes/ner-bibliographic.md` §10 for recommended fine-tuning path.
gemea/notes/roadmap.md:72:      ▼  [BLOCKED on SR-09]
gemea/notes/roadmap.md:76:NuNER Zero evaluation (SR-08)  →  F1 per label + per era stratum
gemea/notes/ner/sr10_de-titles-distribution.md:109:- **Implication for SR-09 (gold set):** stratify by length as well as era. Pre-1750 long-form records stress the NER model differently from the short modern majority — the TITLE boundary is structurally different. The 9.6% no-year group needs separate treatment — sample by `dc_type` or `silver_tier` instead.
gemea/notes/ner/silver-dataset-pipeline.md:121:| Gold set construction | 🔲 Blocked on SR-09 | Manual annotation |
gemea/notes/ner/silver-dataset-pipeline.md:122:| NER evaluation | 🔲 Blocked on SR-09 | SR-08 |
gemea/notes/ner/sr10_title-length-thresholds.md:3:**Applies to:** `scripts/analyse_title_lengths.py`, gold set stratification (SR-09), NER evaluation (SR-08)
gemea/notes/ner/ref_zhan2026-generative-ner.md:77:### 5.1 NuNER Zero (SR-08) remains relevant
gemea/notes/ner/ref_zhan2026-generative-ner.md:79:[Omitted long matching line]
gemea/notes/ner/ref_zhan2026-generative-ner.md:81:### 5.2 Fine-tuned generative LLMs should be added as SR-08 benchmark
gemea/notes/ner/ref_zhan2026-generative-ner.md:87:If Claude or another LLM is used to generate silver labels or annotate the SR-09 gold set, use **Inline Bracketed** or **Inline XML** output format. JSON formats (especially offset-based) perform significantly worse. Category-grouped JSON also loses positional information.
gemea/notes/ner/ref_zhan2026-generative-ner.md:91:The GENIA biomedical gap is structurally similar to GeMeA's situation: historical German bibliographic text is a low-resource domain where LLM pre-training provides minimal coverage. This supports prioritising the SR-09 gold set and fine-tuning over zero-shot-only deployment.
gemea/notes/ner/sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md:107:This is a structural blind spot: the pattern cannot be fixed by post-filtering `f_person`. Early modern records in the SR-09 gold set will need a separate author-detection strategy (e.g. name-before-title NER, or annotation without relying on the SoR heuristic).
gemea/notes/ner/sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md:123:Pre-1750 titles systematically place the author's name and credentials *before* the main title (not after ` /`), making `f_person` a **false negative** for this entire stratum. The SoR heuristic misses the majority of early modern authors. This is a structural blind spot, not a noise issue — the pattern cannot be fixed by post-filtering. Implications for SR-09 gold set composition: early modern records must be annotated with a different author-detection strategy.
gemea/notes/ner/ref_gliner-nunerzero-comparison.md:52:## 4. Relevance to GeMeA (SR-08)
gemea/notes/ner/ref_gliner-nunerzero-comparison.md:59:The supervised baseline gap (GLiNER-Multi: 32.9 vs. XLM-R supervised: 54.9 on MultiCONER) is consistent with the zero-shot LLM performance gap documented in Zhan et al. (2026). For GeMeA's low-resource historical domain, a similar or larger gap should be expected. SR-08 evaluation on 500 stratified records is necessary before drawing any conclusions about viability.
gemea/notes/ner/sr11_labeling-strategy.md:3:Related: [ner-bibliographic.md §8](../ner-bibliographic.md), [SR-09](../ner-bibliographic.md#29-sr-09--gold-set-composition)
gemea/notes/ner/sr11_labeling-strategy.md:44:| Systematic bias across similar records | Medium | Evaluate on the SR-09 gold set (human-annotated, independent); if LLM errors cluster on a dc_type or era, resample |
gemea/notes/ner/sr11_labeling-strategy.md:54:   - 5 few-shot examples drawn from the SR-09 gold set (once available) or manually annotated
gemea/notes/ner/sr11_labeling-strategy.md:57:5. Use as fine-tuning data; evaluate on SR-09 gold set (held out, human-annotated)
gemea/notes/ner/sr11_labeling-strategy.md:61:The few-shot examples in the prompt should ideally come from the SR-09 gold set — but SR-09 requires annotation work first. Two options:
gemea/notes/ner/sr11_labeling-strategy.md:66:Option A is cleaner — the 50-record manual seed is also the start of the SR-09 gold set.
gemea/notes/ner/sr11_labeling-strategy.md:145:Include 5 examples covering the main structural patterns. Production examples should come from the manually annotated SR-09 seed set. Until then, use the following (verify against real DDB records before the full batch run):
gemea/notes/ner/sr06_historical-scope.md:59:**Conclusion:** n=200 is sufficient for the decision this study supports — determining whether a Latin stratum is needed in the gold set (SR-09) and confirming Early Modern German as the primary historical challenge. It is **not** sufficient for precise per-class prevalence estimates publishable as corpus statistics; a larger stratified sample (n≥500 per stratum) would be needed for that.
gemea/notes/ner/sr06_historical-scope.md:61:**Unsampled strata:** Leichenpredigt and pre-1800 Monografie represent ~186k records. Pre-1800 Periodika, Handschrift, and Einblattdruck are not covered. Their language distribution may skew differently (e.g. Handschrift may have higher Latin prevalence). Flag for SR-09 gold set design if these dc_types are included.
gemea/notes/ner/sr06_historical-scope.md:172:SR-09 gold set design does not need a dedicated Latin stratum. Early Modern German is the primary historical challenge. The gold set should include a **pre-1700** stratum (long-form Leichenpredigt and legal/administrative Monografie) as a proxy for the full historical register.
gemea/notes/ner/sr06_historical-scope.md:189:**What is needed:** the SR-09 gold set (pre-1700 stratum, human-annotated) or LLM-labeled pre-1750 records as an interim (see [ner-bibliographic.md §8.2](../ner-bibliographic.md)).
gemea/notes/ner/sr09_gold-set-composition.md:1:# GeMeA — Gold Set Composition (SR-09)
gemea/notes/ner/sr09_gold-set-composition.md:3:**SR-09** in [ner-bibliographic.md](../ner-bibliographic.md).
gemea/notes/ner/sr09_gold-set-composition.md:4:Blocks: SR-08 (NuNER Zero / GLiNER evaluation), SR-11 (LLM annotation batch), SR-12 (field-level weighting calibration).
gemea/notes/ner/sr09_gold-set-composition.md:12:1. **Model evaluation** — compute span-level F1 per label type on a held-out human-annotated sample; drives SR-08 (NuNER Zero / GLiNER zero-shot benchmark vs. fine-tuned baselines)
gemea/notes/ner/sr09_gold-set-composition.md:240:These thresholds are consistent with the "practical minimum for reliable boundary learning" framing in SR-11 §1 and with the SR-08 decision gate in ner-bibliographic.md §2.8.
gemea/notes/ner/sr09_gold-set-composition.md:246:| SR | Dependency on SR-09 |
gemea/notes/ner/sr09_gold-set-composition.md:248:| SR-08 (NuNER Zero / GLiNER evaluation) | Needs full 500-record gold set to run zero-shot benchmarks |
gemea/notes/ner/sr12_field-level-weighting.md:54:- **External**: once SR-09 gold set exists, measure NER F1 for models trained at different score cutoffs and select the elbow.
gemea/notes/ner/sr12_field-level-weighting.md:56:The external method is preferred — it directly measures annotation quality impact rather than proxy precision metrics. It requires SR-09 to be completed first.
gemea/notes/ner/sr12_field-level-weighting.md:96:- SR-09 completion (for external calibration of tier boundary)

   100→- **Phase 1 scope — Work:** initial NER evaluation covers Work-level labels only: `TITLE`, `OTHER_TITLE`, `PERSON`. This is the minimum viable label set for the paper's linking quality metrics.
   101→- **Phase 2 scope — Expression:** once Work-level extraction is validated, add Expression-level labels: `TRANSLATOR`, `PARALLEL_TITLE`, `MEDIUM`. These can be derived from the Manifestation record but require additional heuristics (language field, ` /` SoR role disambiguation, medium indicators).
   102→- **Manifestation labels deferred:** `PUBLISHER`, `PLACE`, `YEAR`, `EDITION`, `SERIES`, `VOLUME` are extractable from the Manifestation string and useful for deduplication, but are not in scope for the paper's NER evaluation.
   103→- **Implication for SR-09:** gold set Phase 1 annotation covers `TITLE`, `OTHER_TITLE`, `PERSON` only. Expression labels (`TRANSLATOR`, `PARALLEL_TITLE`) should be included in the annotation schema from the start (to avoid re-annotation) but are not required for the initial evaluation pass.
   104→
   105→### 2.8 SR-08 — NuNER Zero evaluation
   106→**Status:** Open — blocked on SR-09
   107→- **Requirement:** run NuNER Zero zero-shot on 500 stratified fallback records; assess TITLE and PERSON F1 on gold set
   108→- **Decision gate:** precision ≥ threshold → use zero-shot; else → LLM labeling + fine-tune `xlm-roberta-base` on silver + LLM-labeled set
   109→
   110→### 2.9 SR-09 — Gold set composition
   111→**Status:** Open — blocks SR-08
   112→
   113→See [sr09_gold-set-composition.md](ner/sr09_gold-set-composition.md) for full content.
   114→
   115→- **Size:** ~500 manually annotated records; 50-record seed annotated first (seeds SR-11 prompt and serves as the bootstrapping set)
   116→- **Stratification:** era (modern / 19th c. / 1700–1800 / pre-1700) × silver tier (2 / 1 / 0) × `dc_type`; Leichenpredigt and Einblattdruck oversampled to ~50 each; tier-0 pre-1700 over-represented (primary inference path)
   117→- **Schema:** Phase 1 labels `TITLE`, `OTHER_TITLE`, `PERSON`; annotate Phase 2 labels `TRANSLATOR`, `PARALLEL_TITLE`, `MEDIUM` in the same pass to avoid re-annotation
   118→- **No Latin stratum (from SR-06):** true Latin prevalence ~0.5% — too rare to stratify; mark `lang=la` if encountered
   119→- **Pre-1750 PERSON annotation (from SR-03):** ` /` SoR heuristic is a systematic false negative for pre-1750 — authors appear before the work title. Annotators must use opening credential + name + role pattern; PERSON F1 tracked separately for pre-1700 stratum. See [sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md §5](ner/sr03_silver-label-fp-review.md#5-pre-1750-false-negatives--author-before-title) and [sr09 §4](ner/sr09_gold-set-composition.md#4-annotation-guidelines-for-pre-1750-records)
   120→- **Decision thresholds:** overall F1 ≥ 0.80; TITLE F1 ≥ 0.85; PERSON F1 (pre-1700) ≥ 0.75; LLM agreement ≥ 0.85
   121→
   122→### 2.10 SR-10 — DF_DE_TITLES source and title-length scope
   123→**Status:** Resolved — see [de-titles-distribution.md](ner/sr10_de-titles-distribution.md)
   124→
   125→- **Provenance:** `DF_DE_TITLES` originates in `2023.11 NER.ipynb`; `2024.01 MT-QA.ipynb` produced the dated pkl only. Corpus = 4,477,780 German-tagged DDB TEXT objects (`dc:language` + `langid` = German); no filter by `dc:type`, provider, or era. `all_tokens` = spaCy `de_core_news_sm` token count including stopwords and punctuation; `content_tokens` = stopwords removed, punctuation retained.
   126→- **Token thresholds:** quartiles — short ≤4 (p25), medium 5–14, long >14 (p75). See [title-length-thresholds.md](ner/sr10_title-length-thresholds.md).
   127→- **Length by year:** pre-1750 dominated by long strings (42–50%, median 12–15 tokens); post-1775 shift to median 6–9; 2000–2024 reversal (62% medium). 9.6% of titles have no year.
   128→- **Implication for SR-09:** stratify gold set by length and era; pre-1750 long-form records stress the NER model differently from the short modern majority.
   129→

374:1. **Try NuNER Zero first** — compact zero-shot encoder-based NER, no training data, runs locally, handles arbitrarily long spans. Evaluate on 500 stratified fallback records (see [SR-08](#28-sr-08--nunner-zero-evaluation)). Zero-shot LLM NER is a known step down from fine-tuned models (~88 vs ~93 F1 on CoNLL2003; Zhan et al. 2026) — expect similar or larger gap on out-of-distribution historical German.

   125→- **Provenance:** `DF_DE_TITLES` originates in `2023.11 NER.ipynb`; `2024.01 MT-QA.ipynb` produced the dated pkl only. Corpus = 4,477,780 German-tagged DDB TEXT objects (`dc:language` + `langid` = German); no filter by `dc:type`, provider, or era. `all_tokens` = spaCy `de_core_news_sm` token count including stopwords and punctuation; `content_tokens` = stopwords removed, punctuation retained.
   126→- **Token thresholds:** quartiles — short ≤4 (p25), medium 5–14, long >14 (p75). See [title-length-thresholds.md](ner/sr10_title-length-thresholds.md).
   127→- **Length by year:** pre-1750 dominated by long strings (42–50%, median 12–15 tokens); post-1775 shift to median 6–9; 2000–2024 reversal (62% medium). 9.6% of titles have no year.
   128→- **Implication for SR-09:** stratify gold set by length and era; pre-1750 long-form records stress the NER model differently from the short modern majority.
   129→
   130→### 2.11 SR-11 — Labeling strategy and prompt design (pre-1750)
   131→**Status:** Open — blocked on SR-09 seed (50-record manual annotation)
   132→
   133→See [sr11_labeling-strategy.md](ner/sr11_labeling-strategy.md) for full content.
   134→
   135→- **Dataset size:** Work-only (3 types) requires 1k–2k records; Work + Expression (6 types) requires 3k–5k; Manifestation deferred — ISBD silver covers modern stratum
   136→- **Annotator:** Claude API with Inline Bracketed output format; 4k–5k pre-1750 tier-0 records, stratified by `dc_type` and decade
   137→- **Bootstrapping:** annotate 50–100 records manually as prompt seed (these also seed SR-09 gold set); spot-check 200 LLM-labeled records (~5%); re-run if agreement < 85%
   138→- **Early Modern German prompt:** system prompt must encode author-before-title rule, credential sequence patterns, orthographic markers, and embedded-Latin disambiguation; see §4 of [sr11_labeling-strategy.md](ner/sr11_labeling-strategy.md) for full prompt spec
   139→
   140→### 2.12 SR-12 — Field-level weighting for silver tier assignment
   141→**Status:** Future — blocked on SR-03 extension, SR-04 completion, SR-09 completion
   142→
   143→See [sr12_field-level-weighting.md](ner/sr12_field-level-weighting.md) for full requirements.
   144→
   145→- **Goal:** replace binary `n_fields ≥ 3` threshold with a precision-weighted score (`score = Σ (1 − FP_rate_i)` over active fields), enabling principled tier-boundary calibration and a potential four-tier split for curriculum fine-tuning
   146→- **Missing FP data:** `f_volume` (1.9% coverage) and `f_publisher` (0.2%) not yet sampled — extend SR-03 with ~100 records each
   147→- **Calibration:** tier boundary requires SR-09 gold set to measure NER F1 at different score cutoffs (external calibration preferred over internal proxy)
   148→- **Conditional FP rates** (optional): field co-occurrence effects (e.g. `f_year AND f_other_title` vs `f_year` alone) need a joint sample if a four-tier split is pursued
   149→- **Primary motivation:** curriculum learning — train first on high-confidence tier-2 + high-score tier-1 records, then add lower-confidence augmentation
   150→
   151→---
   152→

   364→2. Fine-tune on silver-labeled DDB records
   365→3. Supplement with HIPE-2022 ajmc and CLEF-HIPE-2020 for historical German signal

   368→**Alternative generative path (from Zhan et al. 2026):** Fine-tuned open-source LLMs (Qwen3-1.7B or LLaMA3.2-1B) with LoRA + Inline Bracketed/XML format match encoder-based models on general-domain NER (CoNLL2003 F1 ≈ 90–94 for 7–8B models; 1–4B models lag by ~10 F1 points). Add a fine-tuned small LLM as a benchmark in SR-08 alongside `xlm-roberta-large` and `mdeberta-v3-base`. Note: Zhan et al. evaluate on modern English/general-domain benchmarks only — performance on historical German bibliographic NER is unknown and likely lower (cf. GENIA biomedical gap). See [ref_zhan2026-generative-ner.md](ner/ref_zhan2026-generative-ner.md).
   369→
   370→---
   371→
   372→## 10. Decision
   373→
   374→1. **Try NuNER Zero first** — compact zero-shot encoder-based NER, no training data, runs locally, handles arbitrarily long spans. Evaluate on 500 stratified fallback records (see [SR-08](#29-sr-09--nunner-zero-evaluation)). Zero-shot LLM NER is a known step down from fine-tuned models (~88 vs ~93 F1 on CoNLL2003; Zhan et al. 2026) — expect similar or larger gap on out-of-distribution historical German.
   375→2. **If NuNER Zero precision is insufficient**, use an LLM to label those same records and fine-tune. Benchmark three options:
   376→   - `xlm-roberta-large` — primary; strongest multilingual encoder; documented HIPE-2022 backbone
   377→   - `mdeberta-v3-base` — lighter multilingual alternative
   378→   - Fine-tuned small LLM (Qwen3-1.7B or LLaMA3.2-1B + LoRA, Inline Bracketed format) — competitive with encoder models on general-domain NER (Zhan et al. 2026); unknown on historical German
